Title: Data Analyst
Description:
This isn’t your regular job. Almedia is a place where those who want to push harder can accelerate their careers faster than anywhere else. We’re aiming to become Germany’s second bootstrapped unicorn. Almedia is already Europe’s #3 fastest-growing company in 2025 (FT1000).

We are building the future of marketing by rewarding our community of over 70 million users for engaging with our advertisers’ products. We are offering a new way to acquire users for the biggest companies in the world.

We are seeking a Data Analyst with a passion for uncovering insights that drive growth. This pivotal role is for a highly analytical individual who will directly influence our product, rewards, marketing, and user acquisition strategies through deep dives into user behaviour and advanced predictive modelling.

Join us and become a central force in optimising our user acquisition and incentivisation, for maximising Lifetime Value (LTV) for millions of users.

What You'll Do 🎯


Focus on Causal Analysis: Quantify the causal impact of monetary incentives and product features on user retention. Identify the key behavioural drivers that lead to long-term LTV growth across Freecash and Link.
Design, Run and Analyse A/B tests: Own the end-to-end experimentation lifecycle (design, power analysis, execution, and interpretation). Use A/B and multivariate testing to validate causal hypotheses and drive measurable incremental growth, across different domains (product, rewards, UA) to drive incremental outcomes for business top line and bottom line.
Drive Predictive Analytics: Focus on analysing user behaviour and marketing activities to predict key metrics like Return on Ad Spend, identifying high-value user segments and opportunities for growth.
Deep User Segmentation: Lead in developing and applying advanced user segmentation models, providing actionable insights that inform product development, marketing strategies, and personalised user experiences.
Performance Optimisation & Strategy: Partner closely with Engineering, Product, and User Acquisition teams to interpret complex performance data, translate findings into strategic recommendations, and directly influence optimisation efforts.
Advanced Data Analysis & Modelling: Utilise strong SQL and Python skills to conduct sophisticated data analysis, build robust analytical models, and maintain relevant data structures that support predictive insights.
Insights Communication & Enablement: Transform complex analytical findings into clear, compelling narratives and actionable recommendations for stakeholders. Mentor and train teams on how to leverage these insights effectively, moving beyond basic reporting.


What You'll Bring 💡


Extensive experience (typically 4+ years) in a data analysis role with a strong focus on business performance, user behaviour, and growth metrics.
Proven experience with technical data analysis, statistics, A/B testing, data modelling and evaluation of algorithmic data solutions.
Exceptional proficiency in SQL and Python for complex data manipulation, analysis, and statistical modelling.
Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and data modelling principles relevant to analytical outcomes.
Experience in collaborating closely with marketing, product, and data science teams to drive measurable business impact.
Ability to communicate complex analytical findings clearly and concisely to diverse audiences, influencing data-driven decisions.


What Makes You a Great Fit 🌟


A Predictive Mindset: You are passionate about uncovering future trends and user behaviour through data, using insights to drive proactive strategies.
Impact-Driven & Strategic: You're not just reporting numbers; you're using them to influence key business decisions and directly contribute to revenue and user growth.
Collaborative Analytical Partner: You excel at translating complex data into actionable strategies for business stakeholders, thriving in a cross-functional environment.


Why Almedia? 🎁


Own Our Growth: We offer all Berlin-based employees equity in Almedia to truly be a part of our success.
Scale With Almedia: Grow alongside a startup that has been profitable from day one.
Central Berlin Office: Work from a fully-stocked modern office built for collaboration, accessible from all around Berlin.
Other Benefits: Transport subsidy, breakfasts and lunches, language learning, Urban Sports Club, and more.
We Listen: We regularly add to our benefits through rigorous employee feedback.


We believe in fostering talent, evaluating all skill levels during the hiring process, and providing a clear path for growth. Almedia is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace and celebrate diversity, and encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply.

Title: Product Data Analyst
Description:
Location: We are flexible! Come and join us either in Hamburg, Berlin or Barcelona.

Freenow empowers smarter mobility decisions helping people to move freely and cities to thrive.

We are looking for a Product Data Analyst (m/f/d) to collaborate with our product squads, using data to build a flawless product experience. You will join the Product Data Team and work closely with PMs, designers, UX researchers, developers, and other data functions to discover new product opportunities, validate hypotheses, and launch valuable features to our users.

Be ready to work in a multinational, diverse, highly motivated and collaborative team of passionate analysts who strive for excellence and like to have fun. Are you ready for your next ride?

YOUR DAILY ADVENTURES WILL INCLUDE:


Providing expertise and collaborating with stakeholders to generate new product feature ideas and improvements, and also estimating the potential impact of planned projects.
Continuously engaging with the product squads to gain an understanding of the problems that need to be solved, gathering requirements, and defining the best deliverables to match the business needs.
Having full ownership of your analysis, from ideation to communication of results and making recommendations to all levels of the company including senior management and prioritization committees.
Developing and evaluating A/B test experiments or other analyses to measure and report the performance of released features, ensuring that we are launching the correct changes and features in our apps.
Working closely with our developers and data engineers to create and capture data that is aligned with the business logic and allows modeling and analysis.
Being involved throughout the complete product lifecycle in order to guarantee a data-driven process.
Bringing new insights and championing them using your analyses, dashboards, and good storytelling! We value ideas over hierarchy, and there's always a new path to follow if your idea is convincing and well-backed with data.
You will have the opportunity to steer our products, which kind of features should be developed, and which changes should be made to solve hundreds of thousands of users' daily needs.


TO BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE:


You have curiosity in your essence, which motivates you to answer WHY things happened, not only scratching the surface, but aiming for well-founded actionable results
Degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, Computer Science, Engineering, or other fields with a strong mathematical-statistical background
3+ years experience in working as a Data Analyst in a data-driven analytics environment, preferably within tech product organizations, having direct contact with stakeholders.
Exceptional analytical skills, lateral thinking, and proven experience in solving highly challenging problems.
Very good knowledge of SQL, databases, and statistical programming tools (e.g. Python, R) so that exploratory analyses can be performed, reports created and ETLs maintained
Good understanding and hands-on experience in analyzing and interpreting A/B test results, backed by statistical knowledge.
Experience in working with data visualisation tools (e.g.Tableau, Qlikview) and event tracking (e.g. Mixpanel, GA, Firebase)
You are able to communicate findings in a concise and clear manner to a wide variety of stakeholders to support data-driven decision making by using data visualisation and storytelling and have excellent English communication skills.


BENEFITS & PERKS IN A NUTSHELL:


Flexible working arrangements
LinkedIn Learning
Sabbatical & special leave policies
WeRoad partnership
Birthday, 24th + 31st December off
Short term EU work policy
Mobility Credit
Health Insurance
Employee assistance program


Plus more local benefits depending on your work location!

DIVERSITY, EQUITY & INCLUSION:

Freenow is an equal opportunity employer and we consider qualified applicants regardless of race, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability or age.

We want you to grow and evolve, bring your true self to work.

ABOUT US:

Freenow by Lyft empowers smarter mobility decisions, helping people to move freely and cities to thrive. Through our multi-mobility app, we feature broad options for everyone across 9 European markets and over 180 cities. Millions of passengers can access services including taxis, private hire vehicles, carsharing, car rental, e-scooters, e-bikes, e-mopeds, and public transport within a single app.

In July 2025 Freenow was acquired by Lyft, a global mobility platform, which connects riders and drivers for billions of rides across North America and Europe. Together, Freenow and Lyft are creating a more caring and connected world, with transportation for everyone. We are a global, diverse, highly motivated, and collaborative team that strives for excellence and likes to have fun. Ready for your next ride?

Title: (Junior) Data Analyst (m/w/d) Data Analytics
Description:
Unser Angebot


Freuen Sie sich auf eine leistungsorientierte Vergütung, flexible Arbeitszeiten und die Möglichkeit, im Homeoffice mit aktuellen Technologien virtuell zusammenzuarbeiten.
Wir wünschen uns, dass Sie mit uns wachsen. Dabei unterstützen und begleiten wir Ihre fachliche und persönliche Entwicklung zielgerichtet und gestalten mit Ihnen zusammen Ihren persönlichen Karriereweg.
Es erwarten Sie ein kollegiales Betriebsklima, zahlreiche Annehmlichkeiten für den beruflichen Alltag sowie verschiedene Rahmenbedingungen, mit denen wir die Vereinbarkeit von Familie und Beruf unterstützen.


Ihre Aufgaben


Sie arbeiten flexibel in interdisziplinären Projektteams aus Kunden- und Syncwork-Mitarbeitenden, direkt bei unseren Kunden vor Ort, von unserem Office bzw. Homeoffice aus.
Sie managen und analysieren große Datenmengen und entwickeln intelligente Algorithmen.
Sie unterstützen bei der Analyse, dem Design und Prototyping sowie bei der Realisierung und dem Rollout innovativer Reporting- und Planungslösungen zur Unternehmenssteuerung mithilfe von Business Intelligence.
Sie konzipieren und realisieren innovative Lösungen im Bereich Industrie 4.0, Advanced Analytics, Machine Learning, Big Data oder Artificial Intelligence.
Sie analysieren Strategien, Organisations- und Kostenstrukturen sowie Prozesse.


Ihr Profil


Sie haben Ihr Studium der Wirtschaftswissenschaften (BWL, VWL), (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, des (Wirtschafts-)Ingenieurwesens, der Mathematik, ein vergleichbares Studium oder Ihre Ausbildung mit bankfachlichem Hintergrund erfolgreich abgeschlossen.
Sie punkten idealerweise mit praktischen Erfahrungen im Bereich Big Data, Advanced und Data Analytics und haben eine Leidenschaft für neue Technologien und digitale Trends.
Sie besitzen vorteilhafterweise Kenntnisse in Programmiersprachen (z. B. R, Python), Datenbanksystemen (z. B. SQL) oder Cloud-Technologien (MS Azure, IBM Bluemix).
Sie denken analytisch und gehen Aufgaben systematisch und strukturiert an.
Sie verfügen über gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse.
Sie wollen durch Ihren Einsatz zum Projekterfolg beitragen und verkörpern unsere durch Teamgeist, Flexibilität und Erfolgswillen geprägte Unternehmenskultur.
Kommunikationsstärke und Eigeninitiative runden Ihr Profil ab.

Title: Data Analyst ( M/F/D )
Description:
As Data Analyst, you play a key role in driving data-informed decision-making across one of LYNX’s most strategic product and business domains.

We are looking for four Data Analysts to join one of our core squads: Onboarding, Demand Capture, Client Experience, or Client & Product Integrity.


Onboarding Squad: Analyze and improve the customer onboarding journey to make it faster, smoother, and more efficient.
Demand Capture Squad: Use data to understand customer interest and help turn prospects into qualified leads and customers.
Client Experience Squad: Analyze client behavior, feedback, and platform usage to improve the trading experience, increase loyalty, and reduce churn.
Client and Product Integrity Squad: Support smarter, data-driven compliance and due diligence processes tailored to client needs and risk profiles.


In this role, you will help turn data into actionable insights that improve client journeys, optimize commercial performance, strengthen compliance processes, and support better business decisions.

You will own the full lifecycle of data-driven discovery, from connecting and exploring data to modelling, visualizing, and deploying solutions. Your work will directly influence how we grow, serve, and protect our clients.

What You’ll Do


End-to-End Analytics Ownership: Own the full process of data-driven discovery, from sourcing and exploring data to modelling, dashboarding, and delivering scalable solutions.
Business Problem Solving: Translate complex business challenges into practical, data-based solutions that create measurable impact.
Dashboard & KPI Development: Build dashboards, reporting frameworks, and tracking models that support decision-making and performance management over time.
Stakeholder Partnership: Work closely with Product, Marketing, Operations, Compliance, and Commercial teams to understand priorities and turn questions into insights.
Data Exploration & Insight Generation: Analyze structured and unstructured datasets to identify patterns, risks, opportunities, and growth levers.
Experimentation & Optimization: Support testing, experimentation, and continuous improvement initiatives with strong analytical input.
Data Quality & Governance: Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across reporting and analytics outputs.
Squad Impact Contribution: Depending on your squad, contribute to improving onboarding flows, lead conversion, client loyalty, churn reduction, or smarter compliance processes.﻿


Who You Are

You are a curious and proactive analyst who enjoys solving business problems with data. You combine technical skills with commercial awareness and communicate insights in a clear and practical way.


You have strong analytical skills and confidence working with structured and unstructured data
You have solid Python and SQL skills, including advanced querying
You have a proactive, self-driven mindset and take ownership of your work
You communicate clearly and can explain complex topics in a simple way
You have a high standard for quality and strong attention to detail
You are comfortable working with cross-functional stakeholders
You are motivated by continuous improvement and measurable impact


Nice to have


Knowledge of financial markets and products such as options and futures
Experience in fintech, brokerage, or other data-rich environments
Experience with BI tools and dashboarding platforms
Experience working in agile product squads


What Do We Offer


A competitive salary based on your educational background and professional experience
Free lunch prepared by our in-house cook, plus fresh juices and fruit
Plenty of room to grow, including a yearly training budget of €2,000
Team trips and social activities with colleagues
A relaxed and informal working environment
Discounted membership with Urban Sports Club
An additional contribution to your company pension scheme
In-house German and English courses with a native speaker, with 50% of the cost covered


Who We Are

“LYNX helps you invest in yourself.”

“Working at LYNX means joining the LYNX family.”

“The development opportunities make LYNX the best place to work.”

This is what employees say about LYNX — and it reflects exactly what we aim to offer: an environment where you can get the best out of yourself.

From our offices in Amsterdam, Ghent, Berlin, and Prague, we work every day to provide private and professional clients with the best trading opportunities through a fast, user-friendly, and reliable platform.

LYNX is an international FinTech company where around 300 colleagues contribute to client success across Europe in an informal, collaborative, and growth-oriented environment.

We're an equal-opportunity employer. We welcome applications from all suitably qualified and eligible candidates without attention to gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation/identity, disability or neurodiversity.

Title: Werkstudent:in (w/m/d) Data Analyst / BI Analyst
Description:
WER WIR SIND




Als 100%-ige Tochtergesellschaft der Porsche AG, sind wir als Porsche Digital angetreten, innovative Visionen, Strategien, Produkte, Geschäftsmodelle und begeisternde Kundener- lebnisse zu entwickeln, um die Marke Porsche im Zeitalter der Digitalisierung erweiternd zu definieren. Dabei geht es sowohl um Strategien als auch innovative Lösungen und weit in die Zukunft gedachte Konzepte.




Gemeinsam mit Dir wollen wir den telefonischen Kundenservice von Porsche auf das nächste Level heben. Du denkst dabei an ein Callcenter - aber viel besser! Du bist ein wesentlicher Teil des Kundenerfolgs von Porsche, daher kannst Du dich genauso gut ein/e Porsche-Botschafter/in nennen. "Sprichst” du Porsche? Denn wir suchen leidenschaftliche Teammitglied, die den Kundenservice von Porsche revolutionieren wollen.




WAS DICH ERWARTET

Title: Data Analyst - Revenue & AI (d/f/m)
Description:
Personio's intelligent HR platform helps small and medium-sized organizations unlock the power of people by making complicated, time-consuming tasks simple and efficient. Our team of 1,500 Personios is building user-friendly products that delight our 15,000+ customers and their 1.5 million employees. Ready to make an impact from day one?

This role requires 2 days per week in the office and is based in Munich, Berlin, or Dublin.

We empower Personio's decision-making with advanced analytics and strategic insights, while boosting productivity through systems improvement and automation. Be part of our transformative journey in HR technology, where your contributions will shape our data-driven future.

The Data Analyst - Revenue & AI reports directly to the Senior Lead, Revenue Analytics, and sits within the Analytics department of Personio's broader Data team. This role sits at the intersection of traditional BI, prescriptive analytics, and AI automation. This is not just a dashboard-builder role — it is a role for someone who can combine deep business understanding with hands-on AI tooling to generate insights, automate analytical workflows, and directly influence GTM and revenue decisions.

You will work closely with Revenue Operations, Sales, Marketing, and Finance to turn data into decisions — faster, and at a greater scale than traditional BI allows. Critically, you will own direct relationships with Sales and Account Management leadership, bringing a clear commercial point of view and a genuine opinion on what levers can be pulled to drive revenue.

Role Responsibilities: What you'll do


Business Partnership: You own direct relationships with Sales and Account Management leadership, acting as a trusted analytical partner who regularly presents findings, leads business rhythm conversations, and proactively flags risks and opportunities with clear recommended actions.
GTM Analytics Ownership: You own end-to-end analytical coverage of GTM and revenue metrics including pipeline health, MRR/NRR movements, expansion signals, cross-sell coverage, and regional performance, partnering with RevOps and Finance to define the single source of truth.
AI-Powered Insights: You design and run LLM-powered analytical workflows (using Claude, GPT, or similar) for automated insight generation, narrative summaries, anomaly detection, and translating unstructured signals into structured analytical inputs.
Analytical Automation: You build automated reporting and alerting pipelines using tools like n8n, Zapier, or Make, replacing manual analytical tasks with intelligent, scheduled workflows that deliver insights at scale.
Self-Serve Analytics Tools: You develop AI-powered self-serve tools that enable stakeholders to query data in natural language, democratizing access to insights across the GTM organization.
Data Infrastructure: You build, maintain, and develop the reporting layer in Snowflake — including dbt models, data dictionaries, and metric definitions — that powers GTM and revenue reporting, managing version-controlled codebases (Git) independently.


Role Requirements: What you need to succeed


You have 3+ years of experience in data analytics, revenue analytics, or a similar role in a B2B SaaS or high-growth environment, with hands-on experience working with CRM data (Salesforce or HubSpot preferred) and RevOps datasets.
You are proficient in SQL and have strong experience with at least one BI tool (Tableau, Looker, or equivalent), with the ability to extract, transform, and analyze data independently.
You have hands-on experience with AI and LLM tools (such as Claude, GPT, or similar) and can design and implement AI-powered analytical workflows for automation, insight generation, and anomaly detection.
You possess strong business acumen and can form clear opinions on GTM performance, understand what drives revenue outcomes, and translate data into actionable recommendations for senior stakeholders.
You have excellent communication skills, able to distill complex analytical findings into clear, compelling narratives for non-technical audiences and confidently present to Sales and Account Management leadership.
You are comfortable with data infrastructure and automation, including experience with dbt, version control (Git), and ideally automation tools like n8n, Zapier, or Make to build scalable analytical pipelines.
You work autonomously and proactively, spotting risks and opportunities before being asked, and driving analytical projects from ambiguous business questions to clear, actionable insights.


Why Personio

Personio is an equal opportunities employer, committed to building an integrative culture where everyone feels welcomed and supported. We embrace uniqueness and understand that our diverse, values-driven culture makes us stronger. We are proud to have an inclusive workplace environment that will foster your development no matter your gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, education level, or race.

At Personio, we value in-person collaboration while also offering flexibility. This role is office-based, with 3 days per week required in your contracted office location. The remaining days can be worked from home or in the office if you prefer. In addition, you’ll have 20 Flex Days per year to work remotely from other locations. Aside from our people, culture, and mission, check out some of the other benefits that make Personio a great place to work:


Receive a competitive reward package – reevaluated each year – that includes salary, benefits, and pre-IPO equity
Enjoy 28 days of paid vacation, plus an additional day after 2 and 4 years
Make an impact on the environment and society with 1 (fully paid) Impact Day
Receive generous family leave, child support, mental health support, and sabbatical opportunities
We enjoy gathering for meals, cultural initiatives, and events like local Summer Sessions and year-end celebrations. There's also healthy snacks, drinks, and a weekly catered lunch.

Title: Business Analyst (Data & Reporting) – Corporate Banking
Description:
Business Analyst (Data & Reporting) – Corporate Banking (m/f/d)




We are looking for a Business Data Analyst to support our Corporate Banking team – someone who understands the business, works confidently with data and reporting, and acts as a key link between Business, Data and Finance.

You will sit close to the business, translate requirements into clear data needs, and help turn complex data into simple, actionable insights.

This role is strongly business-focused – a solid understanding of Corporate Banking and its business processes is essential.




What you will work on

Data Quality & Controls

Safeguard data quality across the Corporate Data Domain.
Run data‑quality checks, analyse gaps and drive corrective actions.
Identify root causes and set preventative controls.

Data Stewardship

Own the Corporate First Line Data Domain from a business perspective.
Maintain key data elements, definitions and business rules.
Promote data ownership and literacy; align with Data Governance.

Data Analysis & Business Enablement

Analyse client, product, portfolio and risk data.
Deliver clear insights that support business decisions.
Spot trends, data gaps and optimisation opportunities.
Ensure reporting logic and definitions are documented and controlled.

Dashboards & Reporting

Translate business questions into reporting requirements.
Build and improve dashboards (Power BI/Tableau).
Automate recurring analyses.

Data Flows & Documentation

Map data sources, flows and system interfaces.
Keep documentation of data lineage accurate and up to date.

Stakeholder Management & Projects

Be the go‑to person for data‑related topics.
Support change initiatives and cross‑team projects.
Drive standardisation, automation and scalability.




What you bring

Solid experience in a banking or financial services environment (ideally Corporate Banking)
Good understanding of corporate banking products or business processes
Experience working with business stakeholders and translating business needs into data or reporting requirements
Comfortable working with data and reports (e.g. analysing, validating, interpreting data)
Strong SQL knowledge (ability to query data and understand results)
Strong communication skills and a proactive, hands-on mindset
Excellent English skills; Dutch or German is an advantage.




What we offer:

An international, diverse team with colleagues from more than 50 countries
A hybrid working model with flexibility grounded in trust.
A collaborative environment in our Berlin office.
The opportunity to professionalise and elevate our Corporate data landscape.
Learning & development tailored to your ambitions.
A culture that values simplicity, ownership and inclusion.
Opportunity to work remotely from other EU countries for up to 30 days per year

Title: BI / Data Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
Was dich erwartet

Du verantwortest unsere Modellierungs-, Governance- und Dashboard-Ebene, welche Auszahlungen, Partnerökonomie und operative Prozesse auf Basis interner Daten steuert. Du arbeitest täglich mit Databricks, dbt und Metabase.

Deine Aufgaben


Aufbau und Pflege von dbt-Modellen in Databricks
Etablierung einer Single Source of Truth für Finance- und Operations-Kennzahlen
Aufbau einer KPI-Governance inklusive Definitionen, Verantwortlichkeiten und Data Lineage
Entwicklung von Metabase-Dashboards für Management, Finance sowie Strategy & Operations
Unterstützung und Steuerung der Migration von Tableau zu Metabase
Sicherstellung der Datenqualität durch Alerts, Schema-Checks und Data-Quality-Tests
Regelmäßige Abstimmungen mit Finance, StratOps und Management
Übersetzung von Business-Fragen in belastbare Analysen und KPIs
Unterstützung bei Compliance-Themen wie DSAR, Löschkonzept und VVZ-Mapping


Was du mitbringst


Mehrjährige Erfahrung in BI, Analytics Engineering oder Data Analytics
Sehr gute SQL-Kenntnisse und produktive Erfahrung mit dbt
Erfahrung mit Databricks, Snowflake oder BigQuery
Sicherer Umgang mit modernen BI-Tools
Kommunikationsstärke im Austausch mit Business- und Management-Stakeholdern
Sehr gutes Englisch sowie Deutschkenntnisse auf C1-Niveau oder Bereitschaft, dieses Niveau zu erreichen


Was wir versprechen

Arbeitsumgebung & Flexibilität


Office-First Company: Du arbeitest hybrid (3 Tage Office / 2 Tag Homeoffice) und selbstbestimmt – so, wie es für dich am besten passt.
Modernes, klimatisiertes Büro mit viel Tageslicht
Zentrale Lage zwischen Gleisdreieck & Potsdamer Platz mit sehr guter Anbindung
Große Dachterrasse mit atemberaubendem Blick über Berlin


Benefits


Freie Getränke, frisches Obst und Müsli
100 % Übernahme eines Deutschlandtickets für deine Mobilität
Dein Hund ist ein Teamplayer? Perfekt! Wir freuen uns über tierische Unterstützung im Büro.
Corporate Benefits Account mit attraktiven Angeboten & Rabatten für viele Marken
Firmenevents und Teamevents, weil uns Zusammenhalt wichtig ist
Betriebliche Altersvorsorge mit 20 % Arbeitgeberanteil – wir denken mit dir an die Zukunft
Spiel & Spaß nach Feierabend: Nintendo Switch, PS5, Dart & Tischkicker warten auf dich


Unternehmenskultur & Zusammenarbeit


Agiles und modernes Mindset – offene Kommunikation – jede Stimme zählt
Spannende Aufgabengebiete: praxistaugliches juristisches Wissen
Wir bieten spannende Aufgaben und praxistaugliches juristisches Wissen – du kannst unsere Reise aktiv mitgestalten.
Feedback ist bei uns keine Floskel, sondern ein echter Gamechanger: Wir setzen auf gemeinsames Wachstum.


Über uns

Legalhero ist die moderne Plattform für Rechtsschutzversicherungen und Anwälte. Wir verfolgen das ehrgeizige Ziel, die Schadensfallabwicklung von Rechtsschutzversicherungen zu revolutionieren. Dazu integrieren wir uns direkt in die Systeme der Versicherer. Unsere Partneranwälte wickeln die Fälle hocheffizient und kundenorientiert auf unserer Tech-Plattform mit Hilfe von KI ab. Legalhero wurde 2017 gegründet und hat bis heute hunderttausenden von Versicherungsnehmern geholfen. Unsere ca. 90 Kollegen sitzen in Berlin, nahe dem Gleisdreieck.

Ein inklusives, gleichberechtigtes und diskriminierungsfreies Arbeitsumfeld ist für uns selbstverständlich. Wenn du eine Beeinträchtigung oder spezielle Bedürfnisse haben solltest und während des Bewerbungsprozesses spezielle Unterstützung benötigst, teile es uns gerne mit.

You will work closely with Customer Support, Professional Services, CX Ops and COO Office to turn customer data into decisions, faster and at a greater scale than traditional BI allows. Critically, you will own direct relationships with CX leadership, bringing a genuine point of view on what levers can be pulled to improve efficiency, Customer Experience, accelerate customer time-to-value and ultimately reduce churn.

Role Responsibilities: What you'll do


Own direct relationships with CX leadership, acting as a trusted analytical partner who understands the customer and business requirements, not just the data
Own end-to-end analytical coverage of CX metrics: onboarding health, CSAT trends, support patterns and CX IC KPIs
Partner with CX Operations and Product to define the single source of truth for key CX metrics
Proactively surface anomalies, risks, and opportunities, not just when asked, but with a clear point of view on what to do about them
Design and run LLM-powered analytical workflows using Claude, GPT, or similar, including unstructured data analysis across CX notes, support tickets, and customer feedback
Build automated reporting and alerting pipelines, replacing manual analytical tasks with intelligent, scheduled workflows
Develop self-serve AI-powered tools that allow CX stakeholders to query customer data in natural language
Maintain and improve dbt models that power CX reporting
Document methodologies, define metric definitions, and maintain data dictionaries


Role Requirements: What you need to succeed

Must haves


Experience with at least one BI tool: Tableau, Looker, or equivalent
Exposure to CRM or CX platform data (Salesforce, HubSpot, Gainsight, or equivalent)
Strong communication skills, able to translate complexity into clear narratives for non-technical audiences


Great to have


Python for analytical automation (pandas, data manipulation, API calls)
Experience building automation workflows in n8n, Zapier, or Make
Experience in a B2B SaaS environment, ideally with exposure to CX or Customer Support functions
Prior work with health scoring, NPS/CSAT analysis, or customer lifecycle modelling


Why Personio

Personio is an equal opportunities employer, committed to building an integrative culture where everyone feels welcomed and supported. We embrace uniqueness and understand that our diverse, values-driven culture makes us stronger. We are proud to have an inclusive workplace environment that will foster your development no matter your gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, education level, or race.

At Personio, we value in-person collaboration while also offering flexibility. This role is office-based, with 3 days per week required in your contracted office location. The remaining days can be worked from home or in the office if you prefer. In addition, you’ll have 20 Flex Days per year to work remotely from other locations. Aside from our people, culture, and mission, check out some of the other benefits that make Personio a great place to work:


Receive a competitive reward package – reevaluated each year – that includes salary, benefits, and pre-IPO equity
Enjoy 28 days of paid vacation, plus an additional day after 2 and 4 years
Make an impact on the environment and society with 1 (fully paid) Impact Day
Receive generous family leave, child support, mental health support, and sabbatical opportunities
We enjoy gathering for meals, cultural initiatives, and events like local Summer Sessions and year-end celebrations. There's also healthy snacks, drinks, and a weekly catered lunch.

Title: AI Fact-Checking Assistant
Description:
About The Role
The AI Fact-Checking Assistant identifies and corrects inaccuracies within our advisory reports. You will work alongside our senior advisors to ensure that every sentence in our written counsel is supported by verifiable evidence. This position exists to bridge the gap between AI-assisted research and the rigorous standards required for board-level decision making.
This is a full-time, permanent position designed for someone early in their career. You will spend your time auditing technical summaries, verifying citations, and checking the logic of drafts. In a firm that relies entirely on the written word, your role is to protect the integrity of the record.

About JHC Consulting
JHC Consulting is a private advisory practice. we provide counsel to chief executives and board committees regarding structural reorganizations and governance transitions. Our firm operates on a specific philosophy: we do not use slide decks or facilitate consensus sessions. We produce authored memoranda that remain on file as a permanent record of a decision. These documents are usually seven to ten pages long and are signed by a single individual. This approach ensures analytical honesty and accountability. Because our clients rely on these reports for high-stakes decisions, our writing must be precise and every fact must be absolute.

What You Will Do
Verify each data point and statistic mentioned in a drafted memorandum against primary sources.
Audit citations to ensure that every quote and reference is attributed correctly and used in context.
Review AI-generated research summaries to identify hallucinations or subtle misinterpretations of source material.
Examine public filings, corporate records, and regulatory documents to confirm timelines of executive actions.
Draft brief, evidence-based notes for senior advisors when a drafted claim cannot be verified.
Maintain a logical filing system for all source materials used to support a specific memorandum.
Check the formatting of footnotes and references to ensure they meet our internal standard for clarity.


What we Need From You
We look for individuals with a skeptical mind and an eye for small details. You should find satisfaction in catching a misattributed quote or an incorrect date that others might miss. You must be able to read dense material and summarize it with perfect accuracy. We value people who prefer working independently and focusing on a single document for several hours at a time. This role requires the ability to explain why a fact is incorrect without using emotional language. High performance in this role is measured by the clarity and accuracy of the final document. You do not need years of corporate experience, but you must be able to demonstrate a high standard for your own research and writing.

Working At JHC Consulting
Work at our firm is conducted entirely in a remote environment. We prioritize deep work and do not fill your schedule with regular video calls or meetings. You will have a clear set of tasks for the day and the autonomy to complete them. Senior advisors provide direct feedback on your research and help you develop your analytical skills. We maintain a quiet and disciplined atmosphere where the quality of the thought is the primary metric for success. Intellectual honesty is expected in every communication.

What We Offer
Full-time salary and permanent employment status.
A fully remote work schedule with no requirement for travel.
Direct mentorship from senior advisors in a specialized field.
Exposure to complex governance and strategic questions at the highest level.
An environment that protects your time for focused, deep work.
Access to a wide range of professional research databases and tools.


JHC Consulting remains committed to the written record as the highest form of professional counsel. We believe that analytical honesty is the only way to help leaders make consequential decisions. Your work ensures that our documents provide a stable foundation for the future of the organizations we serve.

Title: Staff Data Analyst
Description:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.

Job Description

We are on the lookout for a Staff Data Analyst to join the Vendor Strategic Programs team.

As part of this team, you will be working on some of the most exciting and strategically critical projects supporting our restaurant partners. For instance, we improve the affordability of millions of dishes on a daily basis, providing actionable insights to optimize the pricing strategy of hundreds of thousands of vendors globally.

As part of our broader Vendor Team, we jointly contribute to the success of both Delivery Hero and thousands of restaurants, shops, and local businesses. Your contribution will empower vendors with advanced tools to manage their operations, boosting their visibility and reach. The insights you create and the improvements you enable will create growth opportunities for businesses of all sizes, strengthening Delivery Hero’s ecosystem and impact.


Work alongside a top team of data scientists, engineers, product managers, and closely with the commercial teams to deliver strategy-defining insights fast and in high-quality
Inform leadership decisions that define how we grow and support our base of 1m+ restaurants in 65+ countries, how we monetize our services and how we provide affordable meals to millions of people every day
Champion a data-driven mindset, guiding leaders and team members on methodology and best practices in leveraging data effectively
Collaborate closely across departments to make the most of our vast company data pool, driving new solutions and enhancing operational efficiency at scale
Engage with local and global teams on a daily basis to deeply understand their requirements, and business priorities, translating them into actionable insights and strategic recommendations


Qualifications

Significant experience as a BI/Data analyst (preferably in e-commerce or fintech) with a degree in statistics, mathematics, economics/business, computer science or an equivalent education and/or relevant practical experience


Expert-level proficiency in SQL (preferably, experience with BigQuery) and data visualization (Tableau and Looker)
Proficiency in analytics engineering tools, incl. Orchestration tools like dbt and Airflow
Experience with data modeling and turning complex data into actionable insights
Passion for data, with creative problem-solving abilities and an eye for detail
You don’t hide behind data and tickets - you demonstrate top-notch business acumen and relentless curiosity to crack complex business challenges with clear recommendations
You thrive being embedded closely with commercial teams as your key counterparts - as our ideal candidate you have a strong commercial background having worked in a business role or equivalent in the past
You are an outstanding team player, able to coordinate cross-functional work teams and excited to handle time-critical challenges and decision ambiguity
You execute fast to deliver value to your global stakeholders without long iteration loops
Excellent English communication skills, both written and oral


Nice to have:


Practical experience working with Git (or similar version control system)
Practical experience with Python
Practical experience in A/B Testing: Experience designing experiments and analyzing results with a solid background in statistics


Additional Information

Ensuring you and all our Heroes are looked after, happy, and healthy is always on the menu. Because if you’re in good shape, then we’re in good shape.


Make the most of our hybrid working model and join the team for face-to-face connection and collaboration in our beautiful Berlin campus 2 days a week
We offer 27 days holiday with an extra day on 2nd and 3rd year of service
We will support you in developing yourself and your career growth opportunities: 1.000 € Educational Budget, Language Courses, Parental Support and access to the Udemy Business platform to explore a variety of online courses.
Get moving and release those wonderful, mind-boosting endorphins: Health Checkups, Meditation, Gym & Bicycle Subsidy
Cash. Dough. Cheddar. Whatever you call it, we’ll help you with it: Employee Share Purchase Plan, Sabbatical Bank, Public Transportation Ticket Discount, Life & Accident Insurance, Corporate Pension Plan
The power of getting together over some food is unrivaled. Here are a few ways to help you do that. All the yum: Digital Meal Vouchers, Food Vouchers, Corporate Discounts. Courses.
Wondering what relocating to Berlin is like? In this article, we’ve put together 10 things you should know about moving to Berlin and how Delivery Hero can support you. You can also visit our relocation hub and check out more information about moving to Berlin.
Ready to prepare for your interview? Check out the list of the 5 most common interview questions and answers created in collaboration with our recruiters.


Ready to join our team? If you’re excited to grow, collaborate and be part of the world’s leading delivery platform, we’d love to hear from you. Apply today!

We believe diversity and inclusion are key to creating not only an exciting product, but also an amazing customer and employee experience. Fostering this starts with hiring - therefore we do not discriminate on the basis of racial identities, religious beliefs, color, national origin, gender identities or expressions, sexual orientations, age, marital or disability statuses, or any other aspect that makes you, you.

We encourage you to let us know if you need any accommodations or specific accessibility support to ensure a smooth interview experience—just let us know with an email to our Inclusion Officer at inclusion@deliveryhero.com.

Severely disabled applicants with equal qualifications will be given preferential consideration.

You're welcome to share your pronouns (he/she/they) right from the start so we can address you respectfully from our first contact.

Title: Junior Tracking Specialist (all genders)
Description:
Your role

You will support the measurement of user behavior and E-Commerce data on our websites and possible future app(s). For this, you will help continuously evolve the client- and server-side Google Tag Manager setup, including the usage of accompanying features in Google Cloud, as well as improving the GA4 setup.

The position centers around supporting to set up the best possible tracking to provide high-quality data to be used in our marketing channels, for website optimization, and in BI reporting to facilitate further business growth.

You will be an important contributor to the company's website data setup for our 10+ country domains together with our Senior Tracking Specialist.


Continuously improve the quality and quantity of measured data in GTM and GA 4
Support implementing data quality monitoring measures and creating suitable reports with Looker Studio
Participate in shaping our measurement strategy to meet marketing and business needs
Take over parts of the maintenance of Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics
Educate stakeholders about the features and limitations of our tracking setup
Continuously monitor new developments in the digital analytics ecosphere
Collaborate closely with our marketing and data teams to solve business questions, create synergies, and drive business growth through data
Support marketing teams with requests


Your profile


At least 1 year of in-depth experience with Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics 4; App tracking is a bonus
Demonstrated success in the development of tracking setups
Experience with setting up Server-Side GTM in a multi-domain setup
You are accustomed to the tracking needs of paid channels such as Google Ads, Meta Ads, and others
Basic knowledge in SQL, CSS, and JS is a plus
Strong focus on structured approaches, documentation, and organizing maintenance and monitoring of data quality
High interest in closely collaborating with Data and Marketing Teams
Talent to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
An interest in how AI will augment work in data measurement and digital analytics is a plus


What we offer


An appealing discount on all of our products – from essential oils to vitamins & nutrients
We offer 28 days of paid time off per year plus up to 5 days off between Christmas and New Year's (pro rata), provided Sunday Natural is closed for company holidays during this time
The Urban Sports Club membership and either Swapfiets bike rental or a subsidy for the Deutschlandticket
We believe great things happen when we meet in person, which is why we embrace a hybrid model with one home office day per week – up to two depending on the area you are working in – and up to two weeks per year working from abroad
Receive various discounts of up to more than 50 percent on attractive brands such as Flaconi, Bosch, Apple or Expedia
You will be given real ownership and plenty of room for new initiatives, as well as the opportunity to contribute your expertise and implement your great ideas
Inviting office spaces with access to plenty of organic teas and superfoods from around the world
We like to celebrate each other! Regular tea & office parties, Christmas and summer feasts and various influencer events such as Women's Day


Apply with your CV and cover letter.

Apply for this job

About Us

Sunday Natural emerged from a long-standing passion and research in the fields of health, healing, and personal growth. The lack of natural, high-quality products on the market was the original motivation for founding Sunday Natural in 2013. We aim to produce the products we ourselves would love to buy – as natural as possible, absolutely pure, free from any additives, and of the highest quality. In a strategic partnership with CVC, Sunday Natural is now one of Germany's most renowned quality manufacturers, with its own research and development department in Berlin. For the continuous improvement of our products, we collaborate with selected research institutes, scientists, therapists, farmers, and professional associations.

We take our ecological responsibility very seriously: that’s why the majority of our product range is vegan. We also take an innovative approach to packaging: most of our products are packaged in glass, making them almost entirely recyclable.

Our diverse team in Berlin consists of over 350 employees from more than 40 nationalities and continues to grow – but at a sustainable pace. Our goal is to foster a collaborative, supportive, and inclusive work environment with a diverse group of motivated, talented, and visionary team members.

We highly value equality of opportunity and inclusivity, and we would like to particularly encourage women and candidates from under-represented backgrounds to apply, even if you don’t match with 100% of the requirements.

We aim to produce the products we ourselves would love to buy – as natural as possible, absolutely pure, free from any additives, and of the highest quality. In a strategic partnership with CVC, Sunday Natural is now one of Germany's most renowned quality manufacturers, with its own research and development department in Berlin. For the continuous improvement of our products, we collaborate with selected research institutes, scientists, therapists, farmers, and professional associations.

We take our ecological responsibility very seriously: that’s why the majority of our product range is vegan. We also take an innovative approach to packaging: most of our products are packaged in glass, making them almost entirely recyclable.

Our diverse team in Berlin consists of over 350 employees from more than 40 nationalities and continues to grow – but at a sustainable pace. Our goal is to foster a collaborative, supportive, and inclusive work environment with a diverse group of motivated, talented, and visionary team members.

We highly value equality of opportunity and inclusivity, and we would like to particularly encourage women and candidates from under-represented backgrounds to apply, even if you don’t match with 100% of the requirements.

Title: Mid Data Analyst - Finance
Description:
About the team:

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join and strengthen our Commercial Analytics Team within Finance, supporting SumUp’s strategic initiatives. In this role, you will partner closely with the Global and European Finance Planning & Analysis (FP&A) teams, building data models, creating insightful reports and dashboards, and delivering advanced analyses that drive actionable business decisions.

You will play a key role in shaping business impact while working alongside an experienced data team, contributing to the growth and development of our analytics chapter.

What you'll do:


Build and maintain efficient data models to track financial performance and key KPIs across the customer lifecycle—from acquisition to churn—including metrics such as recurring revenue, payback, lifetime value, and customer acquisition cost (using dbt)
Develop intuitive dashboards that enable self-service analytics for the Finance team and SumUp leadership
Partner with stakeholders to translate data into clear insights, combining quantitative analysis with business context to support decision-making
Contribute to data-driven OKRs aligned with SumUp’s business goals, such as merchant segmentation and performance tracking
Work collaboratively with cross-functional teams to ensure data is reliably collected, well-structured, and easily accessible for analytics


You'll be an excellent fit for this role if you possess:


3+ years of experience in data analytics and data modeling, ideally with hands-on
Strong experience building advanced reports and dashboards using Google Sheets or similar tools
Experience developing scalable, user-friendly dashboards following best practices, preferably with Tableau or similar visualization tools
The ability to translate finance and business requirements across different functions into meaningful KPIs, data models, and actionable insights
Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills, with the ability to communicate clearly and collaborate effectively with GTM and leadership stakeholders
A solid technical foundation combined with business acumen in finance, stakeholder management, and data visualization


Why you should join SumUp:

🌍 Opportunity to work with SumUppers globally on large-scale fintech products used by millions of businesses worldwide, from our Berlin office. This involves an office-first setup

🌈 Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion: Be part of a workplace that values and promotes diversity, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone's perspectives are respected and embraced

📚 A dedicated annual L&D budget of €2,000 for attending conferences and/or advancing your career through further education

🚀 Enrolment onto our VSOP program: You will own a stake in SumUp’s future success

💶 A corporate pension scheme where we match up to 20% of your contributions

🔄 30 Days Sabbatical: Enjoy the unique opportunity to take a well-deserved break with our 30 days sabbatical benefit after completing 3 years of employment with SumUp

🔗 Referral Bonus: Earn additional rewards by referring talented individuals to join the SumUp team

🚵🏾‍♂️ Numerous other benefits such as Urban Sports Club subsidy, Kita placement assistance, relocation assistance, subsidised office lunches

About us:

Be empowered to do more that matters.

At SumUp, we're on a mission to empower small businesses across the globe by providing simple and affordable tools that allow them to thrive. Today, over 4 million businesses in 36 markets rely on SumUp as their financial partner to manage payments, finance and customer relationships.

Our commitment to small businesses is reflected in our diverse team of over 3,000 SumUppers from over 90 nationalities, united by global collaboration and an innovative mindset. Our core values lay the foundation for who we are and what we stand for, shaping our work culture and driving our success. We foster inclusivity and a continuous learning culture, providing a safe space for personal and professional growth. Our differences make us unique and strong as we strive to create an environment where everyone belongs and feels supported, no matter how they identify.

SumUp is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer, actively seeking and embracing diversity in our workforce. We don't make hiring or employment decisions based on race, colour, religion or religious belief, ethnic or national origin, nationality, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age or any other basis protected by applicable laws or prohibited by company policy. Our commitment extends beyond recruitment to creating a safe and respectful workplace where harassment of any form is strictly prohibited.

Explore career opportunities with us and join a team driven by collaboration, innovation, and a shared vision of creating a world where everyone can build a thriving business. Discover more about our vibrant and inclusive work culture and how we work on our careers website, and follow our journey on LinkedIn and Instagram.

Job Application Tip

We recognise that candidates feel they need to meet 100% of the job criteria in order to apply for a job. Please note that this is only a guide. If you don’t tick every box, it’s ok too because it means you have room to learn and develop your career at SumUp.

Title: Data Analyst (m/w/d)
HTML:
Starte in unserer Bio-Molkerei!

Bist du bereit für einen Neuanfang? Wir bieten dir die Chance, in der spannenden Welt der Bio-Molkerei Fuß zu fassen. Werde Teil unseres Teams und gestalte deine Zukunft mit uns!

Zur Weiterentwicklung unseres Controlling Teams suchen wir eine/n Data Analyst (m/w/d)

Deine spannenden Aufgabenfelder:

  • Aktive digitale Weiterentwicklung des Controllings unter Einbringung moderner Technologien wie Data Analytics und Künstliche Intelligenz in die Prozesse;
  • Mitwirkung bei der Digitalisierung und Automatisierung von Prozessen;
  • Aufbau datengetriebener Entscheidungsgrundlagen mittels Advanced Analytics;
  • Unterstützung und Leitung von Digitalisierungs- und Optimierungsprojekten;
  • Erstellen und Analysieren von Kennzahlen zur Steuerung und Optimierung der Fertigung;
  • Durchführung von Soll-Ist-Vergleichen und Abweichungsanalysen, inkl. Ableitung konkreter Handlungsempfehlungen;
  • Pflege und Weiterentwicklung von Controlling-Tools und Dashboards (z. B. in Sopra, Power BI oder vergleichbaren Systemen);
  • Unterstützung bei der Budgetplanung, Forecasts und Investitionsrechnungen für Produktionsprojekte;
  • Fungieren als Bindeglied zwischen Controlling und Management.

Deine fachlichen und persönlichen Stärken:

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich (Wirtschafts)informatik, Data Science, Künstliche Intelligenz, Data Analytics, Mathematik, Statistik, Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen oder vergleichbare Qualifikation;
  • Grundverständnis von KI, Machine Learning und datengetriebenen Analysen;
  • Erfahrung in Digitalisierung von Geschäftsprozessen;
  • Kenntnisse im Projektmanagement;
  • Fähigkeit, Digitalisierungsinitiativen eigenständig voranzutreiben;
  • Kenntnisse in Kostenrechnung, Produktionsprozessen und Fertigungssteuerung;
  • Sicherer Umgang mit ERP-Systemen und Reporting-Tools wie Power BI;
  • Analytische Stärke, gepaart mit der Fähigkeit, komplexe Sachverhalte verständlich zu kommunizieren;
  • Proaktive, lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise und Freude an der Zusammenarbeit mit interdisziplinären Teams;
  • Strategisches Denken sowie eine unternehmerische Arbeitsweise;
  • Hohe Eigeninitiative und Freude daran, Themen voranzutreiben.

Was wir dir bieten:

  • Ein familiär geprägtes Umfeld mit flachen Hierarchien und kurzen Entscheidungswegen und WIR-Kultur;
  • Eine authentische, offene und wertschätzende Unternehmenskultur, mit regelmäßigen Feedback- und Entwicklungsgesprächen, während und nach der Probezeit, und Entwicklungspläne;
  • Ein unbefristetes Arbeitsverhältnis sowie abwechslungsreiche Aufgaben
  • Ein Arbeitsumfeld, in dem du gestalten, Verantwortung übernehmen und etwas bewegen kannst;
  • Mobiles Arbeiten (40 %) sowie Gleitzeit in der Verwaltung;
  • 29 Tage Urlaub, plus den 24.12. und 31.12. frei, steuerfreie Zuschläge im gewerblichen Bereich, Prämien für die Anwerbung neuer Kolleginnen und Kollegen;
  • Du erhältst einen monatlichen steuerfreien Zuschuss auf deine Sachbezugswertkarte in Höhe von 50,00 Euro netto zum Einkaufen, sowie eine jährliche Förderung für präventive Gesundheitsmaßnahmen;
  • Jobbike-Leasing, Corporate Benefits, BAV und eine quartalsweise Anwesenheitsprämien von bis zu 1.200 Euro brutto im Jahr;
  • Bei Krankheit des Kindes stocken wir das Krankengeld der gesetzlichen Krankenkasse (60 %) auf.

Deine Ansprechpartnerin bei Rückfragen:

Du hast Lust, das Team der Gläsernen Molkerei zu verstärken? Wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung mit Angabe deines Gehaltswunschs und deines frühestmöglichen Eintrittsdatums.

Wenn du Fragen vorab hast steht dir Nadine Reimann (Leitung Personal & Recht) unter +49 38873 3379-24 als Ansprechpartnerin zur Verfügung.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
This isn’t your regular job. Almedia is a place where those who want to push harder can accelerate their careers faster than anywhere else. We’re aiming to become Germany’s second bootstrapped unicorn. Almedia is already Europe’s #3 fastest-growing company in 2025 (FT1000).

We are building the future of marketing by rewarding our community of over 70 million users for engaging with our advertisers’ products. We are offering a new way to acquire users for the biggest companies in the world.

Data Engineer

You’ll design, implement, scale, and maintain the code that powers our production environment. With real freedom to innovate, you’ll shape the next generation of our data and backend platform—connecting APIs, pipelines, self-managed and cloud infrastructure to deliver fast, reliable, high-impact systems.

A highly technical mindset and a drive to improve daily are essential.

What You’ll Do 🎯


Design and build Python APIs and backend services that power internal and third-party integrations
Develop and operate end-to-end data pipelines, from ingestion to persistence across GCP services
Build high-frequency, event-driven systems for real-time and asynchronous data processing
Apply strong data modeling and database design principles for structured and semi-structured data
Write clean, testable, production-grade code with strong coverage and CI/CD discipline
Drive observability, performance, and reliability across backend and data systems
Own services end-to-end — from schema and API design to deployment and monitoring
Use modern cloud and automation practices (IaC tools like Terraform, containers, orchestration)


What You’ll Bring 💡


Strong experience in backend or data engineering
Strong skills in Python and SQL
Hands-on experience with cloud platforms (AWS or GCP) and data warehouse design (using BigQuery or Snowflake)
Solid engineering discipline — CI/CD, testing, microservices, and end-to-end ownership
Understanding of data modeling, schema design, and performance optimization
Familiarity with orchestration tools (Airflow, Dagster) and observability practices


Nice to Have


Experience with API integrations (CRM, attribution, or marketing systems)
Exposure to containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)
Knowledge of data testing frameworks or event-driven architectures


Why Almedia? 🎁


Own Our Growth: We offer all Berlin-based employees equity in Almedia to truly be a part of our success.
Scale With Almedia: Grow alongside a startup that has been profitable from day one.
Central Berlin Office: Work from a fully-stocked modern office built for collaboration, accessible from all around Berlin.
Other Benefits: Transport subsidy, breakfasts and lunches, language learning, Urban Sports Club, and more.


We believe in fostering talent, evaluating all skill levels during the hiring process, and providing a clear path for growth. Almedia is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace and celebrate diversity, and encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply.

Title: Senior Data Analyst (f/m/d)
Description:
Senior Data Analyst - Join Our Innovative Team at think-cell Software

Our Story

At think-cell, we create powerful software solutions that help professionals be more efficient and productive. Founded in 2002 and based in Berlin, our tools integrate seamlessly with Microsoft Office to help users create presentations, charts, and diagrams with ease. Our dynamic, international team values creativity, collaboration, and technical excellence.

What You Will Do 

We're a Data Team that operates like a scaleup within an established company: real ownership, short decision paths, and room to shape things. No corporate bureaucracy – but the resources of a mature business. Our team is made up of experienced senior individual contributors who work independently, challenge each other constructively, and collaborate closely on solving complex problems. It is an environment with a high level of trust, autonomy, and strong opportunities to learn from experienced peers across both technical and business topics.

You don't just analyze – you drive. You proactively identify gaps, inefficiencies, and opportunities for improvement, build the foundations to answer questions, and make sure stakeholders don't just receive dashboards, but know what to do with them.

Key Responsibilities:


Building and maintaining scalable data models and pipelines
Translating business questions into structured analytical approaches and communicating them in whatever format stakeholders actually need
Collaborating with teams across the company to improve data quality, transparency and accessibility
Contributing to the development of our data culture, standards, and ways of working


You will be great for this position if you have 


3+ years of experience in Analytics, BI, or a similar role
Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with dbt
Experience with PowerBI
You guide stakeholders – not just serve them, both communication and domain wise
A proactive and ownership-driven mindset
Interest in improving processes and increasing efficiency, including through ML/LLM-based approaches


Our Values

As part of our team, you will embody and help shape the following principles that define us: 


Forward Thinking: We embrace change and challenge the status quo.
Ownership: We take pride in our work and learn from our mistakes.
Customer First: We prioritize delivering lasting value.
Unified Team: We foster collaboration, respect, and integrity.
Strive for Excellence: We set ambitious goals and pursue quality.


Why is think-cell the right place for you?

We empower over 1.3 million users across 35,000 companies, including top consulting firms and major global enterprises. With offices in Berlin, Munich, Denver, Boston, London, Tokyo, and Dubai, we’re a diverse team of over 210 people, committed to innovation and excellence.

think-cell is more than a job – it's a chance to be part of an innovative, inclusive team that values collaboration and growth. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply. Together, we can shape the future of productivity software. We can’t wait to see what you’ll bring to the team!

Title: Freelance Data Science Engineer (Python & SQL)
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational data science problems that simulate real-world analytical workflows across industries (telecom, finance, government, e-commerce, healthcare)
Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Pandas, Numpy, Scipy, Sklearn, Statsmodels, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Research Analyst
Description:
Job Title

Research Analyst

Job Description Summary

The Research Analyst is responsible for research, data & analytics and reporting activities across the German market. This role is designed to support the organic growth of the German businesses by thought leadership and insight outputs to help generate leads and opportunities in the business.

The role will directly report to and work closely with the Research Team Lead, Germany to ensure consistency of client experience, processes and operational excellence across the EMEA region. The role will also cooperate with colleagues from local business services departments, as well as other central functions, mainly Marketing and Data & Analytics teams.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities:


Ensure real estate data collection and update of existing databases (office, industrial, retail, residential, hotel, investment etc.) to deliver regular market and economic updates, with specific focus areas determined by team structure, location, and other relevant factors.
Cooperate with other business services teams to gather, analyse, and utilize local and regional market insights, including competitive, client, and industry data, to produce market thought leadership.
Support business development activities, including presenting market updates to clients.
Undertake independent market analyses for ad-hoc reports for clients as defined by the business.
Support marketing and communication activities in line with the company content strategy defined by the Head of Business Development Services, Germany.
Contribute to C&W brand visibility through active participation in public events and social media.
Cooperate with C&W research teams in other countries and the wider EMEA team.


Knowledge & Experience


Strong analytical skills with expertise in data collection, statistical analysis, and market research
Ability to interpret data, identify trends, and assess market dynamics with accuracy
Excellent communication skills for translating complex data into clear reports, presentations, and visualizations
Creative problem-solving and effective collaboration in dynamic, deadline-driven environments
Proficiency in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, OneDrive, SharePoint); familiarity with Power BI, Power Query, and GIS tools (ArcGIS, QGIS)
Experience in professional services or real estate sector, aligned with role seniority

What We Offer

Early assumption of responsibility, flat hierarchies and an international and modern working environment, attractive and performance-related remuneration, flexible working hours with the possibility of hybrid work scheme, 30 vacation days plus special leave days, further training and development opportunities, company pension scheme.

Please note, we do not consider applications per E-mail.

We foster a culture of inclusion that embraces the unique strengths, perspectives, and experiences of all our employees. We firmly believe that our diversity enhances our team's capabilities, leading to improved decision-making, innovation, and business outcomes.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
About Parloa

Parloa’s mission is to make every customer conversation feel effortless for both customers and the companies serving them. As agentic AI accelerates, Parloans are shaping the foundation of a new era in customer experience — one where customer support is no longer transactions, but meaningful exchanges. It is not just a vision; Parloa has powered over ONE BILLION interactions between global enterprise brands and their customers, with companies like Booking.com, HealthEquity, Allianz, SAP, BarmeniaGothaer, and TUI already deploying Parloa at scale. 

About the role:

As a Data Analyst (Strategy & Analytics), you will play a central role in shaping how Parloa uses data to drive growth, efficiency, and strategic clarity. You’ll work across Commercial, Operations, Product, and Tech to turn data into actionable insights that inform decision-making at every level of the company.

This is your opportunity to combine deep analytical work with real business impact by building scalable data products, influencing company-wide strategy, and helping Parloa operate with greater precision and speed.

Areas of ownership:
End-to-End Analytics: Own analytics projects from problem definition and hypothesis building to data modeling, insight generation, and executive communication.
Performance & KPI Frameworks: Partner with cross-functional leaders to define KPIs, reporting structures, and metrics that drive accountability and focus.
Data Products & Reporting: Design and maintain scalable dashboards and reporting solutions that provide clear visibility into company performance.
Advanced Analytics & Modeling: Build forecasting and predictive models to support planning, resource allocation, and strategic decision-making.
Business Insights & Optimization: Conduct deep-dive analyses to uncover growth opportunities, operational inefficiencies, and product usage patterns.
Data Foundation & Quality: Collaborate with data and analytics engineering teams to improve data models, ensure data quality, and scale reliable reporting.
Who you are:
5+ years of experience in data analytics, business analytics, or strategy analytics, ideally in a high-growth tech, consulting, or finance environment
Strong SQL skills; experience in Python or R for advanced analytics is a plus
Solid understanding of statistical methods and machine learning techniques (e.g. regression, classification, forecasting)
Experience building dashboards and reporting frameworks using tools like Tableau, Looker, or similar
Strong understanding of financial data structures and FP&A processes, as well as commercial and operational metrics
Experience supporting FP&A workflows (e.g., budgeting, forecasting, revenue and KPI tracking) by transforming financial data into well-structured reporting layers aligned with accounting logic and business needs.
Proven ability to design metrics and translate business questions into structured analytical approaches
Confidence in communicating insights to senior stakeholders, including executives
Structured, hypothesis-driven mindset with a pragmatic and hands-on approach 
Our Recruiting Process:

Talent Acquisition → SQL Challenge → Hiring Manager → Technical Interview(s) → Bar Raiser

Why Parloa?

We’re at the beginning of a new era in customer experience, one where AI doesn’t just respond, but understands, reasons, and takes action. We’re building agentic AI that enterprises trust with their most important customer moments: complex questions, high volumes, real stakes. When millions of people reach out to a brand, those interactions aren’t just support tickets; they’re defining experiences. We’re here to raise the standard: making every conversation seamless, intelligent, and genuinely helpful. If you care about shaping how businesses and customers connect at scale and want your work to matter in real, everyday moments, this is where you do it.

At Parloa, ownership isn’t a buzzword; it means being accountable for outcomes, not just tasks. We operate in a category that’s evolving fast, where the bar is high, and the problems are complex. We hire people who think in solutions, communicate with clarity, and follow through. People who are comfortable making decisions, taking responsibility, and raising the standard for themselves and those around them.

We’ve grown beyond the earliest startup phase, but we’ve kept the intensity: fast execution, direct feedback, and a strong expectation to contribute meaningfully from day one.

Backed by leading global investors like General Catalyst, EQT Ventures, and Altimeter Capital, we’re scaling with a clear ambition: to become the global category leader in enterprise-grade conversational AI.

 

Parloa is committed to upholding the highest data protection standards for our clients' and employees' data. All our employees are instrumental in ensuring the utmost care, GDPR, and ISO compliance, including ISO 27001, in handling sensitive information.

Parloa is an e-verify employer in the USA. Please click here to learn more.

* We provide equal opportunities to all qualified applicants regardless race, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, national origin, disability status, socioeconomic background and other characteristics.
