Title: Data Analyst, People Analytics
Description:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

The People Analytics team at Lyft exists to build data trust — turning people data into clear, reliable insights that help our HR Business Partners, talent leaders, and executives make better decisions. We're a small, high-impact team in the middle of a meaningful BI transformation: we recently moved to ThoughtSpot as our primary analytics platform, and we're not just replicating old dashboards — we're rethinking what self-service analytics looks like for a People team. That includes exploring ThoughtSpot's AI capabilities to surface proactive insights, enable natural language querying, and reduce the friction between a business question and a data answer. There's real greenfield work here, and we're looking for someone who wants to help define what AI-powered people analytics looks like at Lyft.

We're looking for a Data Analyst to join our People Analytics team, based in Toronto. You'll report to the Senior Manager, People Analytics and serve as the first point of contact for incoming data requests across the People organization — triaging, scoping, and delivering on analytical needs ranging from quick ad-hoc pulls to fully built ThoughtSpot dashboards.

This role is well-timed: you'll be joining as we complete our migration from Tableau to ThoughtSpot, which means you'll have genuine influence over how we build our reporting layer and push into ThoughtSpot's AI features — think natural language search, AI-generated insights, and proactive anomaly detection applied to people data. This isn't a "maintain the dashboard" role. It's a chance to help build something new. You'll be a self-starter who is equally comfortable writing SQL and presenting findings to a VP.

Responsibilities:


Serve as the first point of contact for incoming data requests from across the People organization — scoping needs, setting expectations, and delivering high-quality analytical outputs
Build and maintain ThoughtSpot dashboards and liveboards for a broad stakeholder audience including recruiters, People Team members, HRBPs, managers, and executives
Explore and operationalize ThoughtSpot's AI capabilities — including Spotter and AI Highlights — to surface proactive insights for People stakeholders who may not know what question to ask yet
Build and administer survey programs in Qualtrics — including survey design, distribution logic, and close-out — and translate results into dashboards and ad-hoc analyses that inform People team decisions
Partner with Talent and People team leads to define key metrics and help them tell meaningful stories with their data
Translate analytical findings into clear, actionable insights for both People leadership and senior executives
Communicate fluently across technical and non-technical audiences — from SQL-level data discussions to exec-facing summaries
Collaborate with systems and operations partners to improve upstream data quality and enable better downstream analytics
Pull together ad-hoc datasets from disparate sources to answer discrete analytical questions quickly and accurately


Experience:


4+ years of experience in data or business analytics
3+ years of SQL experience, with comfort pulling from complex, multi-source data environments
Experience with ThoughtSpot or a comparable modern BI platform; enthusiasm for AI-assisted analytics and a curiosity about what those tools unlock for people data specifically
Familiarity with Workday or a comparable HCM (understanding the business processes and data model, not just surface-level reporting)
Familiarity with Greenhouse or a comparable ATS preferred
Experience with Qualtrics or a comparable survey platform, including hands-on program administration and the ability to move from raw survey data to a clean, stakeholder-ready analysis
Experience building and maintaining analytical models that others rely on
Understanding of the nuances of people and recruiting data — org structures, headcount definitions, privacy considerations, and the ways these datasets can surprise you


Benefits:


Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits
Mental health benefits
Family building benefits
Child care and pet benefits
Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account
RRSP plan with company match to help save for your future
In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service
Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.
Subsidized commuter benefits and Lyft ride credits

Title: Data Analyst, Autonomous Vehicles
Description:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

Lyft stands at the very center of autonomous vehicle (AV) innovation by uniting the operational power of comprehensive Fleet Management with our world-class Rideshare network. Through Flexdrive, we are revolutionizing transportation by building the operational backbone for AV fleets— pioneering this new chapter with our first AV depot in Nashville, Tennessee. Lyft brings the autonomous future to life by matching our community of riders with self-driving vehicles to meet their transportation needs.

To strengthen our efforts, we are hiring a Data Analyst to help us make data driven decisions. As a Data Analyst in the autonomous space, this role will uniquely be exposed to both AV Depot Operations and the AV Rideshare network. As we build the future of mobility from the ground up, we are looking for a strong thought leader and executor who is passionate about driving data driven decisions and energized by solving complex problems. You will partner with a wide range of stakeholders – Operations, Business, Finance, Product, Data Science, Engineering and many others. This role will report into the Senior Analytics Manager within Lyft’s Global Growth Org with opportunities for senior leadership visibility.

The ideal candidate - You have strong technical skills, are team-driven and have a growth mindset. You can roll-up your sleeves to undertake large cross functional projects. You are comfortable navigating the ambiguity of innovative projects and operate effectively in fast-paced environments. The Autonomous industry excites you and you are looking for the chance to drive high impact projects in this space as Lyft engineers next-generation transportation.

Responsibilities:


Leverage technical skills to build reports, dashboards and tools for stakeholders
Develop insights through analysis to build informed narratives, deliver strategic recommendations and provide actionable guidance that optimizes business performance
Work with external partners to communicate data needs that enable success in tracking toward critical service levels and manage incoming data requests
Work with business and leadership to define critical KPIs and metrics to support cross-functional teams, manage performance, create operational rigor and proactively seek ways to improve the business
Ability to engage with FP&A partners on P&L Management and financial modeling
Bridge Operations with Tech – become an expert in the vehicle lifecycle, from the day-to-day Fleet Operations that keep AV fleets running smoothly to the tech that transports riders from point A to B
Take full ownership in solving complex problems with data and rally stakeholders to implement solutions
Define processes and streamline the operational cadence for business performance tracking
Comfortable leading meetings, packaging work and presenting in large forums


Experience:


Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field
3-5 years of experience with a mix of business analytics in a high growth or complex business, investment banking with strong analytical exposure and/or role at a top-tier consulting firm
Deep understanding of complex data models, BI methodologies, data architecture principles and metadata
Proficient in SQL and working with raw data sets. (Python or other programming languages is a huge plus.)
Proven success in driving influence within the business
Ability to operate successfully in a lean, fast-paced organization and proactively thrive in ambiguous environments
Outstanding interpersonal and stakeholder management skills with a proven ability to work cross functionally & effectively with individual contributors, management, and leadership across the organization
Operationally-focused, process-oriented, data-driven, and simply able to get stuff done
Strong communication skills with the ability to package findings & technical concepts for leadership consumption
Previous experience in P&L involvement and/or Mobility or Auto-Tech industry is a huge plus


Benefits:


Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits
Mental health benefits
Family building benefits
Child care and pet benefits
Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account
RRSP plan with company match to help save for your future
In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service
Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.
Subsidized commuter benefits and Lyft ride credits

Title: Data Analyst
Company: Global Technical Talent, an Inc. 5000 Company
Description:
Data Analyst


Location: Toronto, ON




Onsite Flexibility: Hybrid — Every Thursday and every third Friday on-site


Contract Details

Position Type: Contract
Contract Duration: 6 months (extension possible up to 2 years; conversion to FTE possible)
Pay Rate: $50.00–$65.00 / Hour (USD)
Shift / Schedule: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (37.5 hrs/week)

Job Summary


This Data Analyst role sits within the Capital Markets line of business, supporting the CMDA (Capital Markets Data Architecture) initiative — a Databricks-centric data platform designed to centralize capital markets data. Typical work includes data ingestion, building data pipelines, enrichment, and modeling to retrieve and serve data from Databricks. The role reports to the Director of Application Services, with day-to-day Jira distribution managed by the Team Lead. The position exists to address work overflow and a long backlog of pipelines and requirements, and the contractor will pick up queued tasks on this strategic, long-running initiative. This is an intermediate role offering mentorship from senior developers and exposure to a global team spanning London and Toronto.


Key Responsibilities

Design, build, and maintain data pipelines on Databricks/Spark for ingestion, curation, and retrieval of capital markets data
Perform data enrichment and data modeling aligned to business needs and platform standards
Write clean, efficient SQL and Python for transformations, validation, and automation
Collaborate with the team lead (assigns JIRAs/work items) and senior developers; participate in code reviews and delivery ceremonies
Operate within Azure (preferred) and adapt concepts from other clouds as needed; contribute to documentation and knowledge sharing

Required Skills

5 years of experience as a Data Analyst/Engineer with big data and data pipelines
Strong SQL and Python
Hands-on experience with Databricks and solid understanding of the Spark engine
Experience with data modeling and enrichment
Cloud experience — Azure preferred (conceptual parity with AWS/GCP acceptable for ramp-up)

Preferred Skills

Capital Markets data/domain exposure (advantageous but not mandatory)
Experience with Azure native services around Databricks; familiarity with AWS or GCP concepts

Required Experience

Minimum 5 years of experience as a Data Analyst or Data Engineer working with big data and data pipelines

Additional Skills

Clear communication; ability to learn new data models quickly
Collaboration with senior mentors and cross-functional partners; ownership and delivery focus
Adaptability in a fast-moving backlog with evolving priorities

About the Client


Leading North American financial institution offering reliable banking products and personalized financial solutions.


About GTT


GTT is a minority-owned staffing firm and a subsidiary of Chenega Corporation, a Native American-owned company in Alaska. We highly value diverse and inclusive workplaces and support Fortune 500 organizations across banking, financial services, technology, life sciences, biotech, utilities, and retail sectors throughout the U.S. and Canada.

  • Shift / Schedule: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (37.5 hrs/week)

Job Summary

This Data Analyst role sits within the Capital Markets line of business, supporting the CMDA (Capital Markets Data Architecture) initiative — a Databricks-centric data platform designed to centralize capital markets data. Typical work includes data ingestion, building data pipelines, enrichment, and modeling to retrieve and serve data from Databricks. The role reports to the Director of Application Services, with day-to-day Jira distribution managed by the Team Lead. The position exists to address work overflow and a long backlog of pipelines and requirements, and the contractor will pick up queued tasks on this strategic, long-running initiative. This is an intermediate role offering mentorship from senior developers and exposure to a global team spanning London and Toronto.


Key Responsibilities
  • Design, build, and maintain data pipelines on Databricks/Spark for ingestion, curation, and retrieval of capital markets data
  • Perform data enrichment and data modeling aligned to business needs and platform standards
  • Write clean, efficient SQL and Python for transformations, validation, and automation
  • Collaborate with the team lead (assigns JIRAs/work items) and senior developers; participate in code reviews and delivery ceremonies
  • Operate within Azure (preferred) and adapt concepts from other clouds as needed; contribute to documentation and knowledge sharing

Required Skills
  • 5 years of experience as a Data Analyst/Engineer with big data and data pipelines
  • Strong SQL and Python
  • Hands-on experience with Databricks and solid understanding of the Spark engine
  • Experience with data modeling and enrichment
  • Cloud experience — Azure preferred (conceptual parity with AWS/GCP acceptable for ramp-up)

Preferred Skills
  • Capital Markets data/domain exposure (advantageous but not mandatory)
  • Experience with Azure native services around Databricks; familiarity with AWS or GCP concepts

Required Experience
  • Minimum 5 years of experience as a Data Analyst or Data Engineer working with big data and data pipelines

Additional Skills
  • Clear communication; ability to learn new data models quickly
  • Collaboration with senior mentors and cross-functional partners; ownership and delivery focus
  • Adaptability in a fast-moving backlog with evolving priorities

About the Client

Leading North American financial institution offering reliable banking products and personalized financial solutions.


About GTT

GTT is a minority-owned staffing firm and a subsidiary of Chenega Corporation, a Native American-owned company in Alaska. We highly value diverse and inclusive workplaces and support Fortune 500 organizations across banking, financial services, technology, life sciences, biotech, utilities, and retail sectors throughout the U.S. and Canada.

Title: Azure Data Analyst
Description:
Position Description

This role is hybrid and requires you to be at our downtown Toronto and/or Client office at a minimum 2 days per week - subject to change at any time.

The Azure Data Analyst will play a critical role in designing, analyzing, and delivering enterprise‑scale data solutions within an Azure cloud ecosystem. This role requires a strong combination of data design expertise, business analysis skills, and domain knowledge in personal banking products. The candidate will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and agile delivery teams to translate business needs into high‑quality, scalable data models and solutions.

Your future duties and responsibilities

Data Design & Architecture


Design and maintain logical and physical data models aligned with business requirements and enterprise standards.
Define end to end data mappings from source systems to target data platforms, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Perform data profiling to assess data quality, structure, and anomalies, and recommend remediation strategies.
Support the definition of data standards, naming conventions, and reference data.


Azure & Databricks Development Support


Collaborate with engineering teams to design data solutions using Azure services (e.g., Azure Data Lake, Synapse, Databricks).
Work closely with Databricks developers to ensure data transformations align with approved data models.
Review and validate data pipelines to ensure compliance with business rules and architectural guidelines.


Business Analysis & Banking Domain Engagement


Partner with business stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document data and reporting requirements.
Apply strong understanding of personal banking products (such as accounts, customers, transactions, lending, and payments) to data design decisions.
Translate business processes and regulatory requirements into structured data representations.


SQL & Data Validation


Write advanced SQL queries for data exploration, validation, reconciliation, and issue investigation.
Support user acceptance testing (UAT) and data validation activities by comparing source and target datasets.
Identify data quality issues and work with technical teams to resolve root causes.


Agile Delivery & Collaboration


Actively participate in Agile ceremonies (sprint planning, grooming, stand ups, reviews).
Estimate and plan data design and analysis tasks in coordination with scrum teams.
Maintain accurate documentation and user stories using Jira and Confluence.
Support continuous improvement of agile data delivery practices.


Communication & Stakeholder Management


Communicate complex data concepts clearly to technical and non technical stakeholders.
Lead data design reviews, walkthroughs, and stakeholder discussions.
Provide guidance and mentorship to junior data analysts or designers when required.


Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Core Technical Skills


Strong experience with Azure Data Platform (Azure Data Lake, Azure SQL, Synapse, or related services).
Hands on experience with Databricks for data transformations and analytics.
Advanced SQL skills for data analysis, profiling, and validation.
Proven expertise in Data Profiling, Data Mapping, and Data Modeling (logical and physical).


Business & Domain Skills


Strong Business Analysis skills, including requirements elicitation and documentation.
Solid knowledge of personal banking products and data domains.
Ability to translate business needs into scalable and reusable data designs.


Tools & Ways of Working


Experience using Jira for backlog and sprint management.
Proficient in Confluence for documentation and knowledge sharing.
Demonstrated experience working in Agile/Scrum delivery environments.


Soft Skills


Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Strong stakeholder engagement and collaboration abilities.
Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail.
Ability to work independently while contributing effectively to team goals.


________________________________________

Nice to Have (Optional)


Experience in regulated financial services environments.
Exposure to data governance, metadata management, or data lineage tools.
Familiarity with reporting or downstream analytics use cases.


"CGI is providing a reasonable estimate of the pay range for this role. The determination of this range includes factors such as skill set level, geographic market, experience and training, and licenses and certifications. Compensation decisions depend on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $80,000–$130,000. This role is an existing vacancy."

!

Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.

At CGI, we value the strength that diversity brings and are committed to fostering a workplace where everyone belongs. We collaborate with our clients to build more inclusive communities and empower all CGI partners to thrive. As an equal-opportunity employer, being able to perform your best during the recruitment process is important to us. If you require an accommodation, please inform your recruiter.

Title: Co-Op Student - Data Analyst
Description:
Req ID: 28272

Vacancy Type: Temporary

Contract Duration: September 2026 - April 2027 (8 months)

Number of Positions: 1

Closing Date: 06/05/2026

To be considered for this competition, applicants must be enrolled in a recognized post‑secondary co-op program and be eligible to complete a Work Term during the specified period. Applicants must be legally eligible to work in Canada. Proof of enrolment in a related program must be uploaded when completing the application.

Job Summary

We are seeking a highly motivated Co-Op Student to join our team as a Data Analyst student. This role offers hands-on experience with real-world data challenges and the opportunity to contribute to impactful projects. You will support your supervisor and team by performing analysis, preparing reports, conducting research, and developing data visualizations to support decision-making and continuous improvement initiatives.

Duties and Responsibilities


Assist with daily operational tasks and provide analytical support to your supervisor/manager.
Perform data analysis to identify patterns, trends, and insights that support business goals.
Conduct research using various methodologies and consolidate findings into reports or presentations.
Create dashboards and visualizations using tools such as Tableau or Power BI.
Draft documents, prepare reports, and track key metrics and project outcomes.
Provide support in customer interactions through email, phone, or in-person inquiries.
Perform administrative tasks including data entry, document filing, and basic troubleshooting.
Participate in customer engagement exercises and field visits as needed.
Assist in the development and implementation of internal programs, policies, and process improvements.


Skills And Qualifications


Must be enrolled in a post-secondary co-op program in Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Knowledge of statistical methods and their practical applications.
Proficiency in at least one programming or scripting language (e.g., Python, SQL, or R).
Familiarity with data management, databases, and data warehousing concepts.
Experience using data visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills with the ability to present findings to technical and non-technical audiences.
Demonstrated understanding of municipal government operations is an asset.
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
Comfortable working in a primarily office-based environment with occasional site visits.


Hourly Rate/Salary: 20.29 - 24.11

Hours of Work: 35

Work Location: Mississauga Transit - 975 Central Parkway, Mississauga

Work Model : This is an on-site role requiring presence in the office five days per week

Organization Unit: T&W/MTOP - Service Delivery

Department/Division/Section: T&W/Transportation&Works Dept , T&W/Mississauga Transit , MT Operations

Non-Union/Union: Non Union

The City of Mississauga may use artificial intelligence (AI) technology in sourcing candidates for some positions, however, it is not being used for screening, assessing, or selecting candidates.

We thank all who apply, however, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. You can also check your application status in your candidate profile online.

All personal information is collected under the authority of the Municipal Act.

The City of Mississauga is committed to creating a respectful and supportive workplace that fosters a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion which broadly reflects the communities and residents we serve. Throughout the employee life cycle, the City is working to include an EDI lens to attract, retain and support the growth of diverse talent.

Learn more about the City’s commitment to Equity, Diversity and Inclusion .

Accommodations

The City of Mississauga is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is committed to removing barriers in our selection process for people with visible and invisible disabilities. In accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005 and the Ontario Human Rights Code, the City of Mississauga will provide accommodations throughout the recruitment, selection and/or assessment process to applicants with disabilities.

If selected to participate in the recruitment, selection and/or assessment process, please complete the following Accommodation Request Form and have it emailed to recruitment.accommodation@mississauga.ca citing the Job ID and Job Title. This will inform Human Resources of the nature of any accommodation(s) that you may require in respect of any materials or processes used to ensure your equal participation.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Overview

Brother is a leading provider of home and office equipment, sewing and crafting products, and industrial solutions. With a legacy spanning over 115 years, our brand is renowned for producing award-winning printers, sewing machines, P-touch labelers, and more. Brother Canada was established in 1960, marking over 65 years of operations. Brother Canada is headquartered in Dollard-des-Ormeaux, Quebec and is a wholly owned subsidiary of Brother International Corporation (BIC), based in the United States. BIC is in itself a subsidiary of Brother Industries Limited (BIL), which was founded in 1908 in Nagoya, Japan and operates in more than 30 countries worldwide.

Why Work at Brother?

At Brother Canada, we’re more than a team; we’re a community guided by our culture drivers of Accountability, Authenticity, Excellence, and Boldness. We foster collaboration, growth, and work-life balance through flexible hours, hybrid work options, wellness subsidies, learning and development, recognition programs, and employee discounts. With roots in Montreal since 1960, we embrace continuous improvement and a culture of “Challenging Spirit,” offering stability while delivering a superior customer experience.

Role Overview

Reporting to the Director, Analytics & Insights, the Data Analyst enables better, faster and simpler decisions through data and science. They generate valid, reliable and timely insights for business operations and strategic initiatives, drive go-to-market tactics through effective performance analysis and ensures that internal customers’ needs are met, and that operational excellence is improved and maintained.

The Data Analyst works closely with the rest of the analytics team to build & maintain tools & processes that enable the manipulation and transformation of data across multiple platforms. They have the mandate to automate processes and imbed data science principles to enhance decision making processes. They meet with stakeholders to gather business requirements, translate them into technical requirements, execute development deliverables, test functionalities, address ad-hoc requests, execute data related standard operating procedures and manage stakeholder expectations along the way. Additionally, the Data Analyst builds, maintains and enhances reports and dashboards as part of their business intelligence responsibilities. Finally, the Data Analyst commits to enhancing customer experience and identifies plans and executes continuous improvement endeavors accordingly.

Duties & Responsibilities

Analysis & reporting


Understand and capture business needs from stakeholders to analyze and track the performance of their activities.
Gather requirements and prepare formal requirements documents.
Develop statements of work to secure buy-in from stakeholders regarding analysis methods, timelines, roles & responsibilities and deliverable formats
Develop new, enhance and maintain reports and dashboards in Tableaus.
Generate valid and reliable insights by designing analysis methodologies and executing analysis projects that are repeatable and that produce statistically significant results.
Address ad-hoc reports and analysis requests.
Communicate execution progress through written status updates

Data management and quality assurance


Build processes to manipulate, transform and extract insights from datasets across various types of data sources using Python & Pandas
Troubleshoot and perform root case analysis activities to resolve technical challenges and recommend scalable solutions
Formulate, maintain, and enhance ETL pipelines
Migrate databases from local servers into cloud data warehouses (AWS, Azure Databricks)
Transform, refactor, and deploy ETL & ML models to production level in a virtual or cloud-based system (AWS, Azure Databricks)
Assemble large datasets from various data sources for prescriptive and predictive analytics
Flag data integrity issues and execute quality assurance procedures.

Experience & Qualifications


Bachelor’s Degree specializing in Business Administration, Economics, Computer Science, Analytics, Computer Engineering, Information Systems, Applied Math/Statistics, or related fields.
Master’s degree is an asset (MBA, MSc, MMA, etc.).
1-3 years of experience in analytics roles.
Has practical experience with statistics and research methods (quantitative and qualitative).
Ability to articulate results and communicate conclusions eloquently.
Can analyze large data sets from multiple sources through efficient database tools & methods
Experience in understanding departmental needs and in building effective, positive partnerships to work collaboratively with cross-functional business teams (technical and non-technical stakeholders).
Advanced proficiency with Python, SQL, etc.
Knowledge of Python ML Libraries such as Sci-kit Learn and TensorFlow
Experience with big data tools like Apache Spark, Pandas and Polars
Knowledge of cloud-based systems like AWS, Azure and development tools like Git, Bash are a plus
Advanced working knowledge with relational SQL and NoSQL databases i.e. MySQLand formulating/manipulating complex queries
Knowledge of Tableau
Is detail-oriented, self-motivated and time-efficient.
Is flexible and can adapt quickly to an ever-changing environment.
Is capable of working in high-pressure, time-constrained situations.

Additional Details For This Role

Hybrid Work Setting – Enjoy the flexibility of a hybrid model, with three days working remotely and two days in the office each week, supporting work-life balance and meaningful in-person collaboration. Exceptions may apply according to business needs #atyourside

Our Benefits

We believe benefits should empower you to thrive at work and beyond. Our health and wellness benefits go beyond the basics, with medical, dental, vision, mental health support, financial advisors, and fitness programs to keep your mind and body in top shape. We’re big believers in recharging your batteries, with flexible vacation, personal days, summer hours, and a company-wide holiday shutdown so you can enjoy real downtime. Our retirement plan helps your savings grow faster with matching contributions and extra perks like a TFSA and mortgage benefits. And because growth fuels success, we invest in you with development programs, from LinkedIn Learning and Brother curated learnings to tuition reimbursement and personalized development plans. But it’s not all work, we love to celebrate! From BBQs to gala nights and our STAR recognition program, your wins get noticed and rewarded. At Brother Canada, we are truly At Your Side, supporting your success every step of the way.

Our Mission, Vision & Culture

Our mission is to live our “at your side” promise to simplify and enrich the lives of our customers, employees, and communities. We aim to be where people and technology meet, providing products and solutions that enhance how people live, work, and create. We look to our strategic culture drivers - accountability, authenticity, boldness, and excellence - to enable us to consistently deliver on our vision, mission, and shared values. These drivers help us shape a culture that empowers the business to succeed.

About Where We Work

Brother Canada’s head office and distribution centre in Montreal’s West Island is at the heart of our operations, housing teams in Marketing, Sales, Customer Experience, HR, IT, Finance, Supply Chain, and more. Our sales office in Mississauga, Ontario, and distribution centre in Richmond, British Columbia, support our coast-to-coast operations. We collaborate across Canada and Brother Americas - always At Your Side.

Equal Opportunity Employer (EOE) Statement

At Brother Canada, we strive to provide an environment where everyone feels valued by respecting individual differences and welcoming diverse life and work styles. We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate or make employment decisions on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

    • Advanced working knowledge with relational SQL and NoSQL databases i.e. MySQLand formulating/manipulating complex queries
    • Knowledge of Tableau
    • Is detail-oriented, self-motivated and time-efficient.
    • Is flexible and can adapt quickly to an ever-changing environment.
    • Is capable of working in high-pressure, time-constrained situations.
Additional Details For This Role

Hybrid Work Setting – Enjoy the flexibility of a hybrid model, with three days working remotely and two days in the office each week, supporting work-life balance and meaningful in-person collaboration. Exceptions may apply according to business needs #atyourside

Our Benefits

We believe benefits should empower you to thrive at work and beyond. Our health and wellness benefits go beyond the basics, with medical, dental, vision, mental health support, financial advisors, and fitness programs to keep your mind and body in top shape. We’re big believers in recharging your batteries, with flexible vacation, personal days, summer hours, and a company-wide holiday shutdown so you can enjoy real downtime. Our retirement plan helps your savings grow faster with matching contributions and extra perks like a TFSA and mortgage benefits. And because growth fuels success, we invest in you with development programs, from LinkedIn Learning and Brother curated learnings to tuition reimbursement and personalized development plans. But it’s not all work, we love to celebrate! From BBQs to gala nights and our STAR recognition program, your wins get noticed and rewarded. At Brother Canada, we are truly At Your Side, supporting your success every step of the way.

Our Mission, Vision & Culture

Our mission is to live our “at your side” promise to simplify and enrich the lives of our customers, employees, and communities. We aim to be where people and technology meet, providing products and solutions that enhance how people live, work, and create. We look to our strategic culture drivers - accountability, authenticity, boldness, and excellence - to enable us to consistently deliver on our vision, mission, and shared values. These drivers help us shape a culture that empowers the business to succeed.

About Where We Work

Brother Canada’s head office and distribution centre in Montreal’s West Island is at the heart of our operations, housing teams in Marketing, Sales, Customer Experience, HR, IT, Finance, Supply Chain, and more. Our sales office in Mississauga, Ontario, and distribution centre in Richmond, British Columbia, support our coast-to-coast operations. We collaborate across Canada and Brother Americas - always At Your Side.

At Brother Canada, we strive to provide an environment where everyone feels valued by respecting individual differences and welcoming diverse life and work styles. We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate or make employment decisions on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Position Snapshot

Business area: Purina

Job title: Business Intelligence Analyst

Location: Mississauga ON located at 2500 Royal Windsor Dr, Mississauga ON L5J 1K8

Hybrid

Position Summary

We are seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst to join our dynamic team, reporting to the Senior Category and Shopper Insights Manager. This role is central to data management, with a strong focus on maintaining data integrity and enabling reporting capabilities that support efficient operations and informed business decisions. This position is an existing vacancy.

A day in the life

You will play a key role in automating data workflows, improving reporting processes, and maintaining the tools and structures that support efficient data delivery. You will help design and enhance dashboards, streamline recurring reporting, and ensure business teams have timely access to accurate, well-organized data and insights.

You will also:


Leverage advanced analytics, reporting, and database management tools to automate data preparation, improve reporting efficiency, and develop scalable data solutions.
Build and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations that convert complex data into clear, actionable business insights.
Support with the development of monthly reports that address business issues and opportunities.
Maintain data quality and consistency across reporting platforms by ensuring accuracy, reliability, and standardized formats.
Champion insights literacy by supporting tool training and support cross-functional teams with data management
Manage research budgets, supplier onboarding, and monitor spend against forecasts and POs


What will make you successful?


3+ years of experience in Customer Insights, Category Management, or Data Analytics
Hands-on experience with Python and Power BI is required
Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word is required
Experience using syndicated data platforms (e.g., NielsenIQ, Numerator) is an asset
Post secondary education in Business Administration, Data & Analytics, Marketing, or related field.
Strong communication skills with the ability effectively convey findings in a clear and persuasive manner
Proven problem-solving skills with the ability to extract insights and formulate recommendations
Proactive self-starter with strong attention to detail, excellent interpersonal, communication, project management, and organizational skills


We have a friendly, supportive team with a coaching and mentoring environment. There are real opportunities for future development and progression – this really could be a move towards the exciting career you’ve always wanted.

At Nestle Canada, we are committed to transparency and fairness in our compensation and job posting practices. This position offers a competitive salary within the range specified above, in compliance with Ontario's pay transparency regulations.

Benefits:


Compensation Range: $81,000 CAD to $85,000 CAD
Comprehensive total rewards benefits package including Health and Dental benefits that start on day one of employment
Company matched pension plan
Three weeks of Vacation and five personal days (Personal Paid Holidays)
Excellent training and development programs, as well as opportunities to grow within the company
Access to Educational Assistance & Tuition Reimbursement
Bonus eligibility
Gender affirmation benefits to ensure access to necessary care, resources, and coverage for gender-affirming procedures
Fertility Services and Surrogacy Medical Coverage to support the pursuit of a path to parenthood regardless of medical conditions, gender or sexual orientation
Adoption benefits to remove some of the financial barriers associated with adoption
Up to 50% off – Nespresso Coffee Machine, Capsules and accessories
Access to the Discount Company store with Nestlé, Nespresso, and Purina products (Located across various Nestle offices/sites)
Bring your dog to work!


What You Need To Know

We will be considering applicants as they apply, so please don’t delay in submitting your application.

Title: Data Analyst (Canada)
Description:
Jobright is a next-generation AI job search platform built to make career navigation faster, smarter, and more personal. They are looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw product and agent data into the kind of insights that shape what gets built, how agents improve, and where the team focuses next.




Why Join Us




Analyze data from AI systems that are actively changing how people find careers, with direct impact on what the product becomes
See your findings drive real decisions, from agent tuning to product roadmap, not sit unread in a dashboard somewhere
Work alongside product, engineering, and research teammates who genuinely rely on data to move forward
Join a team that invests in data infrastructure and tooling, so you spend your time on analysis instead of plumbing




Responsibilities




Dig into product, user, and agent performance data to surface patterns that inform both day-to-day decisions and longer-term strategy
Build clean, well-designed reports and dashboards that make complex findings easy for product, engineering, and external partners to act on
Turn around data requests quickly and thoughtfully, translating fuzzy questions into clear analyses and digestible narratives
Work closely with your manager and cross-functional teammates to refine how agent quality is measured and tracked over time




Qualifications




Required




Recent grad or early-career professional with 0 to 2 years of experience in data analysis, analytics, or a related technical field
Strong communicator who can present trends and tradeoffs clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Working understanding of statistics, experimentation, and the underlying logic of AI and machine learning systems




Preferred




Internship or project experience in a data analyst, analytics, or research role at a tech or AI-focused organization
Track record of pulling practical, decision-ready insights out of messy, large-scale datasets
Hands-on skills in SQL, Python or R, data modeling fundamentals, and visualization tools like Looker, Tableau, or Mode

Title: Data Modeler
Description:
The Data Modeler will support AMI 2.0 analytics program by designing and documenting clear, analytics-ready data models within IBM Netezza. The role focuses on aligning business concepts with data structures to support consistent analytics, reporting, and data sharing across teams. This is a design and enablement role, not an ETL development or platform operations role.




Key Responsibilities:

Perform data discovery on datasets available in IBM Netezza
Design and maintain logical and analytical data models to support AMI 2.0 use cases
Define and align core business entities (e.g., customer, premise, meter, usage, time, grid assets)
Work with business, analytics, and architecture teams to clarify definitions and data requirements
Create and maintain data dictionaries, model documentation, and metadata
Support downstream analytics and reporting needs (e.g., Bidgely, Facts BI) through clear data structures
Assist QA and validation teams by providing data modeling subject-matter support










Required Skills:

Experience in data modeling for analytics or data warehouse environments
Strong understanding of relational and analytical data structures
Ability to translate business requirements into clear data designs
Experience working with enterprise data platforms (IBM Netezza or similar preferred)
Strong documentation and communication skills

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Job ID: 14239

Job Title: Marketing Data Analyst

Contract Length: 10 Months

Location: Remote from within Canada

Federal Government Clearance Level Required: Reliability Clearance

Vacancy Type:

- New Position

________________________________________




About Us:

Maplesoft Group is currently seeking a Remote Marketing Data Analyst for our Federal Government client.

________________________________________




Position Summary:

The resource will provide analytical and data support for the Salesforce Marketing Cloud (SFMC) Marketing Analytics initiative, helping the client strengthen the quality, usability, and governance of marketing data used for campaign execution, reporting, and decision-making. The role will support the transition to SFMC by working across business and technology teams to assess data sources, validate data structures, identify quality issues, and help ensure that marketing data is accurate, complete, and fit for operational and analytical use.

This service includes documenting business and data requirements, supporting data profiling and analysis activities, and contributing to the development of practical governance and reporting approaches that enable trusted insights within the marketing ecosystem. The resource will be expected to work in a highly collaborative environment, translating business needs into clear data requirements and helping build a strong foundation for sustainable analytics, governance, and ongoing optimization of SFMC-related data processes.

________________________________________




Key Responsibilities:

• The resource will be responsible for analyzing marketing and customer data, profiling datasets, documenting data definitions and business rules, and identifying data quality issues that could affect reporting, segmentation, campaign execution, or downstream decision-making.

• This includes gathering and clarifying requirements from business stakeholders, working with technical teams to understand source systems and data flows, and supporting the development of governance practices that improve consistency, traceability, and confidence in the data used across the SFMC environment.

• The role will also support cross-functional coordination by helping align business, analytics, and technology stakeholders on data requirements, priorities, and deliverables related to SFMC Marketing Analytics.

• The resource will contribute to documentation, validation, and issue resolution activities; support the preparation of governance and reporting artefacts; and help ensure that implemented data structures and processes are practical, well-documented, and aligned with broader enterprise data management expectations.

________________________________________




Required Qualifications:

Top Skills Required:

• Data analysis and profiling

• SQL and Python

• Data quality techniques

• Requirements, data definitions, and business rules documentation

• Cross-functional collaboration




Other Skills Required:

• Excel and Power BI

• Marketing / CRM data analysis

• Stakeholder requirements gathering

• Familiarity with enterprise data platforms / governance tooling such as Microsoft Purview, Azure, or Snowflake

________________________________________




Preferred Qualifications:

• Prior work on SFMC implementation in a Data capacity

________________________________________




Compensation:

Salary Range: $60.00 – $70.00 per hour

________________________________________




Our recruitment process is led by human recruiters who review all applications and make the final hiring decisions. We use AI-assisted tools to help screen and organize applications. These tools do not replace human judgment, and all hiring decisions are made by people.

Please note that data collected by the Company may be stored or processed on servers located outside of Canada.




________________________________________

Wednesday, June 3 2026, 12:00pm EST

________________________________________

Title: Data & Reporting Analyst - SQL (Power BI and Tableau)
Company: Astra-North Infoteck Inc. ~ Conquering today’s challenges, achieving tomorrow’s vision!
Description:
Data & Reporting Analyst


Location: Toronto
Work Model: Hybrid (4 Days WFO)
Duration: 12 Months
Top 3 Required Skills


SQL

Power BI

Tableau


Job Description


We are seeking a skilled Data & Reporting Analyst to join the analytics team and support data-driven decision-making across the organization. The ideal candidate will have strong SQL expertise, hands-on experience with reporting tools such as Tableau and Power BI, and the ability to build scalable reporting and analytics solutions.


The candidate will work closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and leadership teams to deliver actionable insights, optimize reporting processes, and improve data accessibility across the enterprise.


Key Responsibilities


SQL Development & Data Analysis


Write complex and optimized SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from multiple sources.

Improve query performance and ensure efficient data retrieval.

Convert repetitive manual queries into reusable stored procedures, functions, and views.


Reporting & Dashboard Development


Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Tableau and Power BI.

Build scalable and user-friendly reporting solutions for business stakeholders.

Support self-service analytics initiatives through structured reporting frameworks.


Semantic Layer & Data Modeling


Design semantic layers and logical data models to support consistent reporting and analytics.

Ensure data consistency, integrity, and usability across reporting assets.


Database Migration & Optimization


Support migration of stored procedures and reporting logic across database platforms.

Validate data accuracy and minimize disruptions during migration activities.


Stakeholder Collaboration


Gather business requirements and translate them into analytical and reporting solutions.

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Company: Mitsubishi Motor Sales of Canada, Inc.
Description:
Mitsubishi Motor Sales of Canada, Inc. is an equal opportunity employer fostering an inclusive, accessible environment. We are committed to building an environment where all employees and customers feel valued, respected, and supported. Mitsubishi Motor Sales of Canada, Inc. will support applicants requiring accommodation during the recruitment process.




All employees are required to espouse MMSCAN’s company values, MMC WAY, and conduct themselves accordingly:

Think of Our Customers First; Strengthen Trust
Enrich Society
Welcome All Facts; Share Difficult News First
Conduct and Challenge Yourself Professionally
and Respect All; Work as Part of a Broader Team




Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)

MMSCAN does not utilize AI to review or assess applicants. All screening is conducted internally by a member of the HR team and/or the hiring manager.




PURPOSE

The Analyst, Data (Marketing) will transform complex data into actionable insights that drive business decisions and marketing performance across Mitsubishi’s digital ecosystem. This role supports internal marketing teams and external partners by collecting, cleaning, organizing, and analyzing data from multiple sources—including websites, paid and organic channels, CRM systems, and ad-hoc reports.




The ideal candidate is naturally curious, detail-oriented, and skilled at turning data into stories that inspire positive change, recommending actions that generate business outcomes.




This role reports to the Manager, Marketing Operations.




RESPONSIBILITIES:




Data Collection & Organization

Gather data from digital platforms (web, paid and organic social, CRM, media tools, ad-hoc reports).
Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and taxonomy alignment across all marketing channels.
Develop datasets and visualizations (e.g., heatmaps, attribution models) that clarify digital customer journeys.




Analysis & Insight Generation

Assist the marketing team in identifying patterns, correlations or trends, and work alongside the Communications team on data-driven recommendations that will improve the performance and efficiency of digital campaigns.
Analyze website digital journeys and campaign performance to identify insights that would maximize upper and lower funnel goals (awareness, consideration, conversion)
Evaluate A/B test performance for creative, media, and landing page assets.
Segment and assess audience clusters to enhance marketing efficiency and effectiveness.




Reporting & Data Storytelling

Produce recurring and ad-hoc reports (weekly, monthly, quarterly) summarizing key performance metrics and recommendations.
Present findings, insights, and recommendations through visual and concise reports (charts, graphs, tables)
Collaborate with internal and external partners to deliver data-driven recommendations that inform strategy.




Tool Management & Optimization

Operate analytics and reporting tools such as Google Analytics (GA4), Google Tag Manager, Campaign Manager, and Looker Studio Enterprise.
Support optimization within digital platforms (Google Ads, DV360, Meta Ads, Search Ads 360).
Maintain and improve tracking, tagging, and measurement frameworks to ensure data consistency and KPI alignment.




REQUIREMENTS:




3 years of experience in Data Analytics and Digital marketing
Proven ability to extract, interpret, and present insights across multiple data sources.
Strong knowledge of digital marketing KPIs, attribution, and optimization principles.
Proficiency in Google Analytics (GA4), Looker Enterprise (or equivalent visualization tools).
Experience with tag management systems (Google Tag Manager), and paid media platforms (Google Ads, DV360, Meta, etc).
Critical thinker with strong problem-solving skills, creativity, and a keen attention to detail.
Excellent communicator who can translate complex data into clear, actionable narratives for senior stakeholders.

Title: Intermediate Data Analyst

Description:
Intermediate Data Analyst


Location: Toronto, ON




Onsite Flexibility: Hybrid — 1 day/week, every 4th Friday on-site


Contract Details

Position Type: Contract
Contract Duration: 6 months (extension possible; conversion to FTE possible)
Start: June 1st
Pay Rate: $50.00–$65.00 / Hour (USD)
Shift / Schedule: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM / 37.5 hours per week
Work Authorization: Applicants must be authorized to work for ANY employer in the U.S. We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time.

Job Summary


The HR Technology Team supports HR operations by delivering and maintaining enterprise-scale HR technology solutions. The team focuses on data integrations, ETL processing, SaaS application integrations, and ongoing modernization of HR data platforms (including migrations to Azure Databricks) to enhance HR business processes and user experience.




The Intermediate Data Analyst will support the HR Technology Team by designing, developing, and maintaining Azure Databricks–based ETL solutions. The role involves supporting complex HR technology projects, integrating data across multiple back-end systems and SaaS platforms, and contributing to the modernization of existing data workflows. The contractor will work closely with internal stakeholders and technical teams to ensure reliable, scalable, and efficient HR data solutions.


Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain Azure Databricks–based ETL solutions
Support complex HR technology projects
Integrate data across multiple back-end systems and SaaS platforms
Contribute to the modernization of existing data workflows
Work closely with internal stakeholders and technical teams to ensure reliable, scalable, and efficient HR data solutions

Required Skills

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Clear communication and ability to work with technical and non-technical stakeholders
Adaptability in a fast-paced, enterprise environment
Team-oriented with the ability to work independently
Willingness to learn and adapt to evolving HR technology platforms

Preferred Skills

Experience with ServiceNow / Jira
Informatica (not a deciding factor)
AutoSys
Feedhub integration

Required Experience

3–5 years of Data Analytics / Developer experience
3 years of experience with Azure Databricks
Experience working with Azure-based data platforms

Important Notes

Interview process consists of 2 interviews
Strong possibility of contract extension or conversion to full-time employment

About the Client


Leading North American financial institution offering reliable banking products and personalized financial solutions.


About GTT


GTT is a minority-owned staffing firm and a subsidiary of Chenega Corporation, a Native American-owned company in Alaska. We highly value diverse and inclusive workplaces and support Fortune 500 organizations across banking, financial services, technology, life sciences, biotech, utilities, and retail sectors throughout the U.S. and Canada.

Title: Statistical Data Analyst
Company: York Region (The Regional Municipality of York)
Description:
ABOUT US





Almost 1.2 million residents call York Region home, making it one of the largest regions in Canada – and the fastest growing with a population that’s expected to grow to more than 2 million by 2041. Our geography, which is comprised of about 1,800 square kilometers over nine different municipalities, is as beautiful, interesting and diverse as our people. Local government is organized in a two-tier structure and we work together with our local municipalities to provide residents and businesses access to a broad selection of services and resources.





WHAT WE OFFER





Consistently named one of Canada’s Best Employers by Forbes, we offer a collaborative, progressive workplace that takes pride in our organizational culture and is committed to living The 13+ Factors of Psychological Health and Safety in the Workplace – aligned with our vision to create strong, caring and safe communities both within and outside our walls.






Defined Benefit Pension Program - With the Ontario Municipality Employees Retirement System (OMERS) defined benefit pension plan, you can confidently retire knowing that you will have income for life. Eligibility from date of hire as a full-time employee and includes employer-matched contributions.


Employer of Choice - Recognized as the highest-ranking government employer in Canada and fourth overall among 300 organizations.


Benefits and Wellness - Employees and their loved ones have access to an employee health care spending account, access to a 24/7 Employee and Family Assistance Program and corporate discounts and purchase plans for day-to-day products and services. Casual employees are eligible to receive payment in lieu of benefits and/or vacation.


Inclusive and Diverse Workforce - We’re committed to fostering an environment that celebrates all dimensions of diversity and ensures everyone can develop to their full potential, participate freely in society and live with respect, dignity and freedom from discrimination. Our robust Inclusion, Diversity, Equity and Accessibility program continues to grow, and has been recognized by the United Nations and many other organizations for our leadership.






 



ABOUT THE ROLE

This position is responsible for the extraction, compilation, analysis, reporting and application of statistical data and metrics for programs and services; ensuring data integrity and databases meet data governance requirements. This role is also interpreting, summarizing, creating and maintaining statistical data files; writing reports and dashboards on trends and analysis; designing and developing complex spreadsheet and database applications; contributing to the development and monitoring of surveillance indicators and systems, and developing service profiles.



 





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING






Compiles, analyzes, models and reports on various statistical data from a variety of sources/databases to identify trends that will impact on strategic planning for programs and services and recommends appropriate service delivery strategies to address and/or mitigate the impacts.


Develops and maintains metrics, dashboards, visualizations and reports.


Monitors and reviews database information and conducts data audits, reviews and edits contents to ensure data integrity.


Coordinates and prepares comprehensive data analysis and statistical reports for research, policy, program and service activities and to improve operational efficiency and effectiveness. 


Designs and/or revises complex database and spreadsheet applications including the collection, manipulation and analysis of statistical data.


Prepares reports relating to budget and statistical data to meet Government, Department/Branch/Division objectives and mandates.


Coordinates the collection, reconciliation, submission and reporting of data from within the Department, other Departments and external organizations.


Develops service profiles, utilizing data from various databases and external sources; conducts benchmarking of other program/services to determine and develop best practices for reporting, and process improvements.  


Responds to inquiries and resolves concerns, as appropriate.


Performs other duties as assigned, in accordance with Branch and Department objectives.






 





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR






Successful completion of a University Degree in Business Administration, Mathematics, Statistics, Health Sciences or relevant field, or approved equivalent combination of education and experience


Minimum three (3) years experience in statistical data analysis and data management, including database design, spreadsheet applications and report writing.


Strong report writing, research, analytical and presentation skills and work to tight deadlines.


Demonstrated analytical skills to interpret and reconcile complex statistical reports. 


Demonstrated skills establishing, maintaining and producing complex databases/spreadsheets including current experience with database design, report generation and modifications.

Title: Data Review Analyst
Description:
As a Data Review Analyst, you will play a key role in supporting the Match Group Shared Care & Moderation Services Team by completing a variety of labeling requests. This role involves both straightforward labeling tasks and more nuanced analysis across multiple platform ecosystems. You’ll apply your understanding of Trust & Safety, detection systems, and moderation workflows to ensure consistent, accurate, and high-quality review and annotation.




 




You’ll be part of a cross-functional, collaborative team that directly supports product, data science, analytics, and policy teams through scalable, insight-driven data labeling and review.







How you’ll make an impact:

Complete task requests, including:


Structured labeling and annotation tasks


Ad hoc data reviews requiring critical thinking and contextual judgment






Analyze patterns and surface insights across labeling outputs


Follow documented guidelines to ensure consistency and accuracy in decision-making


Maintain confidentiality and data integrity across sensitive datasets






We may be a Match if:





Prior experience in Trust & Safety, content moderation, QA, or data labeling








Strong attention to detail and a commitment to high-quality output








Clear written and verbal communication, especially when documenting rationale or edge cases








Ability to interpret platform policies and apply them consistently across data review tasks








Comfort working independently in a structured queue-based environment








Experience working across multiple brands, languages, or international markets 








Willingness to learn and adapt across different platform ecosystems








Comfort with ambiguity in early-stage projects or exploratory analysis













Why Match Group?




Our mission is simple – to help people find love and happiness! We love our employees too and understand the importance of all life's milestones. Here are some of the benefits we are proud to offer:




Mind & Body – Medical, mental health, and wellness benefits to support your overall health and well-being

Financial Wellness – Competitive compensation, 100% employer match on 401k contributions up to 10% (cap at $10,000), as well as an employee stock purchase program to help you feel supported in your financial security

Unplug – Generous PTO and 14 paid holidays so you can unplug

Career – Annual training allowance for professional development and ERG membership opportunities and events so you feel connected and empowered in your work

Family – Families come in all shapes and sizes so we offer 20 weeks of 100% paid parental leave, fertility, adoption, and child care resources, as well as pet insurance and discounts  

Company Gatherings – We host company events where our employees get to know each other and build a sense of connection and belonging!




We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and we value the rich dynamics that diversity brings to our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, ancestry, disability, marital status, age, sexual orientation, sex (including pregnancy and sexual harassment), gender identity or expression, uniformed service or veteran status, genetic information, or any other legally protected characteristic.  Period. 




If you require a reasonable accommodation to participate in the hiring process — such as during pre-employment testing or interviews — please indicate this by selecting “Yes” in the accommodation request field. We’ll reach out to discuss your needs if you're selected for the interview stage.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Reports To: Data Science & Analytics Manager

Location: Montreal, QC (Hybrid)




About Us

Broadsign is a growing software company with a mission to make buying, selling, and delivering out-of-home media easier than ever. Our software is operated by some of the most successful out-of-home businesses and powers impactful, compelling campaigns seen across the world.




About the Role

As a Data Analyst at Broadsign, you will join our Data Science & Analytics team to solve real-world problems and generate significant business impact. You will play a pivotal role in supporting our data-driven product portfolio, working closely with commercial and engineering teams to ensure our clients succeed in the rapidly growing Digital Out of Home (OOH) media channel.




What to Expect

Data Operations & Product Performance: You will support daily and monthly data operations while helping to build monitoring and QA processes. This includes learning the methodologies behind our data products and ensuring high performance through detailed reporting.
Commercial Support & Problem Solving: Working with commercial team members, you will diagnose and address client-facing data issues. You will be a key collaborator for engineers, data scientists, and business leaders to drive data-driven decision-making.
Innovation & Independent Analysis: You will take ownership of independent coding and analysis projects to explore new methodologies. We expect you to contribute creative ideas for new innovations that will help shape our future product roadmap.




What you need to perform in this job

Technical Proficiency: Strong experience with SQL and business intelligence tools like Looker or Tableau. You should be comfortable coding in Python and have experience with Snowflake and AWS platforms. Experience with software engineering best practices (CI/CD) and GIS data and systems is a plus, as is experience with web languages/standards.
Analytical Mindset: Formal training in data science, including statistical analysis, visualization, and machine learning. Strong problem-solving and critical-thinking skills are critical for this role, as you will be asked to tackle problems that have not been encountered before.
Soft Skills: A natural curiosity to learn how things work and quickly embrace new technologies and tools. You must be able to communicate clearly on technical matters and be able to work effectively within a team while taking independent ownership of your tasks.




What we bring to the table

Comprehensive Benefits: Complete company insurance plan (health, dental, vision, travel) effective from day one, 100% employer-paid. This includes a $500 annual Health Care Savings Account (HCSA) and unlimited access to the Telus Health virtual platform.
Wellness & Fitness: A $500 annual Wellness fund to support your mental/physical health and office-related expenses.
Paid Time Off: 3 weeks vacation, 5 floater days, 5 sick/personal days, and 2 volunteer days.
Retirement Savings: Group RRSP with 50% employer matching up to 4% of your salary.
Financial Perks: Transportation reimbursement for travel to a Broadsign office.
Family Support: Parental leave salary supplement, including a 23-week maternity top-up.
Growth Opportunities: A dedicated yearly budget for training and professional development.




At Broadsign, we value the varied social identities that make up our community. We recognize talent comes in different forms and encourage applications that reflect different backgrounds and experiences. Our promise is to be an inclusive employer and partner, open to learning, with thoughtful strategies and practices that amplify the different voices of our industry.




Knowledge of French is required for positions permanently located in Quebec so incumbents can communicate with their colleagues and partners in Quebec as necessary. French-language training is offered to all incumbents in permanent positions in Quebec who do not have a good knowledge of French. Fluent English is required for this position in order to communicate with colleagues, clients and partners (or suppliers) located outside Quebec and to understand the technical and scientific documentation used in our industry.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
LayerZero

The Future is Omnichain.

Founded in 2021, LayerZero’s vision is to create a community of cross-chain developers, building dApps that are no longer constrained by individual blockchain capabilities. With LayerZero's simple, generic messaging protocol, builders will develop cross-chain dApps designed to unify the power of individual blockchains.

We are funded by the best investors in the world including:

a16z, Sequoia, PayPal, Binance Ventures, Coinbase Ventures, Uniswap Labs, Circle Ventures, Delphi Digital, and many more.

About The Role

As we continue to grow at LayerZero, we're faced with an exciting set of challenges: more data, more developers, more teams, more projects, and more opportunities for innovation. To tackle these problems head-on, we're expanding our data team with a dedicated Data Analyst.

As a Data Analyst at LayerZero, you'll be at the forefront of shaping a rich data foundation for a company that's making a real impact in the web3 space. You'll work closely with teams and leaders to uncover insights, drive decision-making, and fuel our next-generation products and services.

What You'll Do

As a key member of the data team at LayerZero, you'll be diving headfirst into the world of crypto data - exploring on-chain wallets and contracts, block and transaction data, insights from in-house systems, and third-party intelligence. Your mission will be to combine these diverse datasets into rich, actionable data products for a broad group of stakeholders.


Leveraging and expanding our ever-growing Kimball dimensional model.
Writing SQL to create and expand insights in our in-house reporting solutions.
Collaborating with stakeholders across the organization to conduct ad-hoc explorations and analytics - from engineering questions to help understand production issues to product insights that illuminate the broader crypto landscape.
Be a key owner of data quality, building out insights that serve the data team itself to help guide our data efforts towards cleaner and richer data.
Compose pipelines by writing SQL code to clean, combine, refine, and aggregate data into the insights the organization needs.
Collaborating on new datasets to ingest into our Snowflake data warehouse, working closely with data engineers on your team.
Not afraid of pushing code that supports tens of billions of dollars in daily transaction volume.


Who You Are

We are looking for someone with previous data analyst experience. Likely with a bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics or related field, but we also consider and highly value equivalent practical experience.

Required


Strong SQL knowledge and experience
Proven track record in data modeling, statistics, and analytics
Experience working with a broad range of stakeholders
Strong convictions weakly held.
You enjoy working in a fast-paced start-up environment, where you'll need to take initiative and solve problems independently.
You understand the importance of data quality through incremental data development.


Nice to have


Experience with general programming, we use Python and Go on the data stack
Experience with Snowflake
Experience building DAG based data pipelines
Experience with streaming real-time data pipelines
Previous experience with blockchain technologies, smart contracts, and decentralized finance
Experience with Kimball dimensional modeling
Working on a mid-to-large scale data stacks


Equal Opportunity Employer

LayerZero Labs is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. LayerZero Labs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, protected veteran status, disability, age, or any other legally protected status.
