Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Marketing Data Analyst in Canada.

This role sits at the intersection of marketing, data engineering, and analytics, supporting initiatives that ensure marketing data is accurate, reliable, and actionable. You will work with large and complex customer and campaign datasets, helping teams improve data quality, governance, and reporting capabilities across Salesforce Marketing Cloud environments. Acting as a key bridge between business and technical teams, you will translate marketing needs into clear data requirements and support the foundation for segmentation, targeting, and campaign performance analysis. This position offers exposure to modern cloud data platforms and enterprise marketing systems in a highly collaborative and innovation-driven environment. You will play a meaningful role in improving how data is structured, trusted, and used for decision-making. It is an ideal opportunity for a data-driven professional who enjoys solving data quality challenges and enabling impactful marketing strategies.

Accountabilities


Analyze, profile, and interpret marketing and customer datasets to ensure accuracy, consistency, and usability for analytics and campaign execution.
Identify, document, and support the resolution of data quality issues impacting marketing performance, reporting, and segmentation.
Define and maintain business rules, data definitions, and documentation supporting marketing data governance and standardization.
Collaborate with marketing, analytics, and technical teams to translate business requirements into clear data and reporting specifications.
Support Salesforce Marketing Cloud (SFMC) data readiness activities, including validation, cleansing, and issue resolution.
Assist in the development and enhancement of reporting frameworks and dashboards used for marketing performance measurement.
Contribute to data governance initiatives ensuring compliance, consistency, and reliability across marketing data assets.
Work with cross-functional stakeholders to improve data flows, integration logic, and overall data usability.
Participate in testing and validation of marketing data pipelines and datasets across platforms.
Support ongoing improvements to data processes within cloud-based environments such as Azure and Snowflake.


Requirements


Experience in data analysis, data profiling, or marketing/CRM analytics roles.
Strong proficiency in SQL and Python for data extraction, transformation, and analysis.
Hands-on experience with data quality assessment techniques and data validation processes.
Experience gathering business requirements and translating them into technical documentation.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex datasets and identify meaningful insights.
Proficiency with Excel and Power BI for reporting and visualization purposes.
Experience working with marketing or CRM data, ideally within Salesforce Marketing Cloud environments.
Familiarity with cloud data platforms such as Azure, Snowflake, or Microsoft Purview is an asset.
Strong communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Detail-oriented and organized, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.


Benefits


Competitive compensation aligned with experience and market standards.
Remote work flexibility within Canada.
Opportunity to work on modern marketing analytics and cloud data platforms.
Exposure to enterprise-scale Salesforce Marketing Cloud implementations.
Collaborative and innovation-driven work environment.
Opportunities for professional growth in data analytics and marketing technology.
Work with cross-functional teams across marketing, analytics, and engineering domains.
Inclusive workplace committed to diversity and equal opportunity.
Supportive environment focused on learning, development, and continuous improvement.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Analyst Health Data
Description:
REPORTS TO: Director, OHTN Cohort Study (OCS)

STATUS: Permanent, full-time

SALARY RANGE: Grade 2 - $58,978.07 – 72,535.59

LOCATION: 1300 Yonge Street, Toronto, ON (Hybrid)

Vacancy type: Existing Position

Employee Group: OPSEU Local 5120




ABOUT US

The Ontario HIV Treatment Network (OHTN), established in 1998, is a nonprofit organization with a mission to improve the health and lives of people living with and at risk of HIV by using data and evidence to drive change. We work with three main types of partners -- 1) testing programs and clinics; 2) AIDS service and other community-based organizations; and 3) policy and system leaders within and beyond the health sector – to support the implementation of the provincial HIV strategy, priorities, and directions.

ABOUT THE ROLE

The OCS prospectively follows approximately 4500 people living with HIV across Ontario, and collects socio-demographic, clinical, and psychosocial data from multiple sources, with the overall goal to improve health and social outcomes for people living with HIV in the province (ohtncohortstudy.ca).




The Analyst, Health Data is an individual contributor and reports to the Director, OCS. They are responsible for supporting data processing and quality assurance to ensure OCS data accuracy, reliability, and validity. Primary responsibilities will involve supporting data acquisition and processing, and data quality assurance in association with data collected through the OCS Data Administration Portal (ODAP) and the OCS questionnaire. The Analyst, Health Data will:




Support data acquisition and processing:

Develop and maintain data dictionaries, data maps, and data availability documentation
Carry out questionnaire mapping and online programming, including variable definition, response options, skip logic, and version control
Gather and incorporate site feedback on language, formatting, layout, accessibility, and cultural appropriateness of data collection tools (e.g., survey, data platform, etc.)
Support and collaborate on annual questionnaire update process
Conduct regular testing of OCS questionnaire and OCS Data Administration Portal (ODAP) system in collaboration with sites, internal OCS staff, and scientific investigators
Coordinate with OCS sites to ensure data format, quality, and consistency of electronic medical record (EMR) data is in alignment with OCS data systems.
Create, maintain, and update manuals and standard operating procedures (SOPs) for data acquisition (e.g., questionnaire, clinical chart abstraction, EMR data, and sub-study data) and participate in regular training with OCS sites to improve data collection quality and accuracy.




Support data quality assurance through:

Creating and maintaining documentation on data sources and data quality assurance processes and procedures
Establishing systematic processes to normalize and standardize data inputs
Carryout regular data cleaning and quality control procedures for questionnaire and clinical data in collaboration with research team
Support data quality assurance activities to facilitate data management and warehouse needs




Support data analysis as requested:

Support generating data cuts for researchers and data users
Participate in analyzing data and statistical analysis for reports, conferences and academic papers
Support the OCS team and perform other duties as required




ABOUT YOU




Bachelor’s degree or higher with a focus on epidemiology, public health, health sciences, health services research methodology, biostatistics

REPORTS TO: Director, OHTN Cohort Study (OCS)

STATUS: Permanent, full-time

SALARY RANGE: Grade 2 - $58,978.07 – 72,535.59

LOCATION: 1300 Yonge Street, Toronto, ON (Hybrid)

Vacancy type: Existing Position

Employee Group: OPSEU Local 5120


ABOUT US

The Ontario HIV Treatment Network (OHTN), established in 1998, is a nonprofit organization with a mission to improve the health and lives of people living with and at risk of HIV by using data and evidence to drive change. We work with three main types of partners -- 1) testing programs and clinics; 2) AIDS service and other community-based organizations; and 3) policy and system leaders within and beyond the health sector – to support the implementation of the provincial HIV strategy, priorities, and directions.

ABOUT THE ROLE

The OCS prospectively follows approximately 4500 people living with HIV across Ontario, and collects socio-demographic, clinical, and psychosocial data from multiple sources, with the overall goal to improve health and social outcomes for people living with HIV in the province (ohtncohortstudy.ca).


The Analyst, Health Data is an individual contributor and reports to the Director, OCS. They are responsible for supporting data processing and quality assurance to ensure OCS data accuracy, reliability, and validity. Primary responsibilities will involve supporting data acquisition and processing, and data quality assurance in association with data collected through the OCS Data Administration Portal (ODAP) and the OCS questionnaire. The Analyst, Health Data will:


Support data acquisition and processing:

  • Develop and maintain data dictionaries, data maps, and data availability documentation
  • Carry out questionnaire mapping and online programming, including variable definition, response options, skip logic, and version control
  • Gather and incorporate site feedback on language, formatting, layout, accessibility, and cultural appropriateness of data collection tools (e.g., survey, data platform, etc.)
  • Support and collaborate on annual questionnaire update process
  • Conduct regular testing of OCS questionnaire and OCS Data Administration Portal (ODAP) system in collaboration with sites, internal OCS staff, and scientific investigators
  • Coordinate with OCS sites to ensure data format, quality, and consistency of electronic medical record (EMR) data is in alignment with OCS data systems.
  • Create, maintain, and update manuals and standard operating procedures (SOPs) for data acquisition (e.g., questionnaire, clinical chart abstraction, EMR data, and sub-study data) and participate in regular training with OCS sites to improve data collection quality and accuracy.


Support data quality assurance through:

  • Creating and maintaining documentation on data sources and data quality assurance processes and procedures
  • Establishing systematic processes to normalize and standardize data inputs
  • Carryout regular data cleaning and quality control procedures for questionnaire and clinical data in collaboration with research team
  • Support data quality assurance activities to facilitate data management and warehouse needs


Support data analysis as requested:

  • Support generating data cuts for researchers and data users
  • Participate in analyzing data and statistical analysis for reports, conferences and academic papers
  • Support the OCS team and perform other duties as required


ABOUT YOU


  • Bachelor’s degree or higher with a focus on epidemiology, public health, health sciences, health services research methodology, biostatistics
  • Demonstrated 2 years of applied experience working with ‘real-world’ data
  • Experience with SAS, SQL, R, and/or other programming language
  • Demonstrated experience creating data documentation
  • Demonstrated experience with data cleaning, quality control and assurance
  • Familiarity with version control processes and software such as GIT an asset
  • Experience with Electronic Medical Records (EMR) data an asset
  • Knowledgeable about epidemiological research study design, data collection, and analysis
  • Familiarity with databases/data systems and information systems technologies
  • Understanding of ETL processes and pipeline creation an asset
  • Analytical mindset with problem-solving skills
  • Ability to work in both a team setting and independently



WHY WORK WITH OHTN

Defined Benefit Pension (HOOPP)

Eligible employees can participate in the Healthcare of Ontario Pension Plan (HOOPP) — a defined benefit pension plan that provides predictable retirement income for life. Eligibility begins on your date of hire as a full-time employee.


Benefits and Wellness

Employees and their eligible dependents have access to a comprehensive employer-paid benefits program, including extended health, dental, and life insurance, and a 24/7 Employee Assistance Program (EAP).

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re searching for a Data Analyst. Are you searching for new possibilities?

Westland Insurance Group Ltd is growing and has an exciting opportunity for an Data Analyst to join our growing team. This hybrid role is based out of our Head Office in Surrey, BC.

Expected Compensation: The expected hiring range for this role is $62,400- $78,000 with a 37.5-hour work week. Your rate of pay will be determined based on your individual qualifications and experience, while considering internal equity. 

Westland also offers a competitive annual short-term incentive program that enables employees to share in the rewards of the business and emphasizes the measures that are key to our success.

Westland is committed to continuous learning where employees can grow their potential and write the stories of their careers. Financial assistance is available for approved work-related training/development courses leading to certification, and programs that offer professional development, including seminars and conferences.

Discover What’s Possible, With Westland.

The Westland story is all about growth, and that means plenty of possibility for everyone on our team. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of clients, communities, and each other with equal commitment. As we continue to open new doors, we're inviting amazing people like you to join us.

As a Data Analyst, you’ll have the unique opportunity to lay the foundation for how Westland can leverage data to make important decisions.

Interested in learning more?

Why You'll Love Westland


An open, flexible and welcoming workplace
Plenty of opportunities to grow and learn
Autonomy to drive your own success
Be part of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds
A total rewards program that takes care of your financial, physical, and mental health
Flexible paid Values Days to celebrate days important to you
Paid Volunteer Days for employees as part of Amplifying Communities, a program to support causes that matter most to our employees and clients


Why We’ll Love You


You strive to understand the data and love seeking ways to improve the quality of the data and reporting
You enjoy collaborating with others to identify and prioritize use cases for analysis and build out models to answer business questions
You love numbers and have a strong background in math, statistics or equivalent such as a bachelor’s degree in those areas or related fields
You can translate complex information and findings for business leaders
At least two years’ experience in a data analytics role
Previous experience with data analysis and modeling using SQL, R, and Python
Bonus: Experience in P&C insurance
Bonus: Experience using data platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake or Microsoft Fabric Bonus: Experience with Git based version control on platforms such as GitHub or Azure DevOps


Once Here, You'll


Be a contributing member of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds.
Working with IT and others to design and improve the quality of our data and reporting by understanding current and future data analysis needs, business opportunities and risks.
Continuously seek out and identify areas to improve data reporting practices, data quality, and processes
Prepare and present findings to diverse audiences, including partners


Want to get to know each other better? Send your resume our way.

You belong here

We strive to be much more than a great place to work. Here, you join a collective of incredibly vibrant, purpose driven, and open-minded people. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of communities, clients, and each other with equal commitment. You will be supported and respected for who you are and for the voice you add to the conversation.

At Westland, you will feel the power of community. Westland Insurance Ltd. is an inclusive organization that values diversity in its workforce. We listen, champion equity and diversity, and create safe welcoming spaces where everyone has a voice and is free to be themselves. Westland Insurance Ltd. encourages applications from all qualified individuals and will accommodate applicants' disability-related needs, up to the point of undue hardship, throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.

If you require a disability-related accommodation in order to participate in the recruitment process, please contact the recruitment team by email at careers@westlandinsurance.ca

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

UHN is Canada’s #1 hospital and the world’s #1 publicly funded hospital. With 10 sites and more than 44,000 TeamUHN members, UHN consists of Toronto General Hospital, Toronto Western Hospital, Princess Margaret Cancer Centre, Toronto Rehabilitation Institute, The Michener Institute of Education and West Park Healthcare Centre. As Canada's top research hospital, the scope of biomedical research and complexity of cases at UHN have made it a national and international source for discovery, education and patient care. UHN has the largest hospital-based research program in Canada, with major research in neurosciences, cardiology, transplantation, oncology, surgical innovation, infectious diseases, genomic medicine and rehabilitation medicine. UHN is a research hospital affiliated with the University of Toronto.

UHN’s vision is to build A Healthier World and it’s only because of the talented and dedicated people who work here that we are continually bringing that vision closer to reality.

www.uhn.ca

Job Description

Union: Non-Union

Replacement or New: New

Site: Hydro Place - 700 University Avenue

Department: Finance – Corporate Billing and Accounts Receivable

Reports to: Senior Manager, Finance

Hours: 37.5 per week

Salary: $68,932 to $103,408 annually (To commensurate with experience and consistent with UHN compensation policy)

Status: Permanent Full-Time

Closing Date: June 10, 2026

Position Summary

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Revenue within Corporate Billing and Accounts Receivable (CBAR), the Data Analyst supports revenue reporting with a focus on new revenue generation and identifying revenue leakage. The role is responsible for analyzing data, developing and maintaining reports and dashboards, and translating business requirements into actionable insights, while also supporting ad hoc reporting, stakeholder collaboration, and basic project coordination activities.

Duties


Analyze and interpret data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities related to revenue generation and leakage
Deliver actionable insights and business recommendations to support informed decision-making
Perform advanced data analysis using tools such as Excel, Power BI, SQL, and other query languages
Leverage AI tools and techniques to enhance analytical depth, forecasting, and insight generation
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into meaningful analytical outputs
Design and develop insightful reports and dashboards that highlight key performance indicators and drivers
Evaluate existing reports and data flows, recommending improvements to better support business objectives
Proactively identify revenue optimization opportunities and areas for process or financial improvement
Apply structured problem-solving and critical thinking to complex data and business challenges
Communicate findings clearly and effectively, providing regular updates and strategic input to stakeholders



Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Business Administration, or a recognized equivalent
Minimum 2-3 years of related experience, or an equivalent combination of education and experience
Experience in data management and analytics
Proficiency with data analysis tools such as Excel, Power BI, SQL, and other query languages
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment



Additional Information

Why join UHN?

In addition to working alongside some of the most talented and inspiring healthcare professionals in the world, UHN offers a wide range of benefits, programs and perks. It is the comprehensiveness of these offerings that makes it a differentiating factor, allowing you to find value where it matters most to you, now and throughout your career at UHN.


Competitive offer packages
Government organization and a member of the Healthcare of Ontario Pension Plan (HOOPP https://hoopp.com/)
Close access to Transit and UHN shuttle service
A flexible work environment
Opportunities for development and promotions within a large organization
Additional perks (multiple corporate discounts including: travel, restaurants, parking, phone plans, auto insurance discounts, on-site gyms, etc.)



Current UHN employees must have successfully completed their probationary period, have a good employee record along with satisfactory attendance in accordance with UHN's attendance management program, to be eligible for consideration.

All applications must be submitted before the posting close date.

UHN uses email to communicate with selected candidates. Please ensure you check your email regularly.

Please be advised that a Criminal Record Check may be required of the successful candidate. Should it be determined that any information provided by a candidate be misleading, inaccurate or incorrect, UHN reserves the right to discontinue with the consideration of their application.

UHN is an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive recruitment process and workplace. Requests for accommodation can be made at any stage of the recruitment process. Applicants need to make their requirements known.

We thank all applicants for their interest, however, only those selected for further consideration will be contacted.

Title: Manager, Data Analyst
Description:
Requisition ID: 259823

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Manager, Data Analyst will work with Canadian Wealth Management Data Operations to provide data-driven solutions to various Wealth business applications data. This position involves maintaining data extraction, transformation processes, and reporting development to support regulatory, compliance and ongoing business operations.

Is this role right for you? In this role you will:


Work closely with the Canadian Wealth Management Operations team to support day-to-day reporting activities
Deliver the daily scheduled reports and support Adhoc reporting requirements with complete data analysis and provide the technical solution to meet the customer requirements
Work with data investigation, analysis, develop and maintain a strong knowledge of wealth management business workflow to deliver the expected solution
As a Senior Business Analyst Specialist who ensures re-engineering, you will work with the business to understand existing functionalities, and develop new solutions, and write scripts for analysis, testing and regression, understanding relationship database structure and how the data maps to various business application
Identify programming / analysis skill gaps and keep up with the new data analysis tools and programming software to consistently improve self and existing processes.
Be responsible for performing heavy data-driven analytics and reconciliations for complex calculation logic in various wealth application data which is highly statistical and computation intensive


Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? – We’d love to work with you if you have:


A University degree in quantitative discipline (mathematics, science, computer science, or engineering)
A minimum of 5 years of industry and business analysis experience
An understanding in wealth business management
Data Knowledge in wealth or financial related business
In-depth knowledge of relational database concepts
In-depth knowledge of Data analytics and Business intelligence
SQL, AS400, Microsoft Access, Microsoft VBA, or VB .Net, and Sybase
Experience using Power BI, Python, Databricks is an asset
Experience with GCP is an asset
Experience in AI is an asset
The ability to adapt to change quickly and adjust work accordingly in a positive manner
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with a high level of attention to detail


Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Tucows (NASDAQ:TCX, TSX:TC) is possibly the biggest Internet company you’ve never heard of. We started as a simple shareware site in 1993 and have since grown into a stable of businesses: Tucows Domains, Ting Internet and Wavelo.

We embrace a people-first philosophy that is rooted in respect, trust, and flexibility. We believe that whatever works for our employees is what works best for us. It’s also why the majority of our roles are remote-first, meaning you can work from anywhere you can connect to the Internet!

Today, over one thousand people work in over 20 countries to help us make the Internet better. If this sounds exciting to you, join the herd!

About The Role

We’re looking for a curious, technically strong, and business-minded Data Analyst to join our Data Engineering team. This is a high-impact analytics position for someone who works at the intersection of data engineering, business intelligence, finance analytics, and AI-enabled decision support.

You’ll partner closely with Finance, Operations, Data Engineering, and business leadership to turn complex questions into trusted, actionable insights — with a particular focus on supporting the Finance organization. This role reports to the Data Engineering Manager.

What You’ll Do


SQL & Business Analysis: Translate ambiguous business questions into precise SQL queries against our Snowflake warehouse. Own and deliver recurring financial reporting, reconciliation, and operational analytics with an eye for improving efficiency over time.
Diagnostic & Advanced Analytics: Go beyond dashboards to explain why metrics move. Conduct root cause analyses, build forecasting models, and support decision-making through churn analysis, customer segmentation, CLV, A/B testing, and similar techniques.
Business Intelligence & Self-Service Analytics: Build, maintain, and optimize Looker dashboards, Explores, and LookML definitions that provide governed, reliable, self-service analytics. Govern the semantic layer — metrics, dimensions, and business logic — that powers trusted reporting and AI-enabled analytics. Train business users to leverage these tools confidently.
Finance Partnership & Compliance: Serve as the primary analytical partner to the Finance team. Own financial reporting, reconciliation, and forecasting workflows. Support SOX audits and ITAC testing, ensuring financial data is accurate, auditable, and well-documented.
Data Quality & Cross-Functional Collaboration: Monitor and improve data quality across analytical pipelines, especially for finance-critical datasets. Gather requirements from stakeholders, translate business needs into technical specs, and collaborate with Data and Analytics Engineers to resolve upstream issues.



What You Bring

Must Have


Experience: 3+ years in a Data Analyst, Analytics Engineer, or BI Analyst role in a modern cloud data stack environment.
SQL mastery: Advanced SQL including CTEs, window functions, joins, and performance-aware querying in Snowflake or a comparable cloud warehouse.
Looker expertise: Hands-on experience with Looker: dashboard development, Explores, LookML, governed metrics, and enabling self-service analytics for business users.
dbt proficiency: Ability to read, maintain, test, and document dbt models. Familiarity with sources, tests, macros, lineage, and dbt development workflows.
Finance domain knowledge: Solid understanding of GL, journal entries, trial balance, financial statements, reconciliations, and period-close processes. SOX/ITAC experience is highly valued.
Data modeling: Working knowledge of dimensional modeling — fact/dimension tables, star schemas, slowly changing dimensions.
Statistical & predictive analysis: Applied experience with regression, classification, A/B testing, segmentation, and forecasting using SQL, Python, or similar tools.
Communication: Ability to explain complex analysis clearly to both Finance leadership and technical peers. Strong written and verbal skills.
Version control: Comfortable with Git/GitHub: branching, PRs, and collaborative development in a terminal environment.
AI & LLM analytics: Experience with LLMs or AI-assisted analytics tools (Claude, Gemini, GPT, Looker Conversational Analytics). Exposure to Cortex Analyst, Cortex Search, or AISQL capabilities. Understanding of MCP integration patterns is a plus.



Nice to Have


Telecom / subscription domain: Experience in telco, connectivity, or subscription environments (MVNOs, ISPs). Familiarity with churn, ARPU, MRR, billing, and provisioning metrics.
Workflow orchestration: Familiarity with Airflow, Dagster, or Prefect for understanding scheduled pipelines and diagnosing failures.
Certifications: Looker, Snowflake, dbt, or cloud platform certifications.



The base salary range for this position is $79,650 - $88,500. Range shown in $CAD for Canadian residents. Other countries will differ. Range may vary on a number of factors including, but not limited to: location, experience and qualifications. Tucows believes in a total rewards offering that includes fair compensation and generous benefits

We also know that diversity drives innovation. We are committed to inclusion across race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status or disability status. We celebrate multiple approaches and diverse points of view.

We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request an accommodation.

We use AI-enabled tools throughout our recruitment process to help us work more efficiently and consistently. These tools support our hiring teams by organizing and reviewing information, while final hiring decisions are always made by people.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Technology

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Greater Montreal Metropolitan Area, Canada

Reporting To: Software Engineering Manager

Description

Le Data Analyst sera responsable de la conception, de la création et de la maintenance de la couche analytique ainsi que des rapports destinés aux clients, reposant sur la plateforme Compliance Pro.

Au sein de l’équipe Reporting, ce rôle consiste à traduire des exigences complexes en matière de conformité et de sécurité en tableaux de bord robustes et en rapports tabulaires qui répondent aux besoins des utilisateurs finaux. Le Data Analyst sera responsable de la livraison de bout en bout : modélisation des données, création et mise à jour des tableaux de bord et des widgets, définition des meilleures représentations des données, ainsi que garantie de l’exactitude, de la performance et de la cohérence des rapports avec les données affichées dans CPro.

Ce rôle est fortement orienté client : une grande partie du travail consiste à créer et mettre à jour des rapports personnalisés pour des opérateurs spécifiques, à résoudre des problèmes en production et à s’assurer que les rapports critiques pour l’entreprise sont fiables et à jour.

Construire des solutions innovantes ; permettre des lieux de travail plus sûrs pour tous.

Nous allons créer un monde du travail plus sûr, en développant des logiciels pour soutenir un réseau mondial d'acheteurs, de fournisseurs et de partenaires responsables.

Aujourd'hui, chez Veriforce, nous soulageons plus de 50 000 organisations à travers le monde des contraintes de conformité, en les aidant à protéger leurs employés, leurs activités et la planète.

Nous veillons à ce que notre réseau de clients, de fournisseurs et de sous-traitants respecte les normes les plus importantes, qu'il s'agisse de santé, de sécurité, de développement durable ou de comportement éthique, en élaborant les meilleures solutions du marché.

La technologie que nous construisons aujourd'hui créera un avenir meilleur.

{Insérer une déclaration d'impact tirée du dossier d'appel d'offres.}

Anglais De Niveau Avancé

Cette exigence est requise puisque dans le cadre de cette fonction, vous aurez à intervenir auprès des clients, partenaires et/ou nos filiales internationaux sur une base récurrente tant à l’oral qu’à l’écrit.

Dans le présent document, le genre masculin est utilisé dans le seul but d'alléger le texte

Qu'est-ce Que Cela Signifie Au Jour Le Jour


Créer et améliorer des tableaux de bord et des rapports spécifiques aux clients
Assurer la mise en valeur des données (« data storytelling ») afin que celles-ci soient pertinentes et exploitables pour la prise de décision
Veiller à ce que les rapports Sisense reflètent fidèlement les données de l’application en production, tant en termes de valeurs que de mise en forme
Diagnostiquer et résoudre les incidents en production
Collaborer étroitement avec les Product Owners, les équipes QA, les parties prenantes internes et les équipes en contact avec les clients afin d’affiner les critères d’acceptation, clarifier la logique des données et valider les rapports nouveaux ou mis à jour
Mettre en place et maintenir des standards de reporting cohérents à travers l’environnement Sisense et les produits de reporting
Collaborer avec les équipes d’ingénierie et de données pour identifier et modéliser de nouvelles sources de données lorsque nécessaire
Maintenir une documentation technique claire afin de faciliter la maintenance et l’intégration des nouveaux collaborateurs
Participer aux rituels agiles, utiliser Jira pour gérer les tâches et fournir des estimations, des mises à jour d’avancement et des livrables clairs pour le QAT


Ce Dont Vous Aurez Besoin Pour Réussir


Expérience pratique dans le développement de solutions analytiques avec Sisense (ou une plateforme BI similaire), incluant la création de tableaux de bord complexes et de rapports tabulaires avec filtres, widgets et champs personnalisés
Solides compétences en SQL et expérience en modélisation de données pour le reporting (jointures, agrégations, champs calculés, conversions de types de données, optimisation des performances)
Expérience démontrée dans la gestion de rapports critiques en environnement de production, incluant :
L’analyse des écarts entre les systèmes sources et les données produites
La correction des problèmes de données et de mise en forme
La maintenance de rapports opérationnels à fort enjeu
Capacité à interpréter des spécifications métiers détaillées et à les traduire en solutions de reporting précises
Excellentes compétences en « data storytelling » et solide compréhension des bonnes pratiques en reporting
Expérience dans l’analyse et l’amélioration de rapports Sisense existants
À l’aise pour collaborer étroitement avec les Product Owners, le QAT et les équipes en contact avec les clients, notamment pour valider le comportement des tableaux de bord, des filtres et des résultats
Grande attention aux détails et souci de la qualité
Excellentes capacités d’analyse et de résolution de problèmes, avec aptitude à identifier des enjeux à travers plusieurs couches de données


Ce Que Vous Obtiendrez En Retour

Nous voulons que vous puissiez donner le meilleur de vous-même. Nous mettons l'accent sur les moyens de soutenir notre équipe pour qu'elle donne le meilleur d'elle-même. Voici quelques-uns des avantages que nous offrons :

Santé et bien-être personnel / Avantages

>🧠 Soutien intégré à la santé mentale et au bien-être

>🌴 Vacances - à partir de 3 semaines

>💟 Journées du bien-être et journée annuelle de dons - une occasion supplémentaire de se rendre service ou de rendre service à la communauté

>🩺 Couverture médicale et dentaire complète

>😴 Fin de l'année, fermeture de l'entreprise pour vous permettre de vous détendre et de vous ressourcer

Planification future

>🏫 Licence d'apprentissage LinkedIn pour la mise à niveau et le développement

Vous êtes intéressé(e) mais ne pensez pas répondre à toutes les exigences ?

Notre équipe de recrutement évalue et examine toutes les candidatures en fonction du poste et des besoins de l'entreprise. Nous croyons que les gens ont des compétences transférables et non techniques et nous voulons que vous sachiez que nous prenons en considération les cas où une personne ne répond pas à tous les critères, mais possède néanmoins les aptitudes et les capacités nécessaires. Notre priorité est de veiller à ce que les candidats soient en mesure de réussir. Nous prendrons une décision finale en déterminant si nous pouvons offrir le soutien nécessaire pour améliorer les compétences ou fournir le soutien au développement dont vous avez besoin pour tirer le meilleur parti de cette opportunité avec nous !

Apportez votre entièreté au travail.

Veriforce est fière d'être un employeur qui respecte l'égalité des chances. Nous nous engageons à ce qu'aucun candidat ne soit discriminé en raison de son identité et de son expression sexuelles, de sa race, de son handicap, de son ethnicité, de son orientation sexuelle, de son âge, de sa couleur, de sa région, de sa croyance, de son origine nationale ou de son sexe. Nous sommes déterminés à développer une équipe diversifiée tout en continuant à créer un environnement inclusif où chacun se sent en sécurité et a la possibilité d'être lui-même.

Ce à quoi vous pouvez vous attendre si vous postulez :


Une réponse à votre candidature dans les 15 jours ouvrables
Un processus d'entretien comprenant
Un appel initial de découverte avec le recruteur
Un premier entretien via Microsoft Teams
Un entretien supplémentaire (probablement en face à face) avec les parties prenantes avec lesquelles vous travaillerez en étroite collaboration dans le cadre de votre fonction.

Nous tenons à ce que notre processus de recrutement vous permette de donner le meilleur de vous-même. Si vous souhaitez que nous procédions à des ajustements, n'hésitez pas à nous en faire part.
Show more

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Owned by the Ontario Secondary School Teachers’ Federation (OSSTF), Educators Financial Group has been offering financial planning and a wide range of investing and lending products and services exclusively to education members and their families since 1975. It is this strong history that has enabled us to become the education community’s financial specialist – supporting our clients on their financial journey and empowering them to reach their goals.




What We Offer

We’re proud to be recognized as one of the 2024 Best Workplaces™ in the Financial Services and Insurance sector.

Candidates can expect to work in a highly collaborative and supportive work environment with focus on professional growth and development opportunities.

Compensation and benefits packages include:

Annual salary (hiring range): $70,000 - $90,000 (subject to the candidate's relevant experience, qualifications and skills), plus annual Short-Term Incentive Plan (STIP) bonus.
Health Benefits Plan, including Extended Health, Dental & Vision coverage, Basic Life Insurance, Short-Term and Long-Term Disability Benefits and Employee Assistance Programs. Premiums are paid by the company, with the exception of LTD premium.
Defined benefit pension plan, with the company’s contribution ranging from 6% to 8% to provide employees with a predictable lifetime retirement income, survivor benefits, early retirement options.
Hybrid work environment for those who live within 50km of our Head Office. Remote arrangements can be made for those living farther away, with some exceptions.
3 weeks of paid vacation and other leave entitlements.
2 paid Employee Choice days to give employees the flexibility to observe and celebrate days that are meaningful to their cultural, religious, or personal values.
Learning and development support to aid professional growth.
Experienced leaders to coach and empower employees and supportive colleagues to help when needed.
Employee-led committees to boost engagement, initiative and idea sharing.
A Community Care program to make a difference in the lives of people in need.
Team building activities and optional social events.




About the Role

We are seeking a proactive Data Analyst who will lead the development of business intelligence capabilities across the organization with a primary focus on Power BI dashboards, data modeling, and actionable insights.

Reporting to the Vice-President, Business Technology, this role is accountable for delivering high-quality, timely, and business-relevant reporting, and will be instrumental in shaping and enhancing the organization’s data and analytics capabilities.

This posting is to fill an existing vacancy.




Key Responsibilities

In this role, you will be responsible for:




Data Analysis, Business Insights & Sales Reporting

Collaborating with internal departments including Sales, Marketing, Finance, Operations, and Compliance to translate business insight requirements into technical solutions.
Partnering with different teams regarding reporting procedures and/or discrepancies to establish controls and ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across systems and reports.
Designing, developing, and maintaining Power BI dashboards and data models for executive, operational and sales reporting, as well as analyzing data from different systems to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for improvement.
Automating recurring reporting processes to improve efficiency and reduce manual effort.
Managing enterprise-wide sales reporting in the system.




Data Management & Optimization

Maintaining and managing data management strategies to ensure data accuracy and integrity.
Supporting data governance and quality initiatives across the organization.




Project Management

Collaborating with development partners and internal departments to implement solutions and ensure timely completion of projects.
Communicating progress and status updates to internal departments (as needed) and management.




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree or College Diploma in Accounting, Computer Science, Finance, Business or Economics or relevant experience.
At least 3 years of experience in a Data Analyst and/or Business Analyst role within the financial services industry.

Owned by the Ontario Secondary School Teachers’ Federation (OSSTF), Educators Financial Group has been offering financial planning and a wide range of investing and lending products and services exclusively to education members and their families since 1975. What We Offer

We’re proud to be recognized as one of the 2024 Best Workplaces™ in the Financial Services and Insurance sector.

Candidates can expect to work in a highly collaborative and supportive work environment with focus on professional growth and development opportunities.

Title: DATA ANALYST
Description:
We Are Banking At Another Level.

Choosing BDC as your employer means working in a healthy, inclusive, and skilled workplace that puts forward the best conditions to bring together unique teams where employees are empowered to act. It also means being at the centre of ambitious economic and financial projects to see further and to do things differently, to fuel the success of Canadian entrepreneurs.

Choosing BDC As Your Employer Also Means


Flexible and competitive benefits, including an Employee Savings and Investment Plan where BDC matches part of your voluntary contributions, a Defined Benefit Pension Plan, a $750 wellness and health care spending account, to name a few
In addition to paid vacation each year, five personal days, sick days as necessary, and our offices are closed from December 25 to January 1
A hybrid work model that truly balances work and personal life
Opportunities for learning, training and development, and much more...


Explore the BDC Way in our Culture Book

Position Overview

The Data Analyst is a vital member of the Data Platforms and Tools Team, playing a key role in ensuring the reliability and efficiency of BDC’s Enterprise Data Platforms.

You will collaborate with data scientists, data engineers, data governance team, and business data analysts to ensure timely access to high-quality data, enabling advanced analytics and the delivery of actionable business insights.

Playing a key role in the Data & AI value chain, your work will focus on data lineage, data cataloguing, and ensuring quality of data flowing through pipelines from streaming and batch data sources into the Enterprise Data Platforms.

You will also contribute to enterprise-wide best practices, processes, frameworks and libraries that data platform team creates and maintains to create value from data.

CHALLENGES TO BE MET


Analyze and document source data to understand structure, lineage, and business relevance.
Act as a bridge between data engineers and business or data analysts/data scientists by translating technical data structures into business-friendly formats and preparing mapping documentation.
Identify data anomalies, inconsistencies, and gaps; collaborate with data engineers to resolve issues.
Develop and maintain data quality dashboards and reports.
Support root cause analysis and resolution of data-related incidents.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of monitoring and alerting mechanisms.
Provide ad hoc data extracts and support for internal users when needed.
Engage in the estimation of user stories based on defined Acceptance Criteria, ensuring alignment with data engineering workflows.
Participate in the Data Platforms and Tools user community by sharing insights, providing guidance, and promoting best practices.
Collaborate with Data Stewards and Data Governance team to ensure compliance with data policies and standards.


What We Are Looking For


A bachelor’s degree in IT, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related fields.
4+ years of industry experience as a Data Analyst.
4+ experience with SQL for data querying, validation, and transformation.
2+ years of experience using Microsoft Power BI for dashboard development and data visualization.
Proficiency with the Databricks platform.
Experience with Google BigQuery is a plus.
Proficiency with version control systems, primarily Azure DevOps.
Basic knowledge of scripting (e.g., Python) is a plus.
Experience with relational database management systems.
Experience with data modeling, data warehousing, and data profiling tools and techniques.
Familiarity with Agile methodologies and processes.
Strong analytical mindset with attention to detail and data accuracy.
Ability to interpret complex data structures and communicate findings clearly.
Strong interpersonal, communication, and documentation skills.
Bilingual (French & English).


Proudly one of Canada’s Top 100 Employers and one of Canada’s Best Diversity Employers, we are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable, inclusive and accessible environment where all employees can thrive and feel empowered to bring their whole selves to work. If you require an accommodation to complete your application, please do not hesitate to contact us at accessibility@bdc.ca.

While we appreciate all applications, we advise that only the candidates selected to participate in the recruitment process will be contacted.

Title: Power Platform Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking someone to join our team as a Power Platform Data Analyst. The candidate will be working in a team providing reporting through Power BI Dashboards leveraging Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, etc.). The qualified candidate will bring their expertise in Power Platforms, and PowerBI data modeling, analytics and visualizations to define and create key benefits and performance measuring dashboards and reports.

Job Responsibilities

• Design, develop, and maintain interactive Power BI models, reports and dashboards to support business decision-making and analytics.

• Collaborate with internal fleet stakeholders and business users across locations to understand/document business requirements & deliver solutions for dashboard/reports

• Create and document dashboards, data sources, definitions, and logic.

• Support end users with training, troubleshooting, and ongoing enhancements to reporting solutions.

Required Skills

1. Minimum 3 years of experience with data analysis and BI technologies, minimum 5 years of overall working experience in the related

2. Expertise in Microsoft Power BI (Desktop and Service), DAX and Power Query (M)

3. Strong proficiency in Power Platform (Apps, Automate, BI) and Dataverse

4. Proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, and analysis.

5. Strong communication skills with the ability to present insights clearly

6. Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills

Desired Skills:

• Prior experience in finance, investment banking, or related domains is a plus.

• An interest in emerging technologies, particularly use and application of GenAI tooling and capabilities.

• (Nice to have) Familiarity with Snowflake (particularly, Snowflake Cortex)

Title: Data Analyst, Credit Risk Management Department
Company: Industrial and Commercial Bank of China (Canada)
Description:
Department / Branch: Credit Risk Management Department

Reports To: Head and Senior Manager of Credit Risk Management Department

Employment Type: Full-Time Contract/ Permanent

Location: Richmond Hill Office







Responsibilities

1. Model Development and Optimization

Design, develop, and optimize credit rating models to solve business problems.
Experiment with algorithms, architectures, and frameworks to improve model performance.
Conduct regular validation and tuning of models for accuracy and efficiency.

2. Data Analysis

Work with large datasets, ensuring quality, consistency, and relevance for training models.
Identify patterns and insights to refine model inputs and features.

3. Model Evaluation and Testing

Implement evaluation metrics and validation techniques to ensure model reliability.
Perform testing to compare different models and configurations.

4. Monitoring and Reporting

Deploy models to production environments and monitor their real-time performance.
Troubleshoot and resolve issues to maintain model robustness over time.
Conduct model performance monitoring and reporting.

5. Collaboration and Communication

Collaborate with data stakeholders to align model objectives with business goals.
Communicate findings, methodologies, and results to technical and non-technical audiences .or regulator.

6. Other works assigned by Head and Senior Manager of Credit Risk Management Department.




Qualifications:

Bachelor degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Machine Learning, Financial Engineering or related fields.
At least 3 years of experience in financial modelling, preferably within banking or financial services sector.
Strong knowledge in model development, validation, evaluation and optimization.
Proficient in Python, R or similar programming languages.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and teamwork abilities.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English with work proficiency.
Fluency in Chinese is an asset.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Senior Reporting Data Analyst

Location: Toronto ,Canada;




About the Role

Reporting, DWH, P&C Insurance (SME) Workers comp preferred

ROLE SUMMARY

The Senior Reporting Data Analyst brings 15+ years of experience in enterprise data warehousing and reporting, with deep expertise in the Property & Casualty (P&C) insurance domain, especially Workers’ Compensation. Acts as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) on enterprise data warehouse (EDW) content and insurance source systems, ensuring accurate data interpretation, strong data lineage, and reliable, business-aligned reporting outputs.




ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES

• Act as a Data & Domain SME across P&C insurance subject areas including policy, claims, billing, and financials.

• Demonstrate deep expertise in Commercial Lines: Workers’ Compensation, General Liability, Commercial Auto, Property, Umbrella/Excess.

• Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather, analyze, and validate reporting and data extract requirements.

• Translate complex insurance business processes into data models, KPIs, metrics, and dimensional structures.

• Leverage strong knowledge of Guidewire (PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, BillingCenter), Genius, and SAP for accurate data interpretation and lineage.

• Explain and map policy lifecycle events (submission, quote, bind, issue, endorsements, renewal, cancellation, reinstatement) into warehouse models.

• Analyze claims lifecycle data including reserves, payments, recoveries, and claim financials.

• Perform data lineage, source-to-target mapping, and transformation analysis across ETL and EDW layers.

• Develop and validate complex SQL queries, views, and reporting datasets.

• Ensure data reconciliation and consistency between transactional systems and enterprise warehouse.

• Review and certify reports for accuracy, completeness, and regulatory compliance.

• Investigate and resolve data discrepancies across source systems, ETL pipelines, and reporting layers.

• Document data definitions, mappings, lineage, and business rules.

• Support UAT, defect resolution, and reporting enhancements.

• Educate stakeholders on data interpretation, reporting usage, and governance standards.




TECHNICAL QUALIFICATIONS

• 15+ years of experience as a hands-on Reporting/Data Analyst in enterprise environments.

• 10+ years working with Enterprise Data Warehousing (EDW/DWH) solutions.

• 7+ years of experience in Property & Casualty Insurance (Commercial Lines).

• Strong domain expertise in Workers’ Compensation and claims analytics.

• 5+ years of experience with BI/reporting tools: MicroStrategy, Power BI.

• Hands-on experience with Guidewire (PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, BillingCenter), Genius, SAP.

• Advanced SQL skills with extensive experience querying large-scale data warehouses.

• Strong understanding of:

• Data lineage and traceability

• Source-to-target mappings

• Dimensional modeling (facts, dimensions, aggregates)

• Enterprise reporting architecture

• Proven ability to reconcile data between source systems and warehouse environments.




GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS

• Strong initiative with ability to self-manage in complex, ambiguous environments.

• Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

• Proven ability to work with senior business leaders and cross-functional teams.

• Ability to clearly explain complex data concepts to technical and non-technical audiences.

• Recognized as a trusted SME in Insurance Data & Data Warehousing.




EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS

• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, MIS, or related field (or equivalent experience).

Job Title: Senior Reporting Data Analyst

Location: Toronto ,Canada;


About the Role

Reporting, DWH, P&C Insurance (SME) Workers comp preferred

ROLE SUMMARY

The Senior Reporting Data Analyst brings 15+ years of experience in enterprise data warehousing and reporting, with deep expertise in the Property & Casualty (P&C) insurance domain, especially Workers’ Compensation. Acts as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) on enterprise data warehouse (EDW) content and insurance source systems, ensuring accurate data interpretation, strong data lineage, and reliable, business-aligned reporting outputs.


ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES

• Act as a Data & Domain SME across P&C insurance subject areas including policy, claims, billing, and financials.

• Demonstrate deep expertise in Commercial Lines: Workers’ Compensation, General Liability, Commercial Auto, Property, Umbrella/Excess.

• Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather, analyze, and validate reporting and data extract requirements.

• Translate complex insurance business processes into data models, KPIs, metrics, and dimensional structures.

• Leverage strong knowledge of Guidewire (PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, BillingCenter), Genius, and SAP for accurate data interpretation and lineage.

• Explain and map policy lifecycle events (submission, quote, bind, issue, endorsements, renewal, cancellation, reinstatement) into warehouse models.

• Analyze claims lifecycle data including reserves, payments, recoveries, and claim financials.

• Perform data lineage, source-to-target mapping, and transformation analysis across ETL and EDW layers.

• Develop and validate complex SQL queries, views, and reporting datasets.

• Ensure data reconciliation and consistency between transactional systems and enterprise warehouse.

• Review and certify reports for accuracy, completeness, and regulatory compliance.

• Investigate and resolve data discrepancies across source systems, ETL pipelines, and reporting layers.

• Document data definitions, mappings, lineage, and business rules.

• Support UAT, defect resolution, and reporting enhancements.

• Educate stakeholders on data interpretation, reporting usage, and governance standards.


TECHNICAL QUALIFICATIONS

• 15+ years of experience as a hands-on Reporting/Data Analyst in enterprise environments.

• 10+ years working with Enterprise Data Warehousing (EDW/DWH) solutions.

• 7+ years of experience in Property & Casualty Insurance (Commercial Lines).

• Strong domain expertise in Workers’ Compensation and claims analytics.

• 5+ years of experience with BI/reporting tools: MicroStrategy, Power BI.

• Hands-on experience with Guidewire (PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, BillingCenter), Genius, SAP.

• Advanced SQL skills with extensive experience querying large-scale data warehouses.

• Strong understanding of:

• Data lineage and traceability

• Source-to-target mappings

• Dimensional modeling (facts, dimensions, aggregates)

• Enterprise reporting architecture

• Proven ability to reconcile data between source systems and warehouse environments.


GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS

• Strong initiative with ability to self-manage in complex, ambiguous environments.

• Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

• Proven ability to work with senior business leaders and cross-functional teams.

• Ability to clearly explain complex data concepts to technical and non-technical audiences.

• Recognized as a trusted SME in Insurance Data & Data Warehousing.


EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS

• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, MIS, or related field (or equivalent experience).

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At CI, we see a great place to work as one that is a safe place for everyone to have a voice, where people are empowered to take ownership over meaningful work, where there is an opportunity to grow through stretching themselves, where they can work on innovative products and projects, and where employees are supported and engaged in doing so.

We are currently seeking a Data Analyst to join the Data Solutions team in the Wealth Technology group at CI Wealth. The Data Solutions team focuses on developing data-driven solutions, driving innovation, and uncovering key insights to support strategic initiatives. The successful candidate will play a key role in leveraging data to deliver solutions that enhance decision-making, streamline business processes, and support strategic objectives.

The ideal candidate will have a strong background in both data analysis and business processes.

Responsibilities


Analyze and interpret large datasets to uncover key insights that drive business objectives.
Collaborate with stakeholders across teams to identify data needs and deliver actionable solutions.
Develop and maintain data processes that enhance efficiency and support data-driven decision-making.
Document and maintain data mapping specifications and business rules to support data governance efforts.
Assist in building reports, dashboards, and analytics tools to monitor key metrics and trends.
Identify inefficiencies in existing processes and recommend data-driven improvements.
Respond to stakeholder inquiries and data requests in a timely and effective manner.
Partner with Data Engineers to support the successful implementation of solutions.


Experience

QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS:


2+ years of work experience in quantitative analysis.


Education/Training


Post-secondary degree in a quantitative discipline.


Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities


Strong problem-solving skills and attention to details.
Solid understanding of data and data modeling.
In-depth knowledge and experience with the following tools:
Visualization e.g., Sigma, AWS Quicksight
Modelling e.g., MS Excel
Database system e.g., PostgreSQL, Snowflake
Cloud Technology e.g., AWS, Microsoft Azure
Strong knowledge and understanding of SQL
Working knowledge and understanding of Python.
A strong analytical mindset and sophisticated written and verbal communication skills.
Experience in the Financial Services Industry is an asset.
Ability to work within an organization based upon continuous improvement.


This opportunity is for an existing vacancy with the company. The anticipated base salary range for this position is $55,000 - $95,000. Exact salary depends on several factors such as experience, skills, education, and budget. Salary range may vary based on geographic location. In addition to base salary, this position is eligible for participation in a bonus program. In addition, The Company offers a variety of benefits to eligible employees, including health insurance coverage, wellness programs, life and disability insurance, retirement savings plans, paid leave programs, education-related programs, paid holidays and vacation time, and many others. Many of these benefits are subsidized or fully paid for by the company.

This role follows our in‑office work model and requires employees to be onsite four days per week to support collaboration and business needs.

CI Financial is an independent company offering global wealth management and asset management advisory services through diverse financial services firms. Since 1965, we have consistently anticipated and responded to the changing needs of investors. We are driven by a commitment to provide individuals and institutions with the highest-quality investments and advice. Our commitment to the highest levels of performance means that whatever their position, CI employees must be comfortable in a fast-paced environment that will stretch them to tap into their highest potential. Employees with a healthy dose of ambition, a desire to commit to a curious mindset for continuous learning, and a willingness to go the extra mile thrive at CI.

A Supportive Environment for Success

We offer an in-office environment, competitive benefits, and a supportive workplace to help our employees thrive both personally and professionally.

What We Offer


Modern HQ location within walking distance from Union Station
Training Reimbursement
Paid Professional Designations
Employee Savings Plan (ESP)
Corporate Discount Program
Enhanced group benefits
Parental Leave Top–up program
Paid time off for Volunteering


We are focused on building a diverse and inclusive workforce. If you are excited about this role and are not confident you meet all the qualification requirements, we encourage you to apply to investigate the opportunity further.

Please submit your resume in confidence by clicking “Apply”. Only qualified candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. CI Financial Corp. and all of our affiliates (“CI”) are committed to fair and accessible employment practices and provide reasonable accommodations for persons with disabilities. If you require accommodations in order to apply for any job opportunities, require this posting in an additional format, or require accommodation at any stage of the recruitment process please contact us at accessible.recruitment@ci.com, or call 416-364-1145 ext. 4747.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
StackAdapt is the leading technology company that empowers marketers to reach, engage, and convert audiences with precision. With 465 billion automated optimizations per second, the AI-powered StackAdapt Marketing Platform seamlessly connects brand and performance marketing to drive measurable results across the entire customer journey. The most forward-thinking marketers choose StackAdapt to orchestrate high-impact campaigns across programmatic advertising and marketing channels.

Who are we:

StackAdapt is a self-serve advertising platform that specializes in multi-channel solutions including native, display, video, connected TV, audio, in-game, and digital out-of-home ads. We empower hundreds of digitally-focused companies to deliver outcomes and exceptional campaign performance everyday. StackAdapt was founded with a vision to be more than an advertising platform, it’s a hub of innovation, imagination and creativity.

The Business Data Analyst role is a unique combination of technical execution and internal customer relationship management. You will serve as a "context-shifter"—equally comfortable navigating complex SQL databases as you are advocating for the needs of our business partners. We are looking for a versatile professional who doesn't just pull data, but seeks to understand the "why" behind every metric to ensure our business objectives are met with precision.

StackAdapt is a remote first company. We are open to hire residents of Canada.


What You'll Be Doing:
Stakeholder Partnership: Act as a customer-facing advocate for the business. You will lead discovery sessions to understand requirements, define key metrics, and draft Business Requirement Documents (BRDs).
Partner with RevOps, Marketing Ops, and CRM administrators to maintain data integrity across systems (e.g., Salesforce), including field-level metric population, account structure governance, and cross-system reconciliation.
CRM & Cross-functional Data Stewardship — Partner with RevOps, Marketing Ops, and CRM administrators to maintain data integrity across systems (e.g., Salesforce), including field-level metric population, account structure governance, and cross-system reconciliation.
Technical Execution: Execute project work and "hands-on-keyboard" development. This includes writing complex SQL queries, navigating disparate databases, and investigating data sets to find "the truth."
Requirement Translation: Bridge the gap between technical and non-technical teams by translating high-level business goals into actionable technical specifications.
Data Discovery & Troubleshooting: Serve as the first line of defense for troubleshooting data issues and fulfilling ad-hoc data requests to support immediate business needs.
Pipeline Foundation: In your first 3–6 months, you will focus heavily on building and optimizing data pipes to ensure a robust foundation for future analysis.
What You'll Bring to the Table
Relational Database Proficiency — Deep comfort navigating unfamiliar RDBMS environments, including complex joins, schema exploration, data lineage investigation, and query optimization. Experience with cloud data warehouses such as Snowflake is a plus.
Exceptional Communication: Strong soft skills with a track record of translating technical concepts for executive-level stakeholders.
Versatility: The ability to "live in multiple contexts" and pivot quickly from deep technical work to strategic business meetings.
Technical Curiosity: You are proactive about personal projects and stay up-to-date with industry trends, including a high proficiency in using LLMs to accelerate workflows.
ETL/ELT Knowledge: Hands-on experience building and maintaining data pipelines.
LLM/AI Literacy — Beyond using LLMs to accelerate personal workflows, an appreciation for how data model design (e.g., star schemas, clear naming conventions) improves AI/ML system integration and reliability.
Python (Plus): Experience with Python for automation or advanced analysis is a nice-to-have.
 

The compensation range listed for this role reflects the expected base salary for candidates located in Ontario, Canada. It is informed by market data and the approved budget for this position. StackAdapt maintains different compensation ranges for roles across other countries and regions, and final offers will be aligned to the candidate’s current location.This range represents base salary only. Depending on the role, candidates may also be eligible for additional compensation such as annual bonuses, commissions, equity awards, and a comprehensive benefits package.Factors Influencing Final Compensation:The final compensation offer will be determined by a variety of factors, which may include, but are not limited to: the candidate's specific experience, technical skills, knowledge, abilities, and relevant education, licensure, and certifications.Other business factors, such as organizational needs and budget alignment, may also be considered in the final offer.

Ontario Salary Band: $84,000 CAD - $99,000 CAD

StackAdapter's Enjoy:
Highly competitive salary
Retirement/ 401K/ Pension Savings globally
Competitive Paid time off packages including birthday's off!
Access to a comprehensive mental health care program
Health benefits from day one of employment
Work from home reimbursements
Optional global WeWork membership for those who want a change from their home office and hubs in London and Toronto
Robust training and onboarding program
Coverage and support of personal development initiatives (conferences, courses, books etc)
Access to StackAdapt programmatic courses and certifications to support continuous learning
An awesome parental leave program
A friendly, welcoming, and supportive culture
Our social and team events!

Please note: Benefits and perks may vary depending on your country of employment and the nature of your engagement. In locations where StackAdapt does not have a legal entity, employment and benefits are administered in accordance with local regulations and partner policies.

StackAdapt is a diverse and inclusive team of collaborative, hardworking individuals trying to make a dent in the universe. No matter who you are, where you are from, who you love, follow in faith, disability (or superpower) status, ethnicity, or the gender you identify with (if you’re comfortable, let us know your pronouns), you are welcome at StackAdapt. If you have any requests or requirements to support you throughout any part of the interview process, please let our Talent team know. We use artificial intelligence (AI) to streamline the resume reviews of candidates and assess their fit based on the criteria outlined in the job posting. We do not use AI to make any final hiring or interview decisions. About StackAdapt We've been recognized for our diverse and supportive workplace, high performing campaigns, award-winning customer service, and innovation. We've been awarded: G2 Top Software for 2026
2026 Best Workplaces™ for Young Talent and in Canada by Great Place to Work®
#1 DSP on G2 and leader in a number of categories including Cross-Channel Advertising To learn more about our privacy practices, please see our Privacy Policy.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At CI, we see a great place to work as one that is a safe place for everyone to have a voice, where people are empowered to take ownership over meaningful work, where there is an opportunity to grow through stretching themselves, where they can work on innovative products and projects, and where employees are supported and engaged in doing so.

We are seeking experienced Data Analysts to support a large-scale data migration and integration initiative. You will be responsible for investigating existing data structures, analyzing business logic hidden in queries and reports, mapping source data to target systems, and collaborating with business stakeholders to ensure accuracy and completeness.

This role is highly investigative and requires a blend of technical analysis and business communication skills. You should be comfortable navigating undocumented environments, reverse-engineering logic, and documenting findings clearly for both technical and business audiences.

Key Responsibilities


Source System Analysis
Investigate source systems and databases to understand data structures, relationships, and logic.
Analyze existing SQL queries, reports, and stored procedures to reverse engineer business rules.
Data Mapping & Transformation
Map source fields to target model requirements.
Identify gaps, data quality issues, and transformation needs.
Work closely with data engineers to validate ETL/transformation rules.
Stakeholder Collaboration
Partner with business SMEs and stakeholders to clarify definitions, logic, and requirements.
Translate technical findings into clear documentation for non-technical stakeholders.
Documentation & Knowledge Transfer
Produce detailed data dictionaries, mapping documents, and gap analysis reports.
Capture undocumented business logic and processes uncovered during investigation.
Validation & Testing Support
Support data validation and reconciliation efforts during development and UAT.
Assist in resolving discrepancies between source and target systems.

Required Skills & Experience


Strong SQL skills, with ability to read, analyze, and write complex queries.
Proven experience in data migration, data warehousing, or system integration projects.
Background in analyzing large datasets.
Strong data profiling, mapping, and reconciliation skills.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities in ambiguous environments.
Strong communication skills to work with both technical teams and business stakeholders.
Experience creating data dictionaries, mapping documents, and gap analysis reports.
Experience with dashboard tools such as Sigma or equivalent
Experience with code repository systems – Bitbucket or Git


Nice to Have


Experience with Snowflake or other modern cloud data platforms.
Familiarity with ETL/ELT processes and tools.
Exposure to financial services or other regulated industries
