Title: Data Engineer
Description:
We're working with a a fast-growing private equity firm focused on accelerating liquidity on private equity portfolios through structured solutions.

The Associate, Data Engineer will be part of a team that is focused on building and maintaining

the firm's cloud data platform — designing scalable data pipelines, curating high-quality data

models, and exploring AI-driven approaches to accelerate and automate data workflows across

the organization.




They will work closely with other team members across Portfolio Analytics, Finance,

and Operations to develop data solutions that minimize risk and allow the firm to move fast.

Reporting to the Manager of Data Engineering, the ideal candidate is energetic, dynamic, and a

team-oriented individual with a strong ability to work independently and as part of a team.




About the role

Designing, building, and maintaining robust ELT/ETL pipelines and data models in BigQuery to support analytics, reporting, and AI/ML initiatives
Developing and maintaining version-controlled SQL views, scheduled queries, and stored procedures following infrastructure-as-code practices (Terraform, GitHub Actions)
Leveraging AI tools and frameworks (e.g., LLMs, AI coding assistants, MCP servers) to accelerate development, automate repetitive tasks, and improve data quality
Collaborating with cross-functional teams (Portfolio Analytics, Finance, Operations) to understand data requirements and translate them into scalable data solutions
Contributing to the continuous improvement of CI/CD workflows, deployment automation, and developer tooling for the data platform
Exploring opportunities to integrate generative AI capabilities into data workflows — such as automated documentation, anomaly detection, and intelligent data transformations

About you

You are passionate about data and AI, and love turning raw information into reliable, scalable data products
You have a strong foundation in data engineering fundamentals — pipelines, warehousing, and SQL — and are eager to apply AI/ML techniques to enhance data workflows
You are curious about emerging technologies like LLMs, prompt engineering, and AI-assisted development, and look for practical ways to incorporate them into everyday work
You understand the value of clean, well-modeled data as the foundation for trustworthy analytics and AI applications
You are a self-starter who takes ownership of problems end-to-end, but thrive in a collaborative team environment where ideas are shared openly
You are comfortable working in cloud-native environments and understand infrastructure-as-code principles
You want to be part of a fast-moving, entrepreneurial organization where your contributions have a direct impact on business outcomes

Title: Data & Automation Analyst
Description:
Data & Automation Analyst (on-site, Calgary)




POSITION SUMMARY:

The Data & Automation Analyst plays a key role in delivering data-driven solutions by combining automation, data engineering, analytics, and emerging AI capabilities. This position focuses on translating business needs into scalable data and automation solutions while supporting the growth of a high-performing analytics function.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:




1. Automation & Digital Solutions

• Design, develop, and implement automated workflows to improve operational efficiency

• Leverage modern automation tools and low-code platforms to streamline business processes

• Support AI and advanced analytics initiatives, including use case development and implementation

• Identify and deliver opportunities for process optimization and digital transformation




2. Data Engineering & Management

• Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from multiple structured and unstructured sources

• Ensure data quality, integrity, and consistency across systems

• Develop and maintain scalable data models to support analytics and reporting

• Collaborate on data architecture and integration across enterprise platforms




3. Analytics & Business Intelligence

• Design and develop interactive dashboards and reporting solutions using modern BI tools

• Translate business requirements into actionable insights and analytical frameworks

• Deliver data-driven recommendations that support operational and strategic decision-making

• Connect analytics outputs to measurable business outcomes




4. Solution Design & Delivery

• Conduct requirements gathering and analysis with stakeholders

• Design end-to-end data, reporting, and automation solutions

• Translate complex and ambiguous business problems into structured, scalable data solutions




5. Team Support & Mentorship

• Provide guidance and mentorship to junior analysts

• Review deliverables to ensure quality and consistency

• Contribute to the development of analytics, automation, and AI capabilities within the team




6. Stakeholder Engagement

• Partner with cross-functional teams to deliver data and automation solutions

• Communicate technical concepts and insights effectively to non-technical audiences

• Act as a trusted advisor for data-driven initiatives




7. Training & Enablement

• Deliver knowledge sharing sessions and training on analytics tools and best practices

• Support adoption of data, automation, and reporting solutions across the organization




ROLE REQUIREMENTS (Technical Emphasis)

• Strong experience with data analytics, automation, and business intelligence solutions

• Hands-on expertise with Microsoft Power Platform, including Power BI and Power Automate

• Strong proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, and analysis

• Experience with data modeling, ETL processes, and data integration across multiple systems

• Familiarity with Microsoft 365 ecosystem and integration with data and automation tools

• Exposure to Azure data services or cloud-based data platforms is considered an asset

• Experience building interactive dashboards and reporting solutions

• Working knowledge of automation tools and low-code/no-code platforms

• Exposure to AI and machine learning concepts, including practical application in analytics workflows

• Strong understanding of data governance, quality, and reporting best practices

• Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions and analytical outputs

• Strong problem-solving, critical thinking, and analytical skills

• Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage both technical and business stakeholders

• Demonstrated ability to work in fast-paced, collaborative environments




REQUIRED EXPERIENCE / EDUCATION

• Post-secondary education in data analytics, computer science, information systems, or a related field, or equivalent experience

• 2+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or data engineering roles

• Experience mentoring or supporting junior team members is considered an asset




COMPENSATION

• Salary range: $75,000 – $85,000

Title: Information Systems Data Analyst
Description:
We exist to create positive change for people and the planet. Join us and make a difference too!

Join a Global Industry Leader!

For nearly 120 years, BSI has helped organizations around the world improve performance, manage risk, and build trust. We're looking for an analytical, data-driven professional to join our team as an Information Systems Analyst – Global Accounts.

In this highly visible role, you'll partner with business leaders across the Americas and globally to transform data into actionable insights, optimize reporting and systems, and support strategic decision-making for some of BSI's most important client accounts.

In Your Day-to-day, You Will


Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and analytics using Power BI, Power Query, Excel, SAP, Salesforce, and other business tools.
Transform complex data into meaningful insights that support operational and strategic decision-making.
Ensure data accuracy and integrity by identifying, investigating, and resolving reporting and data quality issues.
Serve as a subject matter expert for key business systems, supporting users, system enhancements, and continuous improvement initiatives.
Partner with stakeholders across Operations, Sales, Finance, and Global Accounts to support reporting needs and business objectives.
Lead training and enablement efforts to maximize adoption and effective use of reporting tools and systems.
Identify opportunities to improve processes, increase efficiency, and enhance business performance.



To Be Successful In This Role, You Will Have


Experience in data analytics, business intelligence, reporting, systems administration, or a related field.
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including Power Query, with experience creating dashboards and visualizations in Power BI.
Working knowledge of SQL, DAX, SAP, Salesforce, or similar enterprise systems and reporting tools.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a passion for turning data into actionable insights.
Exceptional attention to detail and a commitment to data accuracy and integrity.
The ability to manage multiple priorities and projects in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent communication, collaboration, and stakeholder management skills.
Experience supporting system implementations, process improvements, or change management initiatives is a plus.



What We Offer

BSI offers a competitive total reward package, an independent and varied job in an international environment, flexible working hours, ongoing training and development with the inclusion of; 20-days annual leave, bank holidays, medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, 401(K) with company contribution, short-term and long-term disability, maternal leave, paid parental leave, paid bereavement leave, learning and development opportunities, and a wide range of flexible benefits that you can tailor to suit your lifestyle.

This position offers a base annual salary of $86,400 - $108,000 (local currency); actual compensation is based on various factors, including but not limited to the candidate’s competencies, level of experience, education, location, divisional budget, and internal peer compensation comparisons.

Do you believe the world deserves excellence?

We are proud to be the business improvement company for other organizations to become more sustainable and resilient and finally to inspire trust in their products, systems, services, and the world we live in.

Headquartered in London, BSI is the world's first national standards organization with more than 100 years of experience. We are a global partner for 86,000 companies and organizations in over 193 countries, offering development, auditing, certification, and training services, including innovative software solutions and cyber security expertise for all industries: from aerospace and automotive to food, construction, energy, healthcare, IT and trade sectors. Incorporated by Royal Charter, we’re truly impartial, and home to the ultimate mark of trust, the Kitemark.

Through our unique combination of consulting, training, assurance and regulatory services we bring solid and broad knowledge to every company.

D&I Policy

BSI is committed to ensuring the diversity of our workforce reflects that of our clients and the communities in which we operate. Our goal is to create a sense of belonging for all employees by providing opportunities to develop, grow, and engage with our global organization all while having fun doing great work. BSI is a community where everyone can thrive.

If you require any reasonable accommodations to be made on account of a disability or impairment throughout out our recruiting process, please inform your Talent Acquisition Partner.

About Us

BSI is a business improvement and standards company and for over a century BSI has been recognized for having a positive impact on organizations and society, building trust and enhancing lives.

Today BSI partners with more than 77,500 clients in 195 countries and engages with a 15,000 strong global community of experts, industry and consumer groups, organizations and governments.

Utilizing its extensive expertise in key industry sectors - including automotive, aerospace, built environment, food and retail, and healthcare - BSI delivers on its purpose by helping its clients fulfil theirs.

Living by our core values of Client-Centricity, Agility, and Collaboration, BSI provides organizations with the confidence to grow by partnering with them to tackle society’s critical issues – from climate change to building trust in digital transformation and everything in between - to accelerate progress towards a better society and a sustainable world.

BSI is an Equal Opportunity Employer dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

Title: GTM Insights & Analytics Lead
Description:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a GTM Insights & Analytics Lead in Canada.

This role sits at the core of the go-to-market organization, building the analytical foundation that powers decision-making across Sales, Marketing, and Finance. You will design and scale the BI infrastructure that enables teams to independently access trusted, real-time insights on pipeline health, revenue performance, and campaign effectiveness. Acting as the horizontal analytics layer, you will connect data across functions to uncover patterns that inform forecasting, planning, and strategic investment decisions. Your work will directly influence pipeline reviews, board reporting, and revenue planning cycles. This is a high-impact role requiring both technical depth and strong business acumen, where your outputs become the single source of truth for GTM performance. You will collaborate closely with RevOps, Finance, and Analytics Engineering to improve data quality, modeling, and decision intelligence across the organization.

Accountabilities


Build and maintain scalable BI dashboards that provide real-time visibility into pipeline health, funnel performance, segmentation, and revenue outcomes.
Own the GTM data dictionary, ensuring consistent definitions, logic, and metrics across Sales, Marketing, and Finance.
Lead analytical modeling for forecasting, including conversion rates, pipeline coverage, stage weighting, and forecast variance analysis.
Support annual planning and revenue forecasting through quota modeling, capacity analysis, and segmentation insights.
Develop scenario models for pricing, GTM strategy, and revenue structure decisions in partnership with leadership teams.
Analyze campaign and channel performance to assess pipeline contribution and identify optimization opportunities.
Partner with Analytics Engineering to ensure accurate data modeling across Salesforce, Marketo, and the modern data stack.
Lead cross-functional ad hoc analyses including win/loss, cohort analysis, market sizing, and GTM performance deep dives.


Requirements


Proven experience in GTM analytics, revenue operations analytics, or business analytics within a B2B SaaS environment.
Strong proficiency in SQL and Python for data analysis, modeling, and maintaining scalable analytical frameworks.
Hands-on experience building dashboards in BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Omni, or similar platforms.
Deep familiarity with Salesforce data structures and core GTM objects (leads, accounts, opportunities, campaigns, etc.).
Experience supporting financial planning processes such as forecasting, quota setting, and capacity modeling.
Ability to synthesize data across Sales, Marketing, and Finance to identify cross-functional insights and opportunities.
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate complex analysis into clear, decision-ready narratives.
Experience with modern data stack tools (e.g., Snowflake, dbt, Fivetran, Marketo, Outreach) is a strong plus.


Benefits


Competitive salary with geographically adjusted compensation bands
Unlimited paid time off and flexible remote work across Canada and the U.S.
Comprehensive health and dental coverage
Home office setup budget to support remote work
Retirement savings plan with employer matching
Learning and development budget for continuous growth
Internet and mobile reimbursement support
Exposure to a fast-growing SaaS environment with high-impact analytics ownership
Opportunity to shape go-to-market strategy through data-driven insights
Inclusive, collaborative, and innovation-driven work culture.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About The Company

Our organization is a globally recognized leader in providing innovative solutions that empower businesses and institutions to make informed decisions through advanced data analytics and technology. Committed to excellence, integrity, and inclusivity, we foster a dynamic work environment that encourages continuous learning, collaboration, and growth. Our mission is to deliver impactful insights and cutting-edge tools that support transparency, justice, and societal progress worldwide. With a strong emphasis on leveraging emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and machine learning, we strive to stay at the forefront of the data-driven revolution, ensuring our clients and stakeholders achieve their strategic objectives efficiently and effectively.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly skilled and strategic Lead Data Engineer to join our team. In this advanced individual contributor role, you will spearhead the development of sophisticated, insightful, and visually compelling reporting solutions utilizing tools such as DBT, Snowflake, Claude.AI, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, Python, Excel, and PowerPoint. A key aspect of this position involves pioneering the integration of advanced AI solutions into our reporting processes to significantly enhance data‑driven decision‑making for our stakeholders. You will collaborate strategically with diverse stakeholders to ensure our reporting solutions effectively address complex business needs. Your leadership will be vital in designing, developing, and maintaining complex data pipelines and frameworks, as well as providing mentorship to team members and stakeholders, fostering a culture of innovation and excellence in data analytics.

Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Business Analytics, or related field
Master’s degree is a strong plus
8+ years of progressive experience in data engineering or similar roles
Proficiency in DBT, SQL, Alteryx, Snowflake, Power BI, Tableau, Python, Excel, and PowerPoint
Hands-on experience designing and operationalizing AI solutions and machine learning algorithms
Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication, presentation, and influencing abilities
Proven leadership in technical initiatives and mentoring capabilities
Advanced programming skills in Python or R
Expertise in SQL, Snowflake, and DBT for complex data manipulation and query optimization
Deep understanding of data warehousing, ETL processes, and data governance principles
Experience with emerging AI technologies such as Claude.AI, Copilot, and large language models (LLMs)
Strategic mindset with the ability to connect data insights to business outcomes


Responsibilities


Lead the architecture, development, design, and maintenance of complex, interactive data pipelines, frameworks, and dashboards using DBT, SQL, Python, Claude.AI, Power BI, Tableau, Excel, and PowerPoint
Partner strategically with senior stakeholders to define reporting needs and deliver actionable insights
Champion and integrate advanced AI and machine learning techniques to innovate reporting solutions and provide predictive and prescriptive insights
Conduct in-depth analysis of large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies informing strategic decisions
Establish and enforce best practices for data integrity, accuracy, and governance across all reporting solutions
Mentor team members and stakeholders on analytical methodologies, reporting tools, and emerging AI technologies
Drive continuous improvement initiatives for reporting processes and tools, researching industry trends and technological advancements
Communicate complex findings and strategic recommendations effectively to executive leadership through presentations and reports
Manage data projects from conception to deployment, ensuring alignment with business objectives and technical standards
Serve as a subject-matter expert and escalation point for data analysis and reporting issues


Benefits


Flexible hybrid work model with 2-3 days in the office per week
Work-from-anywhere policy for up to 8 weeks annually
Continuous learning and career development opportunities through Grow My Way programs
Industry competitive benefits including flexible vacation, mental health days, Headspace app access, retirement savings plans, tuition reimbursement, and wellbeing programs
Inclusive and award-winning company culture emphasizing diversity, flexibility, and work-life balance
Paid volunteer days and opportunities to participate in pro-bono consulting and ESG initiatives
Opportunity to make a meaningful impact by supporting justice, transparency, and truth globally


Equal Opportunity

We are an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, or any other protected characteristic. We make reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities to ensure an equitable hiring process and work environment.

Title: BI Analyst
HTML:
Who We Are

Richelieu Hardware is a leading North American distributor and a true change agent in a creative and dynamic industry. We are an importer, distributor, and manufacturer of specialty hardware and complementary products.

Our Customers

More than 120,000 customers across North America, including manufacturers of kitchen and bathroom cabinets, storage and closet systems, residential and office furniture, doors and windows, residential and commercial millwork companies, as well as hardware retailers, including major home improvement chains.

Our Team

Over 3,200 employees, nearly half of whom work in marketing, sales, and customer service roles. Close to 50% of Richelieu employees are shareholders.

The Opportunity

We are currently seeking a full-time, permanent Business Intelligence and Artificial Intelligence Analyst to join our Pricing team, based in our Ville Saint-Laurent offices .

Reporting to the Supervisor, Pricing Management and Data Analytics, you will play an active role in the development, evolution, and optimization of our analytics and artificial intelligence infrastructure to support Pricing operations and decision-making.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop, maintain, and enhance dashboards and analytical tools in Power BI, using SQL and Python;
  • Contribute to the continuous improvement of an existing, well-established, and mature analytics infrastructure;
  • Gradually participate in the integration of artificial intelligence solutions, through Copilot and the development of analytical agents aimed at automating, accelerating, and improving operational and analytical processes within the Pricing team;
  • Develop a deep understanding of the business, the Pricing department, products, and pricing levers to propose high-value analytics and AI-driven solutions;
  • Demonstrate sound judgment in task prioritization, problem-solving, and efficient request management.

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Business Analytics, Business Intelligence, Finance, or a related field;
  • 1–2 years of relevant experience in business intelligence, data analysis, or a similar analytical role;
  • Strong aptitude for numbers, analytical thinking, and problem-solving;
  • Proficiency in Power BI and the Microsoft Office suite;
  • Basic to intermediate knowledge of SQL;
  • Basic knowledge of Python;
  • Strong interest in artificial intelligence, automation, and continuous improvement of operational and analytical processes;
  • Bilingual in French and English (spoken and written);
  • Experience with ERP environments (AS/400) is an asset.

The Ideal Candidate

  • Proactive, curious, and eager to learn;
  • Comfortable working in a constantly evolving analytical and technological environment;
  • Able to translate complex analyses and technical concepts into clear business insights;
  • Autonomous, structured, and well-organized;
  • A strong team player who enjoys collaborating with multiple departments;
  • Aspires to grow into an expert role in business intelligence and artificial intelligence.

What We Offer

  • A Canadian flagship: As a company with proud roots in Quebec but present in Canada and the United States, we enjoy excellent recognition and a great reputation.
  • A mature and stable organization: Over 50 years of market experience, offering a secure and reliable work environment;
  • Continuous growth: Strong financial health and ongoing expansion, creating long-term career opportunities;
  • Competitive compensation aligned with market conditions;
  • Employee share ownership opportunities;
  • Work-life balance supported by a healthy and flexible work environment;
  • High-quality products that meet the expectations of our most demanding customers;
  • Career development opportunities through training and internal growth.

Are you interested in this position? We invite you to visit our corporate website at https://www.richelieu.com/emplois to submit your

We thank all candidates for their interest, however, we will only communicate with candidates selected for the pre-selection process.

Title: Senior Data Engineer
Description:
About Evovest




Do you want to contribute to the new technological era in portfolio management? Evovest is currently looking for a Senior Data Engineer to participate in the development of the company. Evovest is an equity portfolio manager that systematizes the investment process through a scientific approach. As a Senior Data Engineer, you will own the pipelines and infrastructure that ingest, transform, and serve this data to science team, and you will set the standards that keep it accurate and trustworthy.




What will you do as a Senior Data Engineer:




35% - Core Data Engineering: Design, build, and maintain robust, scalable data pipelines to ingest, process, and transform complex financial datasets.
25% - Data Quality & Integrity: Own data reliability across the platform. Implement validation, reconciliation, and data monitoring processes to ensure point-in-time accuracy for all research and production workloads.
15% - Data Modeling & Storage: Architect, structure, and manage the firm’s databases, data warehouses, and storage solutions to efficiently support quantitative workflows.
15% - Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner directly with research and portfolio management teams to translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions and seamlessly integrate new vendor data sources.
10% - Platform Optimization: Drive system performance, automation, and platform observability, while defining and contributing to overarching engineering best practices.




What we look for:




A degree in computer science, software engineering, data engineering, or a related quantitative field
Minimum of 5 years of experience in data engineering, with a track record of designing and operating production-grade data pipelines
Hands-on experience working with financial market data and an understanding of its specific challenges, including point-in-time accuracy and survivorship and look-ahead bias
Strong programming skills and solid software engineering practices 
Experience with modern data tooling, workflow orchestration, and cloud or on-premises data infrastructure
A rigorous, detail-oriented approach to data quality and reliability
Intellectually inquisitive




Bonus points:




Experience in asset management, a hedge fund, or another investment management environment
Familiarity with the Julia programming language
Exposure to machine learning workflows or supporting quantitative research pipelines
Experience with time-series databases or large-scale historical datasets




What can we offer you?




Offices in downtown Montreal in a dynamic environment
Flexible hybrid work from home or the office (physical presence required 3 days per week)




Working conditions:




Expected base salary of $130,000 to $160,000 depending on experience
A discretionary bonus determined based on overall company profitability and individual performance assessments
Collective insurance, pension contribution & public transit monthly pass




Evovest Diversity & Inclusion Statement can be found on our website




Feel free to communicate to info@evovest.com




À propos d’Evovest




Vous souhaitez contribuer à la nouvelle ère technologique de la gestion de portefeuille? Evovest est présentement à la recherche d’un(e) ingénieur(e) de données senior afin de participer au développement de l’entreprise. Evovest est un gestionnaire de portefeuille d’actions qui systématise le processus d’investissement au moyen d’une approche scientifique. À titre d’ingénieur(e) de données senior, vous serez responsable des pipelines et de l’infrastructure qui ingèrent, transforment et livrent ces données à l’équipe scientifique, et vous définirez les normes qui en assurent l’exactitude et la fiabilité.




Que ferez-vous à titre d’ingénieur(e) de données senior :




35 % - Ingénierie de données de base : Concevoir, bâtir et maintenir des pipelines de données robustes et évolutifs afin d’ingérer, de traiter et de transformer des ensembles de données financières complexes.
25 % - Qualité et intégrité des données : Assurer la fiabilité des données à l’échelle de la plateforme. Mettre en place des processus de validation, de réconciliation et de surveillance des données afin de garantir l’exactitude à un moment précis (point-in-time) pour toutes les charges de travail de recherche et de production.
15 % - Modélisation et stockage des données : Concevoir l’architecture, structurer et gérer les bases de données, les entrepôts de données et les solutions de stockage de la firme afin de soutenir efficacement les flux de travail quantitatifs.
15 % - Collaboration avec les parties prenantes : Travailler directement avec les équipes de recherche et de gestion de portefeuille afin de traduire les besoins d’affaires en solutions techniques évolutives et d’intégrer de façon transparente de nouvelles sources de données de fournisseurs.
10 % - Optimisation de la plateforme : Améliorer la performance des systèmes, l’automatisation et l’observabilité de la plateforme, tout en définissant les meilleures pratiques d’ingénierie et en y contribuant.




Ce que nous recherchons :




Un diplôme en informatique, en génie logiciel, en ingénierie de données ou dans un domaine quantitatif connexe
Un minimum de 5 ans d’expérience en ingénierie de données, avec un historique de conception et d’exploitation de pipelines de données de qualité production
Une expérience pratique du travail avec des données de marchés financiers et une compréhension de leurs défis particuliers, notamment l’exactitude à un moment précis (point-in-time) ainsi que les biais de survie et d’anticipation (look-ahead)
De solides compétences en programmation et de bonnes pratiques de génie logiciel 
De l’expérience avec les outils de données modernes, l’orchestration de flux de travail et l’infrastructure de données infonuagique ou sur site
Une approche rigoureuse et minutieuse de la qualité et de la fiabilité des données
Une curiosité intellectuelle




Atouts :




De l’expérience en gestion d’actifs, dans un fonds de couverture ou dans un autre environnement de gestion de placements
Une familiarité avec le langage de programmation Julia
Une exposition aux flux de travail d’apprentissage automatique ou au soutien de pipelines de recherche quantitative
De l’expérience avec les bases de données de séries chronologiques ou les ensembles de données historiques de grande envergure




Que pouvons-nous vous offrir?




Des bureaux au centre-ville de Montréal dans un environnement dynamique
Un mode de travail hybride flexible, à la maison ou au bureau (présence sur place requise 3 jours par semaine)




Conditions de travail :




Un salaire de base prévu de 130 000 $ à 160 000 $ selon l’expérience
Une prime discrétionnaire déterminée en fonction de la rentabilité globale de l’entreprise et des évaluations du rendement individuel
Une assurance collective, une cotisation de retraite et un laissez-passer mensuel de transport en commun




L’énoncé de diversité et d’inclusion d’Evovest est disponible sur notre site Web




N’hésitez pas à communiquer avec nous à info@evovest.com

Que pouvons-nous vous offrir?

  • Un mode de travail hybride flexible, à la maison ou au bureau (présence sur place requise 3 jours par semaine)


Conditions de travail :


  • Un salaire de base prévu de 130 000 $ à 160 000 $ selon l’expérience
  • Une prime discrétionnaire déterminée en fonction de la rentabilité globale de l’entreprise et des évaluations du rendement individuel
  • Une assurance collective, une cotisation de retraite et un laissez-passer mensuel de transport en commun


L’énoncé de diversité et d’inclusion d’Evovest est disponible sur notre site Web


N’hésitez pas à communiquer avec nous à info@evovest.com

Title: Sr Data & Analytics Strategist (Contractor)
Description:
Meet Domaine, the world's largest independent Shopify design and development partner, formed by the union of Half Helix and Tomorrow. We are a team of commerce experts dedicated to creating, building, and growing beloved brands. As pioneers and not just participants in our industry, we are inspired to write, break, and re-write the rules for a new generation of commerce.

Having launched over 300 sites on Shopify, we're proud to be a trusted long-term growth partner for some of the most prominent brands in ecommerce, spanning all industries and verticals.

We’re seeking a Senior Analytics & Measurement Strategist to support and scale Domaine’s analytics and measurement practice. This role partners closely with Strategy, CRO, CRM, Engineering, and Client teams to ensure analytics implementations are not only technically sound, but strategically designed to support business decision-making.

This is a hands-on, senior-level role for someone deeply fluent in ecommerce analytics, tagging, and data architecture—particularly across GA4 and Google Tag Manager—who can think holistically about what should be tracked, how data should be structured, and how insights should be activated across enterprise, omnichannel commerce organizations.

You’ll operate as a trusted analytics advisor to both internal teams and clients, balancing technical rigor with clear communication and strong client presence.

Remote-First Work Environment

This is a remote-first role, aligned to U.S. working hours. We value flexibility, autonomy, and accountability—focused on outcomes, not location.

Responsibilities

Analytics Strategy & Measurement Design


Own analytics strategy for ecommerce clients, with a focus on enterprise-level measurement frameworks across web, app, and supporting channels
Partner closely with Strategy, CRO, and CRM teams to define analytics approaches that support acquisition, retention, and customer lifecycle measurement
Design scalable tracking plans aligned to business objectives, KPIs, and customer journeys
Translate business and marketing questions into clear measurement requirements and data structures
Ensure analytics strategies support omnichannel Shopify-based brands with complex ecosystems and multiple data sources


Tagging, Tracking & Technical Implementation


Lead implementation and governance of analytics tracking using Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and Google Tag Manager (GTM)
Partner with Technology and Engineering teams to support analytics implementation, enhancements, and long-term scalability
Leverage Elevar to support ecommerce event tracking, auditing, and optimization across Shopify implementations
Audit, debug, and improve existing tracking setups to ensure data accuracy, attribution integrity, and reporting reliability
Establish best practices for analytics documentation, QA processes, and ongoing tracking governance
Ensure analytics implementations align to Shopify ecommerce architecture, including core commerce events, custom events, and third-party integrations


Reporting, Dashboards & Insights


Build and maintain reporting dashboards that deliver clear, actionable insights, not just surface-level metrics
Utilize SQL, BigQuery, and Python to support scalable analysis, data modeling, and reporting workflows
Partner with stakeholders to ensure reporting supports both day-to-day optimization and executive decision-making
Identify trends, anomalies, and performance opportunities and translate them into clear, strategic narratives


Client Partnership & Agency Collaboration


Act as a senior analytics partner to clients, bringing strong communication skills and thoughtful “bedside manner”
Confidently explain analytics strategies, tracking approaches, and insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Collaborate within an agency environment, working cross-functionally with Strategy, CRO, CRM, Engineering, and Delivery teams
Support pre-sales efforts through analytics audits, measurement assessments, and opportunity sizing


Internal Enablement & Thought Leadership


Help define and evolve Domaine’s analytics standards, frameworks, and best practices
Support internal education around analytics strategy, measurement planning, and data-informed decision-making
Stay current on analytics platform updates, privacy changes, and evolving ecommerce measurement approaches


Skills

Required


Deep expertise in Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and Google Tag Manager (GTM)
Strong experience designing and implementing ecommerce analytics strategies for Shopify-based brands
Hands-on experience with Shopify ecommerce implementations, including analytics, tagging, and data validation
Advanced proficiency in SQL, with experience querying large datasets in BigQuery
Working knowledge of Python for analysis, automation, or data transformation
Experience building dashboards and performance reporting for ecommerce stakeholders
Ability to think strategically about measurement, not just technical implementation
Experience working in an agency environment with multiple clients and priorities
Strong communication skills and comfort working directly with clients


Nice to Have


Experience with Adobe Analytics
Familiarity with Tableau, Tealium, or other enterprise analytics and CDP platforms
Experience supporting large, omnichannel or enterprise-scale ecommerce organizations
Exposure to privacy-forward measurement, consent frameworks, or advanced data warehousing


Ideal Candidate Profile


Strategically minded analyst who understands how data enables better business decisions
Technically strong, detail-oriented, and comfortable navigating complex data ecosystems
Clear communicator with strong client presence and collaborative instincts
Comfortable operating in ambiguity and helping teams find clarity through data
Curious, proactive, and motivated to help build and mature a best-in-class analytics practice


Perks & Benefits


Flexible working schedule
Remote-first culture
Continued education and personal development opportunities


We are an Equal Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law.

Title: Junior Systems Analyst
Description:
Reporting to the Senior Director of Information Technology, the Junior IT Systems Analyst (Information Management) supports day-to-day operation and improvement of business systems with a focus on information management practices, information policy, and data clean-up activities. This role helps maintain data quality and integrity across key platforms by assisting with data classification, retention and disposal practices, access controls, and documentation. You will work with business teams to gather requirements, troubleshoot issues, support system changes, and contribute to consistent, compliant handling of information across the organization.

This is a 3-4 month, full-time hourly position, with a possibility of extension.

Know-How

Practical/ Technical Knowledge


A post-secondary degree or diploma in Information Technology, Information Management, Business Technology, Library/Records Management, or a related discipline.
0–2 years of experience in an IT support, systems analyst, records/information management, or data quality role (co-op/internship experience considered).
Foundational knowledge of information management concepts (e.g., records lifecycle, retention, disposition, classification, metadata, and discoverability).
Experience performing data clean-up activities (deduplication, standardizing values, resolving missing/invalid fields) using tools such as Excel and/or system import/export functions.
Ability documenting processes and creating clear user guides, data definitions, and simple data standards.
Basic understanding of data privacy, access controls, and secure handling of sensitive information.
Familiarity with ticketing systems and incident/service management practices (e.g., triage, prioritization, escalation, and communication).
Working knowledge of common enterprise platforms (e.g., Microsoft 365, SharePoint/Teams) and an interest in learning new systems quickly.
Basic SQL or reporting knowledge is an asset (ability to run simple queries or validate data).
Demonstrated ability to communicate effectively, collaborate with others, and ask for support when needed.


Planning, Organizing and Integrating


Must be able to work under pressure in order to meet deadlines, manage multiple high-priority projects in a fast-paced and changing environment, and perform as a leader/mentor.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
Excellent understanding of how data is collected, analyzed, and utilized while maintaining awareness of big data developments.
Ability to maintain professional standards, demeanor, and ethics at all times.
Ability to identify when situations require assistance or escalation.


Communicating and Influencing Skills


Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to work with others and use tact and diplomacy to resolve sensitive matters.
Ability to utilize judgment in resolving issues or in making recommendations.
Ability to interact with other departments and business users who are not as technical.
Ability to relate well to people of varying backgrounds.
Demonstrated ability to communicate clearly with stakeholders and work effectively with cross-functional teams.
Ability to interact with other departments and business users who are not as technical in nature.
Working in an environment that coordinates with support providers.
High level of quality and integrity, with a sense of urgency and results orientation.


Responsibilities


Provide Tier 1–2 support for assigned business applications and information repositories by troubleshooting issues, documenting findings, and escalating appropriately.
Support information management practices, including file/record classification, naming conventions, metadata usage, retention, and disposition activities.
Assist with information policy implementation by translating requirements into practical procedures, checklists, and system configurations (where applicable).
Perform data clean-up and remediation tasks (e.g., standardizing values, correcting invalid entries, deduplicating records, and filling required fields) in coordination with business owners.
Support periodic data quality checks and reconcile issues by comparing source and target systems, logs, and reports.
Maintain data dictionaries, basic ERD/process diagrams, and system documentation (how-to guides, runbooks, and change notes) to support consistent practices.
Assist with user access requests and reviews by following established processes for role-based access and least-privilege principles.
Support small system changes and enhancements (e.g., configuration updates, form/field changes, workflow tweaks) and participate in testing/UAT.
Assist with data imports/exports and routine reporting, ensuring outputs align with defined data standards and privacy requirements.
Collaborate with IT, Privacy/Compliance, and business teams to identify opportunities to improve information organization, searchability, and lifecycle management.
Stay current through training and self-directed learning on information management, data quality, and supported technologies.

Title: Data Modeler
Description:
**Must be from BFSI or Insurance Industry




Data Modeler

Toronto, ON - Hybrid (3 Days WFO)

12 months + Possible Extension




**Must be from BFSI or Insurance Industry




Pay: CAD 50/Hr.




Role expectation: Designs and governs enterprise insurance data models aligned with ACORD standards to enable consistent, scalable, and integrated data ecosystems.




Responsibilities

1. Design conceptual, logical, and physical data models aligned to ACORD Information and Data Models

2. Develop enterprise canonical data models for core insurance domains (Policy, Claims, Party, Billing)

3. Ensure data consistency, normalization, and scalability across systems

4. Map business requirements to ACORD-compliant data structures

5. Apply and interpret ACORD Reference Architecture components (Information Model, Data Model, Business Glossary)

6. Perform end-to-end data mapping (source → ACORD canonical → target systems)

7. Enable data integration and interoperability across legacy and modern insurance platforms

8. Support API, messaging, and data exchange design aligned to ACORD standards

9. Design data warehouse / data lake models using ACORD-aligned structures

10. Collaborate with business and technical teams to translate requirements into data models

11. Establish and enforce data standards, naming conventions, and metadata definitions

12. Contribute to data governance, data quality, and lineage frameworks

13. Maintain data models, dictionaries, and mapping documentation with full traceability

14. Support modernization initiatives by aligning legacy data models with ACORD framework

15. Perform model validation, impact analysis, and compliance checks against enterprise and regulatory standards




**Must be from BFSI or Insurance Industry

Title: Support Specialist
Description:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Support Specialist in Canada.

Are you passionate about helping people and delivering meaningful support in a mission-driven environment focused on sustainability? In this role, you will support participants in a residential energy efficiency and emissions reduction program, ensuring they receive clear guidance and timely assistance. You will interact directly with residents, contractors, and stakeholders through phone and email, helping them navigate program requirements and access energy-saving solutions. This position combines customer service, administrative support, and structured problem-solving in a fast-paced, performance-driven setting. You will play a key role in improving customer experience while contributing to broader environmental and energy transition goals. Working remotely with a structured schedule, you will support initiatives that directly impact communities and sustainability outcomes.

Accountabilities


Respond to inbound calls and emails from program participants, contractors, and stakeholders, providing accurate and helpful information about program requirements and processes.
Support residents in understanding and accessing energy-saving solutions and program services.
Assist with application reviews, data entry, and administrative tasks to ensure smooth program operations.
Meet key performance indicators (KPIs), including response time, call handling efficiency, and quality standards.
Maintain accurate records and ensure information provided is consistent and aligned with program guidelines.
Collaborate with internal teams to resolve inquiries, escalate issues, and improve service delivery.
Contribute to a positive customer experience by delivering clear, empathetic, and solution-oriented communication.
Support continuous improvement efforts by identifying recurring issues and suggesting process enhancements.


Requirements


0-1 year of experience in a customer service, call center, or client-facing support role (call center experience is an asset but not required).
Post-secondary education is preferred.
Strong verbal and written communication skills in English; additional language skills (French, Cantonese, Mandarin, Farsi, Punjabi, or Tagalog) are considered a strong asset.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications and basic digital tools.
Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage administrative tasks accurately.
Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a structured, performance-driven environment.
Interest in sustainability, energy efficiency, environmental programs, or related fields is an asset.
Strong interpersonal skills with a customer-first mindset and problem-solving orientation.
Ability to work full-time hours aligned with Pacific Time (8:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.).


Benefits


Competitive hourly compensation ranging from CAD $18.10 to $27.05, depending on experience and qualifications.
Comprehensive health benefits including medical, dental, and vision coverage with company premium support.
Life insurance, accidental death & dismemberment (AD&D), and long-term disability coverage fully paid by the employer.
Retirement savings plan with company matching contributions (RRSP).
Paid vacation, sick leave, personal days, and statutory holidays.
Paid volunteer time to support community engagement initiatives.
Employee recognition program with rewards redeemable for gift cards.
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) supporting personal and professional well-being.
Access to training and development opportunities for career growth.
Inclusive, purpose-driven culture focused on sustainability and social impact.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Title : Data Engineer

Location : Toronto, ON

Position: Contract




Job Responsibilities :

Job Summary

We are seeking a Data Engineer with strong experience in AWS-native data services and Informatica to design, build, and optimize scalable data pipelines. The role involves working with large, structured and semi-structured datasets to support analytics, reporting, and downstream business intelligence platforms in a regulated enterprise environment.




Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain scalable ETL/ELT pipelines using AWS Glue and Informatica (PowerCenter / IDMC / IICS)
Build and optimize analytical datasets using Amazon Redshift
Enable data exploration and ad-hoc analysis using Amazon Athena
Ingest data from multiple sources (RDBMS, files, APIs, cloud storage) into AWS data platforms
Ensure data quality, data validation, and reconciliation across ingestion and transformation layers
Implement partitioning, indexing, compression, and performance tuning strategies for Redshift and Athena
Work with structured formats (CSV, JSON, Parquet, Avro) in Amazon S3
Collaborate with BI, analytics, and data science teams to deliver curated datasets
Implement logging, monitoring, and error handling for data pipelines
Adhere to security, governance, and compliance standards (PII, encryption, IAM, auditability)
Support production issues, root cause analysis, and continuous optimization

Title : Data Engineer

Location : Toronto, ON

Position: Contract


Job Responsibilities :

Job Summary

We are seeking a Data Engineer with strong experience in AWS-native data services and Informatica to design, build, and optimize scalable data pipelines. The role involves working with large, structured and semi-structured datasets to support analytics, reporting, and downstream business intelligence platforms in a regulated enterprise environment.


Key Responsibilities

  • Design, develop, and maintain scalable ETL/ELT pipelines using AWS Glue and Informatica (PowerCenter / IDMC / IICS)
  • Build and optimize analytical datasets using Amazon Redshift

  • Implement partitioning, indexing, compression, and performance tuning strategies for Redshift and Athena
  • Work with structured formats (CSV, JSON, Parquet, Avro) in Amazon S3
  • Collaborate with BI, analytics, and data science teams to deliver curated datasets
  • Implement logging, monitoring, and error handling for data pipelines
  • Adhere to security, governance, and compliance standards (PII, encryption, IAM, auditability)
  • Support production issues, root cause analysis, and continuous optimization

  • Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts (fact/dimension modeling, star/snowflake schema)
  • Cloud & Data Ecosystem
  • Experience with Amazon S3, IAM, CloudWatch
  • Understanding of data lake and lakehouse architectures
  • Experience working with batch and near-real-time data processing

  • Exposure to AWS Step Functions, Lambda, EMR
  • Knowledge of data governance, metadata management, and lineage
  • Experience with Informatica Cloud (IDMC) advanced services
  • Familiarity with Agile / Scrum delivery models

Title: Data Engineer, Analytics Data Engineering
Description:
Dropbox is a Virtual First company. For this role, we are currently only authorized to hire candidates from the following provinces: Alberta, British Columbia, Ontario, and Saskatchewan.

Company Description

Dropbox isn’t just a workplace—it’s a living lab for more enlightened ways of working. We're a global community of bold visionaries and resourceful doers who are shaping the future of Dropbox—and with it the future of work. Our Virtual First model combines the autonomy of a distributed workplace with the power of human connection, making space for both meaningful work and meaningful relationships. With our start-up mindset and enterprise-level opportunities, you can be who you are and grow into who you’re meant to be. Here, you can own your impact to make work more intuitive, joyful, and human—for you as a Dropboxer and for hundreds of millions of people worldwide. If you're ready to push boundaries—and yourself— Dropbox is ready for you.

Team Description

The Dropbox Engineering Team builds the technology that creates more enlightened ways of working for hundreds of millions of people. Every day, our platforms—including Dropbox Dash, Dropbox Sign, and our core sync engine—handle over a billion files for users worldwide, creating engineering challenges as great as the opportunity for impact. Our software engineering team uses a range of technologies to solve interesting problems, including Python, React, Node.js, JavaScript, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, and Android development. We think like a startup but build for an enterprise, exploring new possibilities that transform how people work. If you're excited about turning complex technical challenges into intuitive solutions at scale, join our Engineering team. Areas of work include Machine Learning Engineers, Infrastructure Engineer, Product SWE Frontend and Backend, Mobile Software Engineers (iOS and Android), Engineering Manager, Data Engineer, Software Development Engineer in Test, Security Engineering, Site Reliability Engineer, Technical Program Managers, Network Engineer, Datacenter Engineer, Technical Supply Chain Manager and more.

Role Description

In this role you will build large, scalable analytics pipelines using modern data technologies. This is not a “maintain existing platform” or “make minor tweaks to current code base” kind of role. We are effectively building from the ground up and plan to leverage the most recent Big Data technologies. If you enjoy building new things without being constrained by technical debt, this is the job for you!

Our Engineering Career Framework is viewable by anyone outside the company and describes what’s expected for our engineers at each of our career levels. Check out our blog post on this topic and more here.

Responsibilities


Help define company data assets (data model), Spark, SparkSQL jobs to populate data models
Help define and design data integrations, data quality frameworks and design and evaluate open source/vendor tools for data lineage
Work closely with Dropbox business units and engineering teams to develop strategy for long term Data Platform architecture to be efficient, reliable and scalable
Conceptualize and own the data architecture for multiple large-scale projects, while evaluating design and operational cost-benefit tradeoffs within systems
Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and data scientists to understand data needs, representing key data insights in a meaningful way
Design, build, and launch collections of sophisticated data models and visualizations that support multiple use cases across different products or domains
Optimize pipelines, dashboards, frameworks, and systems to facilitate easier development of data artifacts


Many teams at Dropbox run Services with on-call rotations, which entails being available for calls during both core and non-core business hours. If a team has an on-call rotation, all engineers on the team are expected to participate in the rotation as part of their employment. Applicants are encouraged to ask for more details of the rotations to which the applicant is applying.

Requirements


5+ years of Spark, Python, Java, C++, or Scala development experience
5+ years of SQL experience
5+ years of experience with schema design, dimensional data modeling, and medallion architectures
Experience with the Databricks platform and data lake architectures for large-scale data processing and analytics
Excellent product strategic thinking and communications to influence product and cross-functional teams by identifying the data opportunities to drive impact
BS degree in Computer Science or related technical field involving coding (e.g., physics or mathematics), or equivalent technical experience
Experience designing, building and maintaining data processing systems


Preferred Qualifications


7+ years of SQL experience
7+ years of experience with schema design, dimensional data modeling, and medallion architectures
Experience with Airflow or other similar orchestration frameworks
Experience building data quality monitoring using MonteCarlo or similar tools


Compensation

Canada Pay Range

$128,400—$173,700 CAD

The range listed above is the expected annual salary/OTE for this role, subject to change.

Salary/OTE is just one component of Dropbox’s total rewards package. All regular employees are also eligible for the corporate bonus program or a sales incentive (target included in OTE) as well as stock in the form of Restricted Stock Units (RSUs).

Benefits

Dropbox is committed to investing in the holistic health and wellbeing of all Dropboxers and their families. Our benefits and perks programs include, but are not limited to:


Competitive medical, dental and vision coverage*
Retirement savings through a defined contribution pension or savings plan**
Flexible PTO/Paid Time Off, paid holidays, Volunteer Time Off, and more, allowing you time to unplug, unwind, and refresh
Income Protection Plans: Life and disability insurance*
Business Travel Protection: Travel medical and accident insurance*
Perks Allowance to be used on what matters most to you, whether that’s wellness, learning and development, food and groceries, and much more
Parental benefits including: Parental Leave, Fertility Benefits, Adoptions and Surrogacy support, and Lactation support
Mental health and wellness benefits


Additional Benefits Details Are Available Upon Request.


Where group plans are not available, allowances may be provided
Benefit, amount, and type are dependent on geographical location, based upon applicable law or company policy


Dropbox supports responsible use of AI for preparation, but misrepresentation of skills or experience is not permitted. See our AI philosophy.

Dropbox is an equal opportunity employer. We are a welcoming place for everyone, and we do our best to make sure all people feel supported and connected at work. A big part of that effort is our support for members and allies of internal groups like Asians at Dropbox, BlackDropboxers, enABLE, TODOS (Latinx), Pridebox (LGBTQ), Vets at Dropbox, and Women at Dropbox.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Established in 1973, Roots is a proudly Canadian lifestyle brand that celebrates authenticity, quality, and community. Inspired by the great outdoors, we create timeless, sustainable apparel and leather goods designed for comfort and everyday adventure. At Roots, we believe in fostering a culture of inclusivity, creativity, and teamwork – where every team member plays a role in shaping our brand’s legacy. If you bring passion to everything you do, lead with integrity, and believe in the power of collaboration to drive growth, we’d love to have you on our journey!

Nature & Scope → Your Role at Roots

As an Analytics Engineer, you'll sit at the intersection of data engineering and analytics, turning raw retail data into trusted, scalable assets and the insights that bring them to life. As a collaborative member of our data team, you'll contribute to the data models that power solutions across the Roots business and help build the data products, semantic layers, and self-service tools that empower every team at Roots to access trusted data independently. Our data team is small and collaborative, meaning you'll wear multiple hats and have broad exposure across the business.

Key Responsibilities → How You’ll Make An Impact


Support end-to-end data transformation, converting raw omnichannel source data (POS, e-commerce, inventory, CRM, and marketing) into clean, structured layers within Snowflake.
Contribute to the design and build of components of the Snowflake data mart, applying dimensional modelling best practices including star schemas and slowly changing dimensions, to help create reliable, performant data products that serve ad hoc analysis, BI dashboards, and AI data solutions.
Support Power BI report development end-to-end, from semantic model design to polished, publish-ready reports, giving commercial, merchandising, marketing, and finance teams self-service access to trusted data without analyst dependency.
Perform ad hoc analysis using SQL and Python directly in Snowflake to help answer pressing business questions, uncover trends, and deliver timely insights that inform planning, buying, and operational decisions.
Assist in maintaining data quality standards, including testing frameworks, pipeline alerting, and documentation, to help ensure the data mart is trustworthy and well-understood across the business.
Support the development and maintenance of Gen AI data solutions, including natural language interfaces enabling any business user to query the data mart in plain English.
Partner cross-functionally with merchandising, planning, marketing, and commercial teams to understand analytical needs and help translate them into scalable data products.
Participate in the continuous improvement of our data platform, contributing ideas around new tools, patterns, and AI capabilities that keep Roots ahead of the curve.


Qualifications & Experience → The Skills You Bring


3+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, business intelligence, or an equivalent data analytics role.
Strong SQL proficiency and hands-on experience with Snowflake or a comparable cloud data warehouse.
Hands-on experience with ELT/ETL platforms, with Matillion or a similar cloud-native transformation tool preferred.
Proficiency building data models and dashboards with Power BI.
Solid understanding of dimensional modelling methodologies including star schemas and slowly changing dimensions.
Experience with version control using Git.
Familiarity with data quality practices including testing, documentation, and pipeline monitoring.
Exposure to Gen AI solutions such as Snowflake Cortex or Microsoft Fabric Copilot is preferred.
Retail or apparel industry experience across omnichannel environments is an asset.
Python proficiency for data transformation, ad hoc analysis, or automation is a plus.


At Roots we appreciate that skills and expertise are cultivated through a range of experiences. We are committed to reflecting Canada's diverse landscape in our products, team, and workplace culture. We value your unique perspective and encourage you to apply, even if you don't meet every listed requirement. Accommodations are available for applicants throughout the recruitment process.

Please note: Roots uses technology-assisted tools, including artificial intelligence (AI), to support parts of the recruitment process. These tools may be used to help review applications, assess qualifications, and support interview documentation. All hiring decisions are reviewed and made by our Talent team and key leaders involved in the recruitment process.

The posted salary range is intended to reflect the competitive market value of this role and is provided to support transparency in our hiring process. Final compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to internal equity, relevant skills, demonstrated knowledge, experience, and overall fit for the position.

Title: Analytics & Insights Specialist
Description:
Why Meridian?

At Meridian our aspiration is to integrate our purpose into everything we do for people, the planet, and communities. We believe that our greatest opportunity is to create opportunity and meet people where they are.

Factor us into your next career opportunity. We want you to grow with us and have an experience that’s different. This is a place where you can expect the unexpected.

Find our story here: About Meridian

The role:

The Analytics & Insights Specialist provides proactive, actionable prescriptive analytical support and business insight to the Lines of Business(LOB). With strong analytical skills, a knowledge of reporting systems, and a talent for influencing without authority, they will act as an internal consultant, adding value and insight to key stakeholders and management.

As a subject matter expert, the Analytics & Insights Specialist demonstrates an ability to present complicated analytical material in a concise and compelling manner and, interpret insights in a meaningful way for management and partners.

The role will support the advancement of analytics and data management, processes, and tools to contribute to the achievement of Meridian’s strategic and in year goals. This role will be responsible for maintaining and enhancing the current Reporting environment along with the development of new and adhoc reporting to support the line of businesses.

Key Responsibilities

Insights, Analytics & Reporting


Analyze, prepares and presents scheduled reports (i.e. key business performance indicators, dashboards and scorecards) and ad hoc reports (i.e. in support of projects or departmental initiatives) for the lines of business, both proactively and upon request
Acts as the analytics lead for Line of business on internal and enterprise initiatives
Provides Line of business leaders with management reporting that identifies issues, opportunities and emerging trends that enables the user to take action
Identifies and monitors key performance indicators, sharing relevant, accurate and insightful findings with LOB leadership team and key partners at various levels within the organization on a timely basis
Leads qualitative and quantitative analysis of relevant industry and consumer behaviour trends to highlight improvements, growth opportunities and new business opportunities
Demonstrates an ability to leverage technology to streamline processes, tools and analysis
Maintain & Enhance current LOB Reporting environment
Development of new reporting & conversion of existing legacy reporting using new technology


Business Solutions


Assists with the development of various programs by collecting, analysing and collating Member information ensuring that Meridian delivers a personalized solution that is in relation to their transactional, advice or price-driven relationships
Provides recommendations to enhance the Member Experience while improving the overall efficiency of its processes
Educate the business on opportunities to enhance Member needs and contribute to relationship growth;


Communication


Coordinates the acquisition of data from various, relevant data sources and applies analytical skills to convert data into meaningful information
Assesses information and conducts research to evaluate business issues

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Here at Rakuten Kobo Inc. we offer a casual working start-up environment and a group of friendly and talented individuals. Our employees rank us highly in terms of commitment to work/life balance. We realize that for our people to be innovative, creative and passionate they need to feel valued and supported. We believe in rewarding all our employees with competitive salaries, performance based annual bonuses, stock options and training opportunities.

If you’re looking for a company that inspires passion, personal, and professional growth – join Kobo and come help us on our mission of making reading lives better.

The Role

Rakuten Kobo Inc. is seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our Data Storytelling & Insights team, working closely with our engineering foundations. This role blends strong technical ownership of analytics‑ready data with the ability to translate complex information into clear, actionable insights. You’ll work end‑to‑end across the analytics lifecycle—building scalable, trusted data models and partnering with stakeholders to deliver dashboards and analyses that inform key business decisions.

Key Responsibilities


Design, build, and maintain analytics‑ready data models and pipelines using Dataform and BigQuery, including migrating and modernizing legacy logic into clearer, more reusable, and better governed data assets.
Ensure analytics code and pipelines are reliable, well‑documented, and suited for downstream reporting, experimentation, and analysis.
Build and maintain Tableau dashboards and reports that are reusable, trustworthy, and grounded in centralized, well‑modeled data.
Translate ambiguous business questions into clear analyses that explain what happened, why it happened, and what to do next.
Partner with business stakeholders and analytics peers to define requirements, metrics, and scalable analytics solutions.
Contribute to shared analytics standards and best practices across SQL, Dataform, Tableau, and documentation.
Leverage modern AI tools and LLM‑based coding agents to accelerate analytics workflows (e.g., SQL development, modeling, debugging, documentation), while applying strong judgment to validate correctness, performance, and data quality.


The Skill Set

Must-haves


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or related field.
5+ years of experience in data analytics, analytics engineering, or BI, with demonstrated ownership of analytics data pipelines and models.
Strong SQL skills, including complex joins, window functions, and working with semi‑structured or nested data.
Solid understanding of data modeling and designing tables for downstream analytics and reporting use cases.
Strong analytical thinking, problem‑solving, and communication skills.
Experience working with modern cloud data warehouses (BigQuery preferred).
Hands‑on experience with Dataform (experience with dbt is a plus).
Experience with BI and visualization tools such as Tableau, Looker, or Domo.
Familiarity with analytics engineering best practices (e.g., testing, CI/CD for data).


Nice to haves


Experience using LLM‑based coding assistants to support analytics or data engineering workflows.
Working knowledge of Python for analysis, automation, or ML‑adjacent use cases.
Familiarity with cloud platforms (Google Cloud Platform preferred).
Experience with Git‑based version control and analytics workflow automation.


The Perks


Flexible hours and hybrid remote working environment
Subsidized in-office lunches
Four extended long weekends in the summer
Full benefits starting from your first day
Paid Volunteer days, unlimited sick days, and 3% RRSP matching
Work From Anywhere Program for up to 3 months (90 days)
Lifestyle spending account
Talent and development training budget
Free Kobo device + free weekly e-book or audiobook
Weekly Kobo Tech University sessions
Maternity/paternity leave top up
Dog friendly office


About Rakuten Kobo Inc.

Rakuten Kobo Inc. is the world’s digital bookseller created by and for booklovers. Owned by Tokyo-based Rakuten Group, Inc. and headquartered in Toronto, Rakuten Kobo’s millions of worldwide users can read anytime, anywhere, and on any device. With a mission to make reading lives better for all, Rakuten Kobo connects readers to stories using thoughtful and personalized curation of eBooks and audiobooks, and the best dedicated eReaders and apps for reading. With the singular focus of making reading lives the best they can be, Kobo’s open platform allows people to fit reading into more moments in their busy lives. To learn more about Rakuten Kobo, visit www.kobo.com.

Rakuten Kobo Inc. is an equal opportunity employer. Accessibility accommodations for candidates with disabilities participating in the selection process are available on request. Any information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially.  

Rakuten Kobo would like to thank all applicants for their interest in this role however only qualified candidates will be shortlisted.  

#RKIND

Five Principles for Success

Our worldwide practices describe specific behaviors that make Rakuten unique and united across the world. We expect Rakuten employees to model these 5 Shugi Principles of Success.

Always improve, Always Advance - Only be satisfied with complete success - Kaizen

Passionately Professional - Take an uncompromising approach to your work and be determined to be the best

Hypothesize - Practice - Validate – Shikumika - Use the Rakuten Cycle to succeed in unknown territory

Maximize Customer Satisfaction - The greatest satisfaction for our teams is seeing their customers smile

Speed!! Speed!! Speed!! - Always be conscious of time - take charge, set clear goals, and engage your team

Rakuten is an equal opportunity employer. Accessibility accommodations for candidates with disabilities participating in the selection process are available on request. Any information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially.

Rakuten would like to thank all applicants for their interest in this role however only qualified candidates will be shortlisted.

Beware of fraudulent job offers claiming to be from Rakuten. Rakuten does not send unsolicited job offers or request money during the recruitment process. Learn more: https://rakutenemploymentalert.com/

At the time of posting, Rakuten expects the Compensation (base salary + discretionary bonus) for this role to be within the range shown below. Individual compensation will vary based on job-related factors, including the skills, qualifications, and experience of the successful candidate as well as business need and geographic location. The successful applicant for this role will be eligible for stock options, health, vision, dental insurance, RRSP matching, Personal Time Off (PTO), Volunteer Time Off (VTO), and other employee benefits as the company implements.

CAD $97,020.00 - 147,020.00 annually
