Responsibilities include:

  • Create market analytics, sample data, data stories, and proof points that support sales opportunities, executive conversations, trials, and marketing campaigns
  • Help quantify opportunity volume, account coverage, signal activity, and potential pipeline relevance for prospects
  • Support outbound and inbound marketing campaigns with relevant sample data, market examples, data stories, and other collateral needs
  • Build and maintain reusable assets, including sample reports, vertical-specific examples, sales intelligence workshop materials, trial templates, and AI prompts
  • Help prospects understand how Citylitics identifies nuanced signals often missed by generic search tools, broad data providers, bid platforms, or general AI tools
  • Create and maintain methodology collateral, FAQs, slides, talk tracks, and examples that support sales intelligence workshops, trials, and customer conversations

Configure Trials and Manage Demo Environments

You will help prospects experience a relevant, well-configured version of Citylitics during demos and trials, while ensuring demo environments are ready for AE-owned demos.

Responsibilities include:

  • Set up and configure trial accounts based on prospect needs, target markets, user roles, geographies, use cases, and signal preferences
  • Manage demo environments to ensure they remain accurate, current, and ready for AE-owned demos
  • Conduct 1:1 training calls to help trial users navigate the platform, interpret signals, use dashboards, and apply insights to their workflow
  • Monitor trial activity and support AEs in interpreting user engagement, adoption, feedback, workflow fit, and demonstrated value
  • Identify opportunities to improve trial configuration, demo environment readiness, training, and support processes

Scope Data Integration and Implementation Plans

You will help prospects understand how Citylitics can fit into their existing GTM systems, data environments, and workflows.

Responsibilities include:

  • Scope and present implementation and data integration plans based on customer needs, system requirements, data availability, workflow complexity, and existing GTM systems
  • Translate sales intelligence workshop findings into practical implementation considerations, data integration requirements, data needs, risks, and recommended next steps
  • Support pre-implementation syncs by documenting prospect needs, configured workflows, signal preferences, data integration considerations, and success criteria

Deliver Data Roadmap Proof-of-Concepts

You will help Citylitics evaluate and deliver new data capabilities that align with customer and prospect needs.

Responsibilities include:

  • Own assigned data roadmap tickets tied to customer needs, prospect requests, emerging use cases, and future infrastructure verticals
  • Assess data feasibility for new markets, sectors, signal types, workflows, and customer use cases
  • Research potential data sources, collection approaches, classification methods, enrichment opportunities, and QA requirements for new data areas
  • Build and manage proof-of-concepts for new data areas that Citylitics is exploring or branching into
  • Document learnings from proof-of-concepts, including data availability, quality, coverage, repeatability, operational effort, and commercial relevance

Support Procurement-Stage Handoff

You will help ensure a clean transition from the sales process into implementation and long-term customer success.

Responsibilities include:

  • Document prospect needs, use cases, configured workflows, signal preferences, trial learnings, user feedback, success criteria, and data integration considerations
  • Support the handoff from Sales to Customer Success and Implementation when the deal reaches procurement stage
  • Share relevant context, configuration notes, trial engagement, value proof points, and implementation risks to reduce loss of context between sales and onboarding

Requirements

Must-Have Experience

  • Working experience with AI agents for research, data analysis, workflow configuration, documentation, collateral creation, or customer-facing deliverables is critical
  • Keen interest in infrastructure industries and data products and services
  • 1-3 years of experience in data analysis, solutions consulting, implementation, customer success operations, product operations, market research, business analysis, or a similar role
  • Strong ability to analyze data and translate findings into clear business insights
  • Experience working directly with customers, prospects, or internal commercial teams
  • Comfort leading customer-facing conversations, data workshops, training sessions, or executive-level presentations
  • Strong communication skills, including the ability to explain data, methodology, data integration considerations, and workflow recommendations clearly
  • Strong comfort working with dashboards, reports, data exports, and analytical tools
  • Experience working with customer data environments and following data security and integration best practices
  • Ability to create clear collateral, summaries, and presentations using data
  • Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple sales cycles, trials, roadmap tickets, and internal requests at once

Nice-to-Have Experience

  • Experience in a data, SaaS, AI, market intelligence, sales intelligence, business intelligence, or research platform company
  • Experience supporting pre-sales, pilot, or trial processes
  • Experience conducting user training or customer enablement sessions
  • Experience creating market analytics, opportunity analyses, methodology collateral, or sales collateral
  • Experience scoping data integrations, implementation plans, data workflows, CRM workflows, or customer-specific configuration requirements
  • Experience with CRM integrations and data security is a major advantage
  • Experience supporting early-stage data product discovery, feasibility analysis, proof-of-concept development, or new-market data exploration

Benefits

What's in it for you?

  • Make real-world impact: Influence positive change across one of the biggest societal challenges of our generation: building and maintaining sustainable public infrastructure
  • Sell a genuinely differentiated platform: You'll represent a disruptive, data-driven solution with a compelling value proposition in a market that is eager to engage
  • Operate at the intersection of infrastructure, scale-up, and data science: We live where infrastructure meets AI and advanced data. There is no other team like ours in Toronto
  • Move fast, learn faster: There's no corporate bureaucracy here. You'll accomplish more in a few months than you would in years at a large, entrenched technology company
  • Be empowered by data and AI: We believe Data and AI will play an outsized role in the future of sales and decision-making. Every CityZen has access to generative AI tools and our full data universe to enhance productivity and encourage experimentation
  • Grow with intention: We invest deeply in our people through in-role coaching, skill-based development, an internal mentorship program, and clear internal promotion pathways
  • Win as a team: We work hard, we support one another, and we celebrate progress along the way. We're serious about solving infrastructure and just as serious about enjoying the journey together

Citylitics is an equal opportunity employer. We are passionate about providing a safe workplace where everyone is accepted and has the opportunity to grow with us. We are committed to making diversity and inclusivity part of our culture!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Konrad is a next-generation digital consultancy. We are dedicated to solving complex business problems for our global clients with creative and forward-thinking solutions. Our employees enjoy a culture built on innovation and a commitment to creating best-in-class digital products in use by hundreds of millions of consumers around the world. We hire exceptionally smart, analytical, and hard-working people who are lifelong learners.

About The Role

This role sits on our consulting team, where your internal title will be Consultant, working primarily in a data analytics capacity across client engagements.

As a Consultant focused on Data Analytics, you are an early-to-mid career data professional with a strong foundation in analysis and a desire to grow both your technical and consulting skill set. Leveraging your analytics background, you will support the delivery of digital products for our clients through analytics and reporting solutions that help organizations better understand their data and make informed decisions.

In addition to your analytics work, you will gain exposure to broader areas of digital consulting, including digital product development, digital transformation, and user experience (UX/CX). You are interested in understanding how data supports product decisions, customer experiences, and overall business strategy.

You will work closely with senior analysts, data scientists, engineers, product managers, designers, and business stakeholders across a variety of client engagements. With guidance and mentorship, you will contribute to analytics initiatives including dashboarding, exploratory analysis, KPI tracking, and data quality validation, while also building broader consulting skills across areas such as project management, user experience, and design thinking.

You are curious, detail-oriented, and motivated to continuously improve your analytical and consulting capabilities.

What You’ll Do


Support client and internal teams in understanding business questions and translating them into analytical requirements
Analyze data to identify trends, patterns, and insights under the guidance of senior team members Build and maintain dashboards and recurring reports to track key metrics and performance indicators
Assist in documenting metrics, definitions, and analytics processes
Collaborate with data engineers and other analysts to support data modeling and reporting layers Communicate insights clearly through visualizations, summaries, and presentations
Proactively identify opportunities to improve reporting, data quality, and analytical workflows


Qualifications


Minimum 2+ years of experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or analytics-focused role
Programming experience in Python, Bash scripting, R or related language
Working proficiency in SQL and experience querying cloud data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Redshift, BigQuery)
Experience with Business Intelligence and data visualization tools (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Power BI)
Foundational understanding of data modeling and analytics best practices
Experience defining and tracking KPIs and metrics
A/B testing and causal analysis
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail
Clear written and verbal communication skills Ability to work collaboratively in cross-functional and consulting-style environments
Interest in learning about data governance, privacy, and security best practices


Perks And Benefits


Comprehensive Health & Wellness Benefits Package
Retirement Planning
Parental Leave Program
Flexible Working Hours
Work from Home Flexibility
Service Recognition Programs
Socials, Outings & Retreats
Culture of Learning & Development


Bonus Points

Have you taken any courses at BrainStation? A lot of our design and development best practices and processes are taught during our courses, make sure to highlight this experience in your cover letter if you have!

Konrad is committed to maintaining a diverse work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status will receive consideration for employment. If you have any accessibility requirements or concerns regarding the hiring process or employment with us, please notify us so we can provide suitable accommodation.

The estimated compensation for this position is $75,000 to $100,000. This is an estimate and a compensation offer will vary based on applicant’s education, experience, skills, abilities and alignment with market data.

While we sincerely appreciate all applications, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Sales Analyst
Description:
Why Join Us?

At CARMA, our vibrant culture is the foundation of everything we do, fostering connections and ensuring employees feel valued, engaged, and part of a supportive community.

Founded in 1977, CARMA was the first Measurement Canada-approved supplier of electronic submetering systems, installing over 450,000 meters nationwide, with more than 100,000+ suites under contract. We provide submetering systems, billing services, and data for cost recovery and sustainability reporting across Canada, with offices in Lindsay, Toronto, Calgary, Edmonton, and Vancouver.




Proud to be Great Place to Work certified, we are committed to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion (DE&I), Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles, and Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR). With a robust benefits package, we create an environment where employees can thrive both personally and professionally.




Our core values of accountability, teamwork, customer-centric, respect, and excellence guide us in every decision and interaction. If these values align with yours, CARMA could be the perfect place for your next career move - where we encourage growth, prioritize collaboration, and celebrate success together.




Here’s what you can expect as part of the CARMA experience:

Competitive Salaries: A rewarding structure based on experience and performance.
Company-Wide Annual Salary Increase: Recognition of your contributions through annual salary adjustments.
Comprehensive Benefits Package: Including a healthcare spending account, dental and prescription drug coverage, life and disability insurance, an employee and family assistance program, and more.
Paid Sick Leave: Your health matters. We offer paid sick leave so you can rest and recover without financial worry.
Paid Personal Leave: Maintain work-life balance with paid personal leave for family time, appointments, or self-care.
Additional Vacation Leave: Additional days off to help you relax, recharge, and maintain a healthy work-life balance.
Wellness Week: A dedicated time to focus on health and well-being.
Investment in Tools & Resources: We invest in the latest tools, technology, and training to support your success and professional growth.
Professional Development Support: We provide resources and allowances for ongoing learning and career growth.
Employee Engagement Program: Initiatives designed to keep employees involved, motivated, and connected.
Team-Building Activities: We regularly organize activities that promote creativity, collaboration, and unity, building strong, trusting teams.
Monthly Company Lunches: A time to unwind, enjoy great food, and strengthen relationships with colleagues, celebrating both work and personal milestones.
Quarterly Townhall Meetings: We value open and transparent communication. These meetings keep everyone informed, encourage discussions, and include training sessions to support continuous learning. Plus, our "Ask Me Anything" sessions allow for direct interaction with leadership.
Volunteer and Community Involvement Opportunities: CARMA supports employees who want to give back to their communities through service.
Employee Referral Program: Rewarding employees for helping us grow the team with successful referrals.
Employee Recognition and Awards: Celebrating achievements and outstanding contributions across the company.
Work from Home Flexibility (Hybrid Role): Offering a balance between remote and office work.




If you’re passionate about uncovering insights through data and thrive in a collaborative, growth-focused environment, we have an exciting opportunity for a Sales Analyst to join our team in Toronto, ON. Our core business hours are from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Monday to Friday (may vary depending on the role). This role is ideal for someone eager to make a meaningful impact by supporting data-driven decision-making, collaborating closely with cross-functional teams, and contributing to the success of our sales operations and customer experience.




Sales Analyst

General Description:

Under general direction of the Chief Executive Officer, the Sales Analyst will be the key person for consulting with the business to understand its goals and objectives, processes, and requirements. The Sales Analyst will focus on the sales health of the business by creating detailed, and conclusive reports that provide important insights for the organization’s management. The Sales Analyst will know what’s really hiding in data; allowing for clearer insight, a better decision, competitive advantage, and most importantly, the success of our business and our customers. The Sales Analyst will also be responsible to manage the organizations CRM program as well as oversight to deal formation and sales contract submittal process.




Characteristics, Features, Requirements:

This position requires strong mathematical skills and the ability to understand and interpret numerical information. Verbal communication skills are imperative as analysis will be presented to senior management and stakeholders.




Reporting:

The Sales Analyst reports directly to the Vice President of Sales and Marketing. The Sales Analyst is required to report as follows, but not limited to:




Meet on a weekly basis with the sales team to share progress and updates.




Important and Essential Duties:

The duties of this position include, but are not limited to, the following:




Sales Analyst Responsibilities

Own end-to-end process of developing and maintaining complex reports and analyses, while ensuring timely and accurate delivery;
Be involved in key strategic projects by aligning data requirements with business needs and execution;
Support business areas by delivering ad hoc data requests, while collaborating to get to the optimal data interpretation;
Liaise with other departments to enhance knowledge of business products, system functionality and data quality;
Identify trends and key metrics to support the business in identifying crucial findings to make better decisions;
Manage CRM database and data entry process as well as ownership of deal formation and contract submission for handoff from Sales to the rest of the business.




Knowledge/skill/abilities

Excellent proficiency with SAS/SQL language;
Proficiency with Microsoft Suite, namely Excel;
Ability to work independently;
Excellent communication skills with the ability to leverage strong oral and written communication skills to effectively present key findings to management and other team members;
Strong work ethic, curious and persistent determination in reviewing and reconciling variances;
Highly organized, detail-oriented, and can initiate proactively;
Ability to produce deliverables accurately and on time with minimal supervision;
Flexibility to adapt to an evolving environment with shifting priorities and with the capacity to handle multiple competing demands and meet tight turnaround timelines;
Problem-solving skills, accuracy and attention to detail.




Qualifications

University Degree in a quantitative field, economics, business, engineering or equivalent;
Combined minimum of 2+ years’ experience analyzing data for sales, operations, customers service, etc. making strategic recommendations to senior management;
Earned experience working in the utility or billing industry, considered an asset;
Experience working with CRM, namely ZOHO One, considered an asset.




Working Conditions

Hybrid (home office/office environment);
On occasion, may be required to work evenings or weekends to meet department deadlines;
A collaborative and supportive work environment focused on employee engagement and professional growth.




Join us in our mission to make a meaningful impact and create a brighter, more efficient tomorrow.




Please note that this position is for an existing vacancy.




CARMA does not use AI to screen applicants.




As a condition of employment, the successful candidate will be required to initiate a Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check (CRJMC) prior to commencing the role. Results must be submitted to the Human Resources team. Any information disclosed will be reviewed in accordance with applicable policies, and consideration will be given to the nature and relevance of any findings.




CARMA Corp. is committed to meeting the accessibility needs of persons with disabilities in an effective and timely manner in accordance with the applicable standards set out in the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005 (AODA), the provisions of the Ontario Human Rights Code, and any other applicable legislation. We foster an inclusive organizational culture as an Equal Opportunity Employer.




Salary: $70,000 to $80,000 per annum. The posted salary range reflects the potential earning span for this role. Starting salary is determined based on factors such as relevant experience, education, training, skills, certifications, and overall alignment with the position. Internal pay equity and market compensation data may also be considered. Final salary varies based on several considerations, and starting offers are usually below the top of the posted range.




We thank you for your interest; however, only successful applicants will be contacted.

Title: RevOps Analyst (Analytics)
Description:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

RevOps Analyst (Analytics)

Department: Revenue Operations

Function: GTM Analyst, Analytics

Location: NYC, SF, Toronto

The Revenue Operations team’s mission is to build and maintain the systems, data, and processes that power our go-to-market engine. We are looking for a RevOps Analyst, Analytics to join the team, reporting to the Head of Revenue Operations, focused on partnering with the business to deliver insight to drive measurable revenue growth and operational impact.

In This Role You May


Partner with GTM leadership to elevate reporting, systems and process to drive revenue growth and decision making
Manage, and enhance our quote-to-cash processes to ensure accuracy and compliance
Collaborate cross-functionally to enable the Revenue team to be able to see a 360 degree view of the customer in the CRM, including product usage, customer engagement (support tickets, sales emails, marketing campaigns, attribution) and contract data to drive revenue growth and improve sales productivity
Drive initiative to improve data hygiene, reporting accuracy and CRM governance
Manage urgent ad hoc operational requests


This Role Is a Good Match If You


Have 3+ years of experience in sales operations or a related role
Have Salesforce experience; CPQ Experience and Admin certification plus
Have familiarity with GTM tech stacks and how they support revenue workflows (CRM, enrichment, engagement, automation, analytics)
Bring 3+ years of big data experience: using BI tools such as Looker or Tableau and/or writing complex yet efficient SQL is a daily habit for you
Have experience getting your hands dirty with ETL and familiar with popular data warehouses (BigQuery, Snowflake, etc)
Are an excellent communicator with senior management audiences - explaining “what it is”, “why it matters” and “what should we do” in terms of your data and analysis; delivering customer-oriented KPIs and dashboards that drive alignment


If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees At Cohere Enjoy These Perks

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

HTML:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

RevOps Analyst (Analytics)

Department: Revenue Operations

Function: GTM Analyst, Analytics

Location: NYC, SF, Toronto

The Revenue Operations team’s mission is to build and maintain the systems, data, and processes that power our go-to-market engine. We are looking for a RevOps Analyst, Analytics to join the team, reporting to the Head of Revenue Operations, focused on partnering with the business to deliver insight to drive measurable revenue growth and operational impact.

In This Role You May

  • Partner with GTM leadership to elevate reporting, systems and process to drive revenue growth and decision making
  • Manage, and enhance our quote-to-cash processes to ensure accuracy and compliance
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to enable the Revenue team to be able to see a 360 degree view of the customer in the CRM, including product usage, customer engagement (support tickets, sales emails, marketing campaigns, attribution) and contract data to drive revenue growth and improve sales productivity
  • Drive initiative to improve data hygiene, reporting accuracy and CRM governance
  • Manage urgent ad hoc operational requests

This Role Is a Good Match If You

  • Have 3+ years of experience in sales operations or a related role
  • Have Salesforce experience; CPQ Experience and Admin certification plus
  • Have familiarity with GTM tech stacks and how they support revenue workflows (CRM, enrichment, engagement, automation, analytics)
  • Bring 3+ years of big data experience: using BI tools such as Looker or Tableau and/or writing complex yet efficient SQL is a daily habit for you
  • Have experience getting your hands dirty with ETL and familiar with popular data warehouses (BigQuery, Snowflake, etc)
  • Are an excellent communicator with senior management audiences - explaining “what it is”, “why it matters” and “what should we do” in terms of your data and analysis; delivering customer-oriented KPIs and dashboards that drive alignment

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees At Cohere Enjoy These Perks

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

Title: Advanced Analyst: Apex
Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

The Data & Insights (D&I) team transforms data into actionable insights that power EA. We guide strategy and governance, build data and AI solutions, and ensure personalization experimentation and analytics; partnering across teams to improve decisions and create more meaningful player experiences.

Reporting to the Senior Manager of Analytics, you will have an impact on all game systems in Apex Legends, one of the world's biggest shooter franchises.

This is a hybrid remote/in-office role.

Responsibilities


Conduct A/B tests and causal inference to evaluate the impact of game content and feature changes.
Analyze behavioral data to provide insights into game features.
Collaborate with team members to improve the game optimization strategies in Apex Legends.
Model and simulate complex gameplay systems throughout the development lifecycle.
Design dashboards and presentations that communicate insights to team members.
Partner with Data Engineers and Architects to promote scalable analysis.


Qualifications


5+ years of professional experience informing product strategy with data analysis in gaming or adjacent digital products with high independence.
Degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Economics, or related discipline).
Expert–level SQL proficiency with experience extracting insights from complex datasets.
Experience applying hypothesis testing, modeling, and experiment evaluation using Python, familiarity with advanced experiment design and analysis approaches.
Experience designing self-service dashboards using Looker, Tableau, or other similar tools.
Familiarity with game design principles, player retention, and monetization models.


We want to connect you with job opportunities that align with your interests, skills, and expertise. When you create an EA Careers Account and are logged into the portal, you can click “Get Recommendations” to view a curated list of job openings. These recommendations are enhanced by automated processing, including artificial intelligence, and take into account your skills and experience. However, all employment decisions are made by our hiring teams, not by automated systems.

Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$114,300 - $156,200 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

Title: Azure Data Analyst
Description:
Position Description

This role is hybrid and requires you to be at our downtown Toronto and/or Client office at a minimum 2 days per week - subject to change at any time.

The Azure Data Analyst will play a critical role in designing, analyzing, and delivering enterprise‑scale data solutions within an Azure cloud ecosystem. This role requires a strong combination of data design expertise, business analysis skills, and domain knowledge in personal banking products. The candidate will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and agile delivery teams to translate business needs into high‑quality, scalable data models and solutions.

Your future duties and responsibilities

Data Design & Architecture


Design and maintain logical and physical data models aligned with business requirements and enterprise standards.
Define end to end data mappings from source systems to target data platforms, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Perform data profiling to assess data quality, structure, and anomalies, and recommend remediation strategies.
Support the definition of data standards, naming conventions, and reference data.


Azure & Databricks Development Support


Collaborate with engineering teams to design data solutions using Azure services (e.g., Azure Data Lake, Synapse, Databricks).
Work closely with Databricks developers to ensure data transformations align with approved data models.
Review and validate data pipelines to ensure compliance with business rules and architectural guidelines.


Business Analysis & Banking Domain Engagement


Partner with business stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document data and reporting requirements.
Apply strong understanding of personal banking products (such as accounts, customers, transactions, lending, and payments) to data design decisions.
Translate business processes and regulatory requirements into structured data representations.


SQL & Data Validation


Write advanced SQL queries for data exploration, validation, reconciliation, and issue investigation.
Support user acceptance testing (UAT) and data validation activities by comparing source and target datasets.
Identify data quality issues and work with technical teams to resolve root causes.


Agile Delivery & Collaboration


Actively participate in Agile ceremonies (sprint planning, grooming, stand ups, reviews).
Estimate and plan data design and analysis tasks in coordination with scrum teams.
Maintain accurate documentation and user stories using Jira and Confluence.
Support continuous improvement of agile data delivery practices.


Communication & Stakeholder Management


Communicate complex data concepts clearly to technical and non technical stakeholders.
Lead data design reviews, walkthroughs, and stakeholder discussions.
Provide guidance and mentorship to junior data analysts or designers when required.


Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Core Technical Skills


Strong experience with Azure Data Platform (Azure Data Lake, Azure SQL, Synapse, or related services).
Hands on experience with Databricks for data transformations and analytics.
Advanced SQL skills for data analysis, profiling, and validation.
Proven expertise in Data Profiling, Data Mapping, and Data Modeling (logical and physical).


Business & Domain Skills


Strong Business Analysis skills, including requirements elicitation and documentation.
Solid knowledge of personal banking products and data domains.
Ability to translate business needs into scalable and reusable data designs.


Tools & Ways of Working


Experience using Jira for backlog and sprint management.
Proficient in Confluence for documentation and knowledge sharing.
Demonstrated experience working in Agile/Scrum delivery environments.


Soft Skills


Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Strong stakeholder engagement and collaboration abilities.
Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail.
Ability to work independently while contributing effectively to team goals.


________________________________________

Nice to Have (Optional)


Experience in regulated financial services environments.
Exposure to data governance, metadata management, or data lineage tools.
Familiarity with reporting or downstream analytics use cases.


"CGI is providing a reasonable estimate of the pay range for this role. The determination of this range includes factors such as skill set level, geographic market, experience and training, and licenses and certifications. Compensation decisions depend on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $80,000–$130,000. This role is an existing vacancy."

!

Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.

At CGI, we value the strength that diversity brings and are committed to fostering a workplace where everyone belongs. We collaborate with our clients to build more inclusive communities and empower all CGI partners to thrive. As an equal-opportunity employer, being able to perform your best during the recruitment process is important to us. If you require an accommodation, please inform your recruiter.

To learn more about accessibility at CGI, contact us via email. Please note that this email is strictly for accessibility requests and cannot be used for application status inquiries.

Title: Sales Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Title: Sales Analyst

Id: 20932

Type: FullTime

Location: Bolton, ON

Vacancy: Replacement

Pay Range: $70,000 - $84,000

This role will be accountable for providing analysis and reporting of order backlog data and sales delivery data to various user groups across the organization. This role will liaise with Commercial Management, Finance, Sales Support, Operations, Customer Support, Logistics, Sales, and Marketing to provide backlog and sales delivery data organized by week, month, quarter and year and segmented by Geographic territory, Products, Manufacturing location, Markets, Sales Organization and other attributes as required.

Responsibilities


Daily monitoring, refresh and reconciliation of backlog data and invoices for unclean orders and shifts from current quarter.
During the course of refresh, reconciliation and monitoring, apply critical analysis to highlight discrepancies, trends, and higher level insights, to inform business leadership
Continuous improvement of the process and tools, to drive improved efficiency and quality of results
Conduct weekly backlog review meetings with Sales Support, Commercial Operations, Operations, Logistics, Customer Support and Accounting to ensure cross functional alignment on revenue recognition including customer acceptance requirements, shipment consolidation requirements, Incoterms and shipping duration and pre-shipment collection status
Document notes during weekly calls
Support backlog management and sales forecast accuracy by properly maintaining revenue recognition month, status color, confidence level, and partial revenue recognition—especially for newly confirmed orders
Assist business leadership with ad hoc backlog analysis
Ensure transparency of shipped goods status, including outbound tracking
Execute monthly backlog closing activities
Follow up on any missed revenue recognition items throughout the month
Lead the cleanup of $200K backlog, including associated revenue recognition
Prepare and submit weekly reports for meetings
Follow up with PE and/or team leaders for orders past due for initiation (kick off) in manufacturing
Follow up with PE and/or team leaders for orders with misalignment between sales order delivery dates and purchase order dates
Follow up with PE and/or team leaders for orders where delivery dates are out of alignment
Preparation and distribution of various views of backlog and sales deliveries data on a weekly basis
Data entry of delivery dates from manufacturing locations not using BaanIV
Data entry of Service order completion dates
Provide support to Orders to Cash continuous improvement project managers with data reporting and analysis required for project scoping, execution or metrics


Qualifications


Expert Knowledge of Microsoft Excel Pivot Tables, coding and other analysis tools and formulas, preferably experience with VBA macro programming
Proven report writing experience, preferably with SAP Business Objects
Prior experience with data analytics
Exposure ERP system use in a manufacturing environment involving sales order control, project control, production control and purchase control activities, preferably with an Infor BaanIV.
Exposure to an order management system, preferably MicroSoft Dyanmics CRM.
Exposure to international shipping and logistics activities, preferably with a strong working knowledge of Incoterms 2010.
Effective intake skills to develop a clear understanding of report requirements and business value.
Analytical ability with a strong attention to detail to manage large amounts of data and draw meaningful conclusions that are presented in clear and concise ways.

Title: Database Marketing Analyst (Data Science) - Hybrid
Description:
Company Overview

The Globe and Mail is a national icon and one of Canada's most recognized media brands. We proudly serve as a trusted destination for Canadians seeking the highest caliber of journalism, and we've garnered international acclaim for our data visualization, design, and creative storytelling.

We are committed to fostering diversity and inclusivity by reflecting all Canadians in both the stories that we tell and the composition of our workforce. We are proud partners with organizations like Indigenous Works, Pride at Work, the Canadian Centre for Diversity and Inclusion, and we are a signatory of the BlackNorth Initiative. Recognizing the importance of work-life balance, we offer flexible work arrangements and support programs. We also invest in our employees' growth through training and mentorship opportunities, enabling you to expand your skills and embrace new challenges.

No matter your position at The Globe, you'll be an integral part of an organization dedicated to making a positive difference in Canada. Join us.

Position Overview

The Globe and Mail is seeking an experienced Database Analyst, Database Marketing (Revenue) to support our subscriber growth, engagement, and revenue strategies in a highly competitive digital landscape. Reporting to the Manager, Database Marketing, this role is responsible for building and maintaining high‑quality customer audiences, enabling data‑driven multi‑channel marketing initiatives, and delivering actionable insights that improve subscriber experiences.

The successful candidate will leverage advanced SQL, Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), and disparate customer data sources to power personalized campaigns across email, web, app, and off‑platform digital channels. This role plays a critical part in automating campaign workflows, ensuring data accuracy and privacy compliance, and acting as a steward of subscriber data quality. This is an existing vacancy, and this position is a hybrid role based in Toronto.

Key Responsibilities

Database Marketing & Campaign Enablement


Translate business and marketing requirements into executable database‑driven marketing campaigns, primarily for email and digital channels.
Build and maintain accurate and scalable audience segments using customer profile, transactional, and behavioral data.
Support a comprehensive subscriber contact strategy, including preference management, exclusions, and consent requirements.
Develop automated and recurring campaign solutions using Oracle SQL, PL/SQL, and JavaScript.


Segmentation, Journeys & Personalization


Design and implement customer journeys that support acquisition, retention, engagement, and revenue goals.
Leverage the Customer Data Platform to deliver personalized and targeted customer experiences.
Consolidate and harmonize data from multiple CEM, CRM, and digital data sources to support journey execution.


Testing, QA & Optimization


Design and execute test‑and‑learn frameworks, including A/B testing and target vs. control campaigns across channels.
Perform end‑to‑end quality assurance to ensure campaign logic, data outputs, and creative assets align with business requirements.
Identify efficiencies, risks, and optimization opportunities throughout the campaign lifecycle.


Analytics & Insights


Analyze campaign performance using SQL, Tableau, and Amplitude, identifying trends, insights, and areas for improvement.
Produce both ad‑hoc and recurring reporting to support revenue and engagement objectives.
Clearly communicate insights and recommendations to internal stakeholders to inform future strategy.


Data Stewardship & Collaboration


Partner with application development and analytics teams to support database design, coding standards, and QA processes.
Ensure all marketing data outputs are accurate, compliant, and privacy‑safe.
Act as an advocate for subscriber data protection, data hygiene, and compliance with privacy legislation.


Required

QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE:


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Database Marketing, or a related field.
3-5 years of experience performing database analytics or marketing operations supporting customer acquisition, retention, and engagement.
Advanced SQL skills (Oracle SQL / PL‑SQL), including automation of scheduled jobs and campaign workflows.
Strong experience working with CRM systems and marketing automation platforms, particularly Customer Data Platforms (CDPs).
Proven experience building email and SMS contact lists using SQL.

Position Overview

The Globe and Mail is seeking an experienced Database Analyst, Database Marketing (Revenue) to support our subscriber growth, engagement, and revenue strategies in a highly competitive digital landscape. Reporting to the Manager, Database Marketing, this role is responsible for building and maintaining high‑quality customer audiences, enabling data‑driven multi‑channel marketing initiatives, and delivering actionable insights that improve subscriber experiences.

The successful candidate will leverage advanced SQL, Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), and disparate customer data sources to power personalized campaigns across email, web, app, and off‑platform digital channels. This role plays a critical part in automating campaign workflows, ensuring data accuracy and privacy compliance, and acting as a steward of subscriber data quality. This is an existing vacancy, and this position is a hybrid role based in Toronto.

Key Responsibilities

Database Marketing & Campaign Enablement

  • Translate business and marketing requirements into executable database‑driven marketing campaigns, primarily for email and digital channels.
  • Build and maintain accurate and scalable audience segments using customer profile, transactional, and behavioral data.
  • Support a comprehensive subscriber contact strategy, including preference management, exclusions, and consent requirements.
  • Develop automated and recurring campaign solutions using Oracle SQL, PL/SQL, and JavaScript.

Segmentation, Journeys & Personalization

  • Design and implement customer journeys that support acquisition, retention, engagement, and revenue goals.
  • Leverage the Customer Data Platform to deliver personalized and targeted customer experiences.
  • Consolidate and harmonize data from multiple CEM, CRM, and digital data sources to support journey execution.

Testing, QA & Optimization

  • Design and execute test‑and‑learn frameworks, including A/B testing and target vs. control campaigns across channels.
  • Perform end‑to‑end quality assurance to ensure campaign logic, data outputs, and creative assets align with business requirements.
  • Identify efficiencies, risks, and optimization opportunities throughout the campaign lifecycle.

Analytics & Insights

  • Analyze campaign performance using SQL, Tableau, and Amplitude, identifying trends, insights, and areas for improvement.

Data Stewardship & Collaboration

Title: Data Governance Analyst
Description:
Data Governance Analyst / Commercial Banking Data Analyst

Toronto, Ontario, Canada Must Need Locals only

$100K on CAD




Key Responsibilities:

Analyze, validate, and monitor large-scale North American Commercial Banking datasets involving customer, account, and business banking information across multiple databases.
Perform data extraction, transformation, reconciliation, and exception analysis using Python, SAS, and SQL for commercial banking reporting and operational processes.
Develop, maintain, and enhance data quality and validation rules to identify missing, duplicate, inconsistent, or high-risk records within commercial banking data environments.
Write, optimize, and troubleshoot complex SQL queries involving joins, aggregations, filtering, and data validation across multiple commercial banking database tables.
Support data governance, compliance, audit, and reporting initiatives by monitoring data accuracy, consistency, and operational standards within commercial banking systems.
Design and develop interactive Power BI dashboards, visual reports, and operational scorecards to present trends, exceptions, KPIs, and business insights to stakeholders and management teams.
Collaborate with business users, data teams, and technical stakeholders to improve reporting processes, automate workflows, and enhance overall data management efficiency.
Assist in identifying process improvement opportunities and implementing automation solutions for recurring operational and reporting tasks.
Document data validation procedures, reporting logic, and operational workflows to support governance and compliance requirements.
Participate in troubleshooting data discrepancies and resolving data-related operational issues.

Required Skills & Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, Finance, or related field.
Strong experience with SQL for data querying, analysis, and validation.
Hands-on experience with Python and/or SAS for data processing and reconciliation activities.
Experience working with commercial banking, financial services, or enterprise-scale operational datasets.
Proficiency in Power BI for dashboard development and data visualization.
Strong understanding of data quality management, data governance, and reporting processes.
Experience with relational databases and large-volume data environments.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional business and technical teams.

Preferred Qualifications:

Experience in North American Commercial Banking operations or financial data environments.
Knowledge of banking compliance, regulatory reporting, and operational risk management.
Familiarity with ETL processes and data reconciliation methodologies.
Experience with automation and workflow optimization initiatives.




Technical Skills:

Power BI

Data Governance

Business Intelligence & Dashboards

Title: Reporting Analyst
Description:
About Us

For over 50 years, our Property and Casualty insurance Company, CAA Insurance, has provided dependable and trustworthy service to protect Canadians and what matters to them. CAA Insurance Company offers a dynamic professional environment which strives to offer the ultimate customer experience. We share in each other’s successes, drive change and support each other while cultivating a great working environment to deliver on what is best for the customer.

Who we are

At CCG, we are committed to delivering an exceptional Associate experience. We offer:

Work-life harmony with access to an award-winning holistic wellness program,

Continuous learning through our robust corporate curriculum and education reimbursement program,

Incredible rewards, travel incentives, and product and service discounts,

Pay-for-performance and best-in-class recognition programs, and

Competitive benefits that include a defined contribution plan, personal spending account, and so much more.

Join our growing team where everyone belongs!

Position Details

What You Will Do


Analyze insurance performance historical data to understand the reasons for past performance and make recommendations that will influence the business growth and/or address portfolio / business concerns
Conduct regular, ongoing reviews on the sales performance measurements for each distribution channels, as well as portfolio analysis to assess quality of business being written
Recommend changes or adherence to prudent underwriting guidelines and practices
Respond to business needs by identifying appropriate information to analyze utilizing a variety of sources in data mining
Identify, understand, and communicate profitability drivers while collaborating with multi distribution channels, claims and accounting areas
Find ways to automate reports based on user requests or by observing frequency of ad-hoc reports being requested
Identify opportunities and responding to changes to ensure adherence to the corporate business success strategy
Communicate and consult proposed changes in underwriting rules, guidelines, practices and gaps based on findings
Provide Insurance field trend analysis, forecasts reports and findings that anticipate outcome
Ability to utilize EDW data / GW data, industry information and trend analysis to provide management recommendations
Provide detailed analysis of statistics that are outside of anticipated norms


Please note, salary range is not inclusive of total compensation. The expected salary range for this position will be impacted by factors such as the successful candidate’s skills, experience and working location, as well as the specific position’s business line, scope and level. Candidates with salary expectations outside of the range are encouraged to apply.

Who You Are


University Degree in Mathematics, Economics or Actuarial Science
3-5 years data reporting experience in the financial services industry. P&C Insurance industry experience is a definite asset.
Forecast reporting, data collection, data analysis, report generation and quality assurance experience is required
Professional designation preferred but not required: CIP/FCIP, CA. CMA. CPA, CGA or equivalent
Database Administration experience is a preferred asset
Working experience with MS Office products, SQL or database query tools including VBM and VBA is an asset
Power BI and Python experience are required
Excellent interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills
Excellent problem solver, detail oriented, ability to work in a team environment as well as work independently
Strong analytical and decision-making skills


Please note that internal applicants are asked to formally express their interest by applying online no later than 10 business days of the position being posted

Please note that we may use AI tools to help us through the recruitment process.

Our Commitment

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing employment accommodation in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005 (AODA). CAA CCG will provide accommodations to job applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. If you require an accommodation, please notify us and we will work with you to meet your needs.

Title: Inventory Analyst, Canada
Description:
Global Industrial

About Global Industrial

Global Industrial Company (NYSE: GIC) is a leading distributor of high-quality, industrial-strength equipment and supplies, serving organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. With more than 75 years of experience, customers rely on us for our broad portfolio of national and private brands, trusted service, and strong value proposition.

We help customers keep their operations running by delivering the right products when they need them—because We Can Supply That™.

Why Join Global Industrial

At Global Industrial, we’re One Team committed to delivering value and empowering our associates to grow. Our culture is built on:


Meritocracy & Pay for Performance – Your impact drives your rewards
High Say-Do Ratio – We follow through on our commitments
Career Growth & Internal Mobility – We invest in your future
Collaboration & Accountability – One team delivering real results


Make an impact. Be recognized. Grow your career with us.

Global Industrial — Delivering Value Starts Here

Key Responsibilities

Inventory Analysis & Optimization


Analyze inventory levels, turns, aging, and demand patterns across products, locations, and suppliers.
Identify surplus, low-velocity, and at-risk inventory and quantify financial impact to working capital and margin.
Develop and maintain inventory KPIs and dashboards (e.g., turns, days of supply, fill rate, aging, forecast accuracy).
Perform root-cause analysis on inventory imbalances, including forecast error, lead time assumptions, MOQ constraints, and demand variability.


Forecasting & Replenishment Enablement


Review inventory levels and replenishment signals using cloud-based planning tools, translating insights into data-driven recommendations that inform purchasing decisions.
Partner with Product Management, Sales, Demand Planning, and Purchasing to improve demand forecasts and replenishment strategies.
Monitor forecast accuracy and bias; recommend improvements to planning assumptions and parameters.
Coordinate returns with suppliers for slow moving SKUs to reduce excess inventory levels and improve turns.
Support new product introductions and product lifecycle planning to minimize inventory risk.


Working Capital & Financial Impact


Translate inventory performance into clear financial outcomes (cash usage, carrying cost, write-down risk)
Support Finance with inventory valuation analysis, reserves, and period-end reporting.
Build scenarios and business cases to support inventory reduction and service-level improvement initiatives.
Track and report progress against inventory, service, and working capital targets.


Cross-Functional Collaboration


Act as a data-driven partner to Product Management, Sales, Operations, Purchasing, and Finance.
Challenge demand and stocking assumptions using facts and analysis.
Support S&OP / IBP processes with inventory insights and recommendations.
Influence decision-making without direct authority.
Work with warehouse operations teams to root cause inventory discrepancies across the distribution network.
Coordinate promotional initiatives with Marketing to expedite the depletion of discontinued and slow-moving SKUs.
Works cross functionally with Product Managers and Vendor Compliance to strategize and resolve supplier issues relating to on-time shipping, responsiveness, and quality.


Continuous Improvement


Identify gaps and inefficiencies in inventory planning processes, data quality, and execution.
Support ERP, planning tool, or BI enhancements related to inventory management
Document best practices and standard operating procedures.
Develop process improvement plans across the operations teams to enhance the inventory management, planning tools, and reporting capabilities.


Competencies And Skills


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Supply Chain, Business, Finance, Economics, Engineering, or Analytics.
3–6+ years of experience in inventory analysis, demand planning, supply chain analytics, or operations finance.
Experience in distribution, manufacturing, or industrial environments with large SKU counts and demand variability.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate data into action
Excellent verbal and written communications skills are also required


Technical Skills


Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, lookups, scenario modeling; VBA a plus).
Experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Dynamics, Infor).
Strong reporting and visualization skills (Power BI, Tableau, or similar preferred).
Solid understanding of inventory planning concepts (service levels, safety stock, EOQ, lead times, forecast accuracy).


Critical Success Traits


Business mindset: understands inventory as cash, not just stock
Intellectual curiosity and strong root-cause thinking
Ability to influence without authority
Bias for action—moves from insight to recommendation
Strong attention to detail


EEO/AA Statement

Title: Analyst - Analytics Engineering - Toronto
Description:
Description

Location: Toronto Metropolitan Area

Base Salary: $95,000 – $105,000 (CAD)

Company Overview

Teragonia is the AI Operating System for Private Equity, helping sponsors and their portfolio companies turn fragmented operational data into decisions that move EBITDA. We operate in the highest-pressure segment of enterprise performance, where outcomes are time-bound by a 3 to 5 year hold period and execution velocity defines returns. Through Astradis™, our AI platform, we unify structured and unstructured data, generate prescriptive insights in near real time, and embed them directly into operating workflows with clear ownership and measurable impact . Teragonia's Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) Department Summary

Teragonia’s Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) division bridges finance, consulting, and technology to support portfolio companies. We combine consulting rigor and AI to address complex challenges and enhance enterprise value through platform solutions and advisory. We prioritize professional growth through mentorship and cross-functional learning.

The Astradis platform provides real-time intelligence through a function-specific command center, combining structured and unstructured data via our Fibron AI layer. Our platform powers diagnostic dashboards, forecasting models, pricing tools, and operations modules, enabling clients to transition from reactive reporting to predictive strategy.

Requirements

Job Summary

We are looking for a talented Analytics Engineer to join our team as an Analyst. As an Analyst, you will work closely with our team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions for our private equity-backed company clients. You will assist in understanding their business needs and developing tailored analytics solutions.

Responsibilities


Support a team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions and be an active contributor to executing on engagement work
Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in working closely with clients to understand their business needs and develop tailored analytics solutions
Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in translating Client communicated business needs into an exploratory data analysis program and lead team efforts to execute
Assist analytics engineering teams in designing, developing, maintaining, and optimizing complex enterprise data models to help solve operational and financial business problems
Develop and maintain high-quality, visually appealing reports and dashboards to communicate complex analytics insights and recommendations to senior client executives, ensuring that findings are easy to understand and actionable by senior executives
Make a concerted effort to begin to develop and maintain strong relationships with clients and internal stakeholders
Participate in and assist in project meetings and contribute to project deliverables
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and prioritize tasks to meet deadlines
Assist in developing and implementing process improvements to increase team efficiency and productivity
Collaborate with data engineers to design, develop, maintain, and optimize data pipelines critical to the integrity of the enterprise data models
Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in data analytics and apply this knowledge to improve our solutions


Overview

As an Analyst, you will be a key contributor to our service delivery, innovation, and practice development. You will have the opportunity, and the management support, to develop specialized knowledge of data analytics, data science, and AI applications in a chosen industry or domain. You will possess excellent communication and presentation skills and be eager to develop as a key contributor in the Value Creation and Analytics (VC&A) Consulting division and develop executive presence as your career with us progresses to Associate, Manager, Principal, Director, and Managing Director levels. We will provide appropriate mentorship and company selected resources for your leadership skill development.

Technical Skills

As an Analyst, you will be quick to understand the client’s business needs and execute on delivering data-driven solutions. You will play a direct role in driving engagement workflows, deliverables, and timelines, ensuring the quality of enterprise-grade data pipelines and business intelligence applications. These efforts aim to enhance pricing, customer segmentation, sales effectiveness, customer lifetime value, attrition management, workforce scheduling, and cost optimization. You will be actively contributing to and delivering the team’s insights around the ideation, design and development of various analytics, data science and generative AI products, working hand-in-hand with our software engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers. 

The ideal candidate is a proactive problem-solver who takes full ownership of delivering high-quality client work. This individual thrives on delving into technical details to ensure the engagement team successfully meets deliverables by employing analytical techniques to develop crucial business insights for our clients. You will have excellent attention to detail and project management skills, essential for working effectively within our diverse, multidisciplinary team. As part of a collaborative group that includes business analysts, software engineers, data engineers, analytics engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers, you must demonstrate strong teamwork abilities. Your role will require not only individual excellence but also seamless integration and cooperation with team members to achieve shared goals and deliver impactful results.

Transforming low-hygiene data sets to develop organized databases with high fidelity is a necessary part of the job. The ideal candidate is an expert in data wrangling skills and possesses excellent knowledge of the software tools used for service delivery, including data manipulation and data visualization software. You will develop deep expertise in the Teragonia technology stack, for which we will provide appropriate training resources to the extent such tools are new to you. 

Engagement and Client Management

You could be delivering multiple client engagements simultaneously, or a large and complex engagement, and the scope of the engagement could evolve based on changing client needs. The financial sponsor universe demands actionable insights in a relatively short timeframe. The ideal candidate will be excited about driving critical client deliverables and solving a variety of business problems in parallel, in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

The engagement planning and management are critical to maintaining high quality delivery and efficiency. You will actively support the Engagement Manager and Associate by delivering on the engagement plan and strategy and contribute your own ideas, asking questions, challenging the ideas and timelines presented to ensure that high quality client deliverables can be delivered in the timeframe our clients request them. You will also support the Engagement Manager and Associate with assessing the project management plan including resource allocation, tasks assignment, priorities, timelines, budget vs actual, fee updates, and more. 

You will have strong communication and presentation skills and be able to clearly articulate to the team, engagement leader(s) and the client, the business applications and impact of the data analytics assets delivered.

Other Key Responsibilities


Innovation – Support cross functional innovation initiatives
Specialization – Develop a sector or domain specialty
Recruitment – Actively contribute to Teragonia’s recruitment efforts to attract top talent
Marketing and business development – Assist executive leadership and officers with strategic marketing initiatives
Thought leadership - Assist with conducting research for thought leadership initiatives


Requirements

Necessary Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Industrial Engineering, or a related field with stellar academic performance from leading universities
2 years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, software engineering or a related field at global consulting firms, top financial services firms, or high-growth startups
Strong interest in learning how analytics insights drive business value
Advanced programming skills in SQL and Python
Experience in performing exploratory data analysis in a professional setting
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or PowerBI
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with cloud-based analytics platforms and big data technologies such as Snowflake, GCP, DBT, FiveTran, Dataiku, etc.
High business acumen and advanced understanding of connectivity between operational data and financial data (ERP CRM PoS HRIS P&L / trial balance / journal entries, etc.)
Experience with data modeling methodologies and concepts (e.g., Kimball Modeling, Data Vault, Data Mesh, etc.)
Strong experience with data modeling
Certification in data science or analytics (e.g., Certified Analytics Professional)
Master's in Business Analytics, Computer Science, or similar advanced degree


Benefits

Canadian Benefits


Competitive Total Compensation - Market-benchmarked salary and bonus structure to attract and retain top talent.
Additional Compensation - Performance-based incentives and signing bonus, as applicable.
Visa Sponsorship - May be available for eligible candidates to join our diverse team subject to applicable laws and regulations in Canada.
RRSP Plan with Employer Contributions - We help secure your future with matching RRSP contributions up to 6.0% of your annual base salary.
WSIB Coverage - Workplace Safety and Insurance Board coverage as required by Ontario law.
Professional Development Opportunities - We will sponsor company-selected training sessions, conferences, and certifications to foster your growth.
Meal Stipend - Reimbursement for lunch at the office, and dinner too if you are working past 7 pm, up to a limit.
Fitness-Wellness Stipend - For fitness & wellness programs, such as gym memberships, up to a limit.
Paid Time Off (PTO) - Eligible for 15 to 20 days PTO based on applicable level. Vacation pay is calculated as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Vacation time may be carried over up to a maximum allowable under ESA requirements.
Statutory Holidays - ESA statutory holidays as well as additional company holidays: New Year’s Day, Family Day, Good Friday, Victoria Day, Canada Day, Labour Day, Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, Boxing Day, Easter Monday, Civic Holiday, Remembrance Day
Comprehensive Healthcare, Vision, and Dental Insurance - We prioritize your health and wellness with insurance coverage through leading Canadian providers equivalent to or exceeding provincial requirements.
Fertility and family-building benefits through leading Canadian healthcare providers.
Flexible Benefits Plan - We provide the opportunity to manage your healthcare expenses efficiently through Canadian tax-advantaged benefit plans where available.
Mental Health Support - Access to mental health support through Canadian mental health providers covered under our healthcare plan.
Health Navigation Services - Expert assistance to help you navigate the complexities of the Canadian healthcare system.
Disability Insurance - We provide short term disability insurance through a leading Canadian provider covering up to 26 weeks at 60% of weekly earnings up to $1,500, exceeding Employment Insurance requirements.
Sick Days - 5 paid sick days annually that reset each year, as required under the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Additional unpaid sick leave available as per ESA requirements.
Leaves of Absence - Teragonia provides unpaid statutory leaves as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA), including pregnancy, parental, caregiver, and bereavement leave. Eligibility and duration follow provincial regulations. Additional unpaid leaves or flexible arrangements may be considered on a case-by-case basis.
Paid Parental Leave - Paid Parental Leave of 12 weeks after 6 months of service with the company.


Flexible Work Policy

We promote flexible work to support wellbeing and performance. Remote work is available though in-person collaboration is encouraged for planning and team connection. Toronto based employees have no mandated office days but are expected in person for key meetings. Employees outside Toronto may work remotely with occasional travel for events. Early career professionals with less than three years of experience should live within commuting distance to receive in person mentorship. Relocation support is available on a case-by-case basis.

Working Hours and ESA Exemption

This position is classified as a specialized professional role under O. Reg. 285/01, s. 2 of Ontario's Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA) and is therefore exempt from overtime pay requirements. While standard hours of work are expected to be approximately 40 hours per week, additional hours may be required to meet client deliverable sand business needs. Such expectations are reflected in the annual salary.

Canadian Experience

We welcome applications from all qualified candidates. Canadian work experience is not a requirement for this position.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Statement

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing a respectful, inclusive, harassment free workplace. We hire based on merit and qualifications in line with the Ontario Human Rights Code and Employment Standards Act. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds.

Accommodation For Disabilities

We are committed to dignity, independence, inclusion, and equal opportunity under the Ontario Human Rights Code and AODA. We provide reasonable accommodations during recruitment and employment. Employees needing support should contact their manager or Human Resources representative.

Retention Notice

In compliance with Ontario's employment standards, Teragonia retains copies of all publicly advertised job postings and associated application forms for a period of three years.

Recruiting Process

Our recruitment process is designed to ensure a thoughtful and comprehensive evaluation of candidates, as well as alignment with the team’s objectives and company values. Candidates are encouraged to ask questions and engage openly throughout. The process typically involves the following stages:


Initial Interviews with Managers – Two interviews focused on fit and goals
Technical Interviews with Principals – One or two sessions assessing skills and problem-solving
Final Interview with Founders – Discuss strategic alignment and long-term potential


AI Use Disclosure

We use artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to support the screening and assessment.

Accessibility in Recruitment

We are committed to accessibility. If you require accommodation during any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us in advance. We will support you in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).
