What We Offer

In addition to a competitive salary and a rewarding career where you can truly make a difference, we offer a comprehensive benefits package that meets the various needs of our diverse employees, including:


Minimum three (3) weeks of paid annual vacation days, increasing with years of service;
Four (4) paid personal days;
Defined benefit pension plan with OMERS, includes 100-per-cent employer matching;
Health and dental benefits, including a health spending account available upon your start date;
Employee and family assistance program;
Maternity and parental leave top up (93% of base salary);
Training and development programs including tuition reimbursement of $1500 per calendar year;
Fitness membership discount;


This job offers the opportunity to work from home as part of a hybrid work arrangement. This arrangement will allow you to work some days at a TCHC work location and the rest of the time from home. The amount of time required to work at a TCHC work location is flexible, while considering operational and service delivery requirements.

Make a difference

The Metrics and Reporting Analyst within Procurement is responsible for advancing procurement reporting, data governance, and performance measurement capabilities across sourcing, contracts, and supplier management systems.

This role ensures procurement data is accurate, standardized, and strategically leveraged to support compliance, financial stewardship, risk management, and sourcing effectiveness.

The position plays a key role in strengthening data integrity across procurement platforms while aligning reporting practices with enterprise data governance principles.

What You'll Do

Procurement Analytics & Performance Reporting


Design, develop, and maintain procurement dashboards and reporting solutions (Power BI and related tools)
Define and standardize procurement KPIs including sourcing cycle time, contract compliance, spend visibility, PO timeliness, and vendor performance
Produce recurring executive reporting packages (e.g., contract expiry monitoring, compliance dashboards, pipeline tracking)
Develop and maintain complex SQL queries and data models to extract and transform data from multiple procurement and financial systems
Automate recurring reporting processes to reduce manual intervention and improve data reliability
Provide analytical insights and trend analysis to support sourcing strategy and operational decision-making


Data Governance & Quality Management


Establish and monitor data quality standards across procurement systems (Yardi, CLM, e-Procurement platforms)
Conduct periodic audits of procurement data to ensure accuracy and policy compliance
Standardize sourcing templates, reporting definitions, and data entry protocols
Identify and resolve recurring data integrity issues
Support enterprise audit, compliance, and risk reporting requirements


Systems & Data Integration Support


Serve as subject matter expert for procurement reporting across multiple systems
Support configuration testing and validation of reporting outputs during system enhancements or implementations
Support structured data ingestion and transformation processes using SQL and automation tools
Document data logic, transformation rules, and reporting methodologies to ensure sustainability and scalability


Continuous Improvement & Modernization


Identify opportunities to improve automation, streamline dashboards, and enhance reporting efficiency
Recommend scalable reporting structures that support future BI modernization initiatives
Support evaluation of tools or approaches that improve procurement analytics capability
Contribute to building a sustainable, enterprise-aligned procurement data framework


What You’ll Need


Bachelor’s degree in business Analytics, Finance, Computer Science, Statistics, or related discipline
5+ years of experience in analytics, procurement reporting, finance, or related function
Experience working in multi-system data environments
Experience supporting system implementations or process improvement initiatives
Advanced SQL
Power BI (or equivalent BI platform)
Advanced Excel (including data modeling and automation)
Strong understanding of data governance principles


Nice To Have


Experience with procurement or financial systems (e.g., Yardi, CLM, e-Procurement platforms)
Experience supporting structured data ingestion or automation workflows (e.g., Python, Power Automate, or similar tools)
Understanding of data modeling concepts (fact/dimension structures)
Experience working with enterprise data or IT teams on reporting alignment


What’s Next

Once you apply, we’ll review your resume and contact you if we believe your skills and experience will make you successful in the role. If you are selected to move forward, the process will include one or more interviews and/or assessments and reference checks.

The amount of time required to work at a TCHC work location is flexible, while considering operational and service delivery requirements.

Make a difference

The Metrics and Reporting Analyst within Procurement is responsible for advancing procurement reporting, data governance, and performance measurement capabilities across sourcing, contracts, and supplier management systems.

This role ensures procurement data is accurate, standardized, and strategically leveraged to support compliance, financial stewardship, risk management, and sourcing effectiveness.

The position plays a key role in strengthening data integrity across procurement platforms while aligning reporting practices with enterprise data governance principles.

What You'll Do

Procurement Analytics & Performance Reporting

  • Design, develop, and maintain procurement dashboards and reporting solutions (Power BI and related tools)
  • Define and standardize procurement KPIs including sourcing cycle time, contract compliance, spend visibility, PO timeliness, and vendor performance
  • Produce recurring executive reporting packages (e.g., contract expiry monitoring, compliance dashboards, pipeline tracking)
  • Develop and maintain complex SQL queries and data models to extract and transform data from multiple procurement and financial systems
  • Automate recurring reporting processes to reduce manual intervention and improve data reliability
  • Provide analytical insights and trend analysis to support sourcing strategy and operational decision-making

Data Governance & Quality Management

  • Establish and monitor data quality standards across procurement systems (Yardi, CLM, e-Procurement platforms)
  • Conduct periodic audits of procurement data to ensure accuracy and policy compliance
  • Standardize sourcing templates, reporting definitions, and data entry protocols
  • Identify and resolve recurring data integrity issues
  • Support enterprise audit, compliance, and risk reporting requirements

Systems & Data Integration Support

  • Serve as subject matter expert for procurement reporting across multiple systems
  • Support configuration testing and validation of reporting outputs during system enhancements or implementations
  • Support structured data ingestion and transformation processes using SQL and automation tools
  • Document data logic, transformation rules, and reporting methodologies to ensure sustainability and scalability

Continuous Improvement & Modernization

  • Identify opportunities to improve automation, streamline dashboards, and enhance reporting efficiency
  • Recommend scalable reporting structures that support future BI modernization initiatives
  • Support evaluation of tools or approaches that improve procurement analytics capability
  • Contribute to building a sustainable, enterprise-aligned procurement data framework

What You’ll Need

  • Bachelor’s degree in business Analytics, Finance, Computer Science, Statistics, or related discipline
  • 5+ years of experience in analytics, procurement reporting, finance, or related function
  • Experience working in multi-system data environments
  • Experience supporting system implementations or process improvement initiatives
  • Advanced SQL
  • Power BI (or equivalent BI platform)
  • Advanced Excel (including data modeling and automation)
  • Strong understanding of data governance principles

Nice To Have

  • Experience with procurement or financial systems (e.g., Yardi, CLM, e-Procurement platforms)
  • Experience supporting structured data ingestion or automation workflows (e.g., Python, Power Automate, or similar tools)
  • Understanding of data modeling concepts (fact/dimension structures)
  • Experience working with enterprise data or IT teams on reporting alignment

What’s Next

Once you apply, we’ll review your resume and contact you if we believe your skills and experience will make you successful in the role. If you are selected to move forward, the process will include one or more interviews and/or assessments and reference checks.

INDS

Title: Data Analyst - Risk Control
Description:
Our employees are at the heart of everything we do. Together, we help people, businesses, and society prosper in good times and be resilient in bad times.

Our employee promise represents Intact’s commitment to you in exchange for living our Values, striving to do your best work, being open to change and investing in your career. In return, we promise to provide support, opportunities and performance-led financial rewards at a workplace where you can shape the future, win as a team and grow with us.

Pay at Intact is about much more than just salary.


Flexible work arrangements and a hybrid work model
Possibility to purchase up to 5 extra days off per year
Multiple benefits offered to support physical and mental wellbeing, including telemedicine, Wellness account and much more
Share plan & other savings: up to 12% of salary or even more (ask how you could earn guaranteed income for life)


Salary Range (but Not Limited To):

69,200 - 84,600

Annual Bonus Target, Based On The Base Salary, With a Potential Payout Of Up To double The Target (subject To Personal And Company Performance):

7.5%

As part of our commitment to Win As A Team, we share our success with employees through our annual bonus plan and Employee Share Purchase Plan (ESPP) – with Intact matching 50% of your net shares.

Our pension offerings provide flexibility and long-term security for our employees beyond their careers. We are one of the few companies offering the opportunity to receive guaranteed income for life via our defined benefit pension plan.

Salary for the candidate will be determined taking into consideration a number of factors including: experience, skills, qualifications, anticipated contribution to role, internal equity, etc. The salary range presented above is based on a 35-hour workweek and would represent a majority of different candidate profiles. However, we encourage candidates who may fall outside of this range to apply as well.

About The Role

We’re looking for a Risk Control Data Analyst (or Sr. Risk Control Data Analyst) to join our growing team. This is a high-impact analytics role for someone who wants to strengthen how Risk Control measures performance, demonstrates value, and reduces operational risk through reliable reporting and scalable data foundations.

You’ll combine strong technical skills (SQL, Power BI, data modeling) and business judgment to spot trends early, surface actionable insights, and help leaders make better decisions. You’ll partner closely with Risk Control and stakeholders across the organization to ensure data is trusted, consistent, and easy to use.

About The Role

As a Risk Control Data Analyst, you will provide advanced analytics and decision support across divisions and business units, with primary support to Risk Control. You will own and continuously improve KPI/scorecard reporting, deliver deep-dive and predictive analytics, and strengthen reporting governance.

A key part of the role is improving reliability—building documented, sustainable processes and backup capability for core reporting and executive decision support—so leaders have timely, accurate insights.

Key Responsibilities:

KPI Ownership, Scorecards & Monthly Reporting


Own production, accuracy, and continuous improvement of KPIs, scorecards, and monthly data compilation.
Create standardized definitions, documentation, and quality controls to ensure consistent reporting.
Establish redundancy/backup-ready processes to mitigate reporting continuity risk.


Power BI Development, Visualization & Governance


Design, build, and maintain Power BI dashboards with detailed visualizations and drill-through capability.
Maintain and enhance Power BI reporting across LC360 and workspace SharePoint environments.
Implement governance and reliability practices (dataset standards, refresh schedules, workspace organization, version control, monitoring).


Data Extraction, SQL & Data Modeling


Write and optimize SQL queries across multiple database sources to create analysis-ready datasets.
Build and maintain semantic models and curated datasets that enable self-service and consistent metrics.
Automate and streamline recurring reporting to reduce manual effort and improve timeliness.


Data Governance & Scalable “Data-First” Foundation (RC)


Improve RC data governance and infrastructure so data is structured, centralized, and scheduled.
Establish repeatable pipelines and documentation (data dictionaries, KPI logic, refresh cadence).
Enable a scalable and reliable data foundation for ongoing analytics and reporting growth.


Cross-Functional Partnership & Shared-Service Enablement


Partner with teams across CL and SL, Data Lab, Claims, and other business units to align methodologies.
Support a shared model covering both Corporate and RC priorities to maximize value across both areas.


Strategic Initiatives, ROI & Value Proposition Measurement


Support national initiatives by providing data strategy, ROI measurement, and loss savings/mitigation estimation.
Build measurement frameworks and analytics for initiatives such as Intelliroute, Farm Sentinel, snow removal program, mass prevention campaigns, emerging loss trending, extreme weather targeting, EB loss analysis, etc.
Monitor (near real-time where feasible) RC value-added benefits such as pre-bind value-add, loss avoidance, risk alerts (Level 1), and premium leakage to support RC value proposition reporting.


Required Qualifications:


Post secondary education in Data Analytics
Minimum 3 years experience delivering analytics, insights, and recommendations for leaders – is required.
Advanced SQL skills; ability to integrate data from multiple sources.
Advanced Power BI skills (data modeling, DAX, visualization, publishing, governance, refresh management).
Strong communication skills, including presenting to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Proven ability to own recurring reporting processes with high reliability and documented standards.


Preferred Qualifications:


Experience with predictive analytics, forecasting, and building leading indicators.
Experience in insurance, claims, risk control, loss prevention, or operational performance analytics.
Experience with data governance practices and building centralized, standardized datasets.
Experience partnering with analytics Data Labs and cross-functional business units.
For candidates located in Quebec, bilingualism is required considering the necessity to interact on a regular basis with English-speaking colleagues across the country.


Core Competencies:


Analytical rigor and business judgment
Strong stakeholder management and collaboration
Clear, executive-ready communication and data storytelling
Ownership mindset and operational reliability focus
Continuous improvement orientation (automation, standardization, governance)

Title: Reporting Analyst
Description:
About Us

For over 50 years, our Property and Casualty insurance Company, CAA Insurance, has provided dependable and trustworthy service to protect Canadians and what matters to them. CAA Insurance Company offers a dynamic professional environment which strives to offer the ultimate customer experience. We share in each other’s successes, drive change and support each other while cultivating a great working environment to deliver on what is best for the customer.

Who we are

At CCG, we are committed to delivering an exceptional Associate experience. We offer:

Work-life harmony with access to an award-winning holistic wellness program,

Continuous learning through our robust corporate curriculum and education reimbursement program,

Incredible rewards, travel incentives, and product and service discounts,

Pay-for-performance and best-in-class recognition programs, and

Competitive benefits that include a defined contribution plan, personal spending account, and so much more.

Join our growing team where everyone belongs!

Position Details

What You Will Do


Analyze insurance performance historical data to understand the reasons for past performance and make recommendations that will influence the business growth and/or address portfolio / business concerns
Conduct regular, ongoing reviews on the sales performance measurements for each distribution channels, as well as portfolio analysis to assess quality of business being written
Recommend changes or adherence to prudent underwriting guidelines and practices
Respond to business needs by identifying appropriate information to analyze utilizing a variety of sources in data mining
Identify, understand, and communicate profitability drivers while collaborating with multi distribution channels, claims and accounting areas
Find ways to automate reports based on user requests or by observing frequency of ad-hoc reports being requested
Identify opportunities and responding to changes to ensure adherence to the corporate business success strategy
Communicate and consult proposed changes in underwriting rules, guidelines, practices and gaps based on findings
Provide Insurance field trend analysis, forecasts reports and findings that anticipate outcome
Ability to utilize EDW data / GW data, industry information and trend analysis to provide management recommendations
Provide detailed analysis of statistics that are outside of anticipated norms


Please note, salary range is not inclusive of total compensation. The expected salary range for this position will be impacted by factors such as the successful candidate’s skills, experience and working location, as well as the specific position’s business line, scope and level. Candidates with salary expectations outside of the range are encouraged to apply.

Who You Are


University Degree in Mathematics, Economics or Actuarial Science
3-5 years data reporting experience in the financial services industry. P&C Insurance industry experience is a definite asset.
Forecast reporting, data collection, data analysis, report generation and quality assurance experience is required
Professional designation preferred but not required: CIP/FCIP, CA. CMA. CPA, CGA or equivalent
Database Administration experience is a preferred asset
Working experience with MS Office products, SQL or database query tools including VBM and VBA is an asset
Power BI and Python experience are required
Excellent interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills
Excellent problem solver, detail oriented, ability to work in a team environment as well as work independently
Strong analytical and decision-making skills


Please note that internal applicants are asked to formally express their interest by applying online no later than 10 business days of the position being posted

Please note that we may use AI tools to help us through the recruitment process.

Our Commitment

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing employment accommodation in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005 (AODA). CAA CCG will provide accommodations to job applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. If you require an accommodation, please notify us and we will work with you to meet your needs.

Title: Inventory Analyst, Canada
Description:
Global Industrial

About Global Industrial

Global Industrial Company (NYSE: GIC) is a leading distributor of high-quality, industrial-strength equipment and supplies, serving organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. With more than 75 years of experience, customers rely on us for our broad portfolio of national and private brands, trusted service, and strong value proposition.

We help customers keep their operations running by delivering the right products when they need them—because We Can Supply That™.

Why Join Global Industrial

At Global Industrial, we’re One Team committed to delivering value and empowering our associates to grow. Our culture is built on:


Meritocracy & Pay for Performance – Your impact drives your rewards
High Say-Do Ratio – We follow through on our commitments
Career Growth & Internal Mobility – We invest in your future
Collaboration & Accountability – One team delivering real results


Make an impact. Be recognized. Grow your career with us.

Global Industrial — Delivering Value Starts Here

Key Responsibilities

Inventory Analysis & Optimization


Analyze inventory levels, turns, aging, and demand patterns across products, locations, and suppliers.
Identify surplus, low-velocity, and at-risk inventory and quantify financial impact to working capital and margin.
Develop and maintain inventory KPIs and dashboards (e.g., turns, days of supply, fill rate, aging, forecast accuracy).
Perform root-cause analysis on inventory imbalances, including forecast error, lead time assumptions, MOQ constraints, and demand variability.


Forecasting & Replenishment Enablement


Review inventory levels and replenishment signals using cloud-based planning tools, translating insights into data-driven recommendations that inform purchasing decisions.
Partner with Product Management, Sales, Demand Planning, and Purchasing to improve demand forecasts and replenishment strategies.
Monitor forecast accuracy and bias; recommend improvements to planning assumptions and parameters.
Coordinate returns with suppliers for slow moving SKUs to reduce excess inventory levels and improve turns.
Support new product introductions and product lifecycle planning to minimize inventory risk.


Working Capital & Financial Impact


Translate inventory performance into clear financial outcomes (cash usage, carrying cost, write-down risk)
Support Finance with inventory valuation analysis, reserves, and period-end reporting.
Build scenarios and business cases to support inventory reduction and service-level improvement initiatives.
Track and report progress against inventory, service, and working capital targets.


Cross-Functional Collaboration


Act as a data-driven partner to Product Management, Sales, Operations, Purchasing, and Finance.
Challenge demand and stocking assumptions using facts and analysis.
Support S&OP / IBP processes with inventory insights and recommendations.
Influence decision-making without direct authority.
Work with warehouse operations teams to root cause inventory discrepancies across the distribution network.
Coordinate promotional initiatives with Marketing to expedite the depletion of discontinued and slow-moving SKUs.
Works cross functionally with Product Managers and Vendor Compliance to strategize and resolve supplier issues relating to on-time shipping, responsiveness, and quality.


Continuous Improvement


Identify gaps and inefficiencies in inventory planning processes, data quality, and execution.
Support ERP, planning tool, or BI enhancements related to inventory management
Document best practices and standard operating procedures.
Develop process improvement plans across the operations teams to enhance the inventory management, planning tools, and reporting capabilities.


Competencies And Skills


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Supply Chain, Business, Finance, Economics, Engineering, or Analytics.
3–6+ years of experience in inventory analysis, demand planning, supply chain analytics, or operations finance.
Experience in distribution, manufacturing, or industrial environments with large SKU counts and demand variability.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate data into action
Excellent verbal and written communications skills are also required


Technical Skills


Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, lookups, scenario modeling; VBA a plus).
Experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Dynamics, Infor).
Strong reporting and visualization skills (Power BI, Tableau, or similar preferred).
Solid understanding of inventory planning concepts (service levels, safety stock, EOQ, lead times, forecast accuracy).


Critical Success Traits


Business mindset: understands inventory as cash, not just stock
Intellectual curiosity and strong root-cause thinking
Ability to influence without authority
Bias for action—moves from insight to recommendation
Strong attention to detail


EEO/AA Statement

  • Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, lookups, scenario modeling; VBA a plus).
  • Experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Dynamics, Infor).
  • Strong reporting and visualization skills (Power BI, Tableau, or similar preferred).
  • Solid understanding of inventory planning concepts (service levels, safety stock, EOQ, lead times, forecast accuracy).

Critical Success Traits

  • Business mindset: understands inventory as cash, not just stock
  • Intellectual curiosity and strong root-cause thinking
  • Ability to influence without authority
  • Bias for action—moves from insight to recommendation
  • Strong attention to detail

EEO/AA Statement

Title: RevOps Analyst (Analytics)
Description:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

RevOps Analyst (Analytics)

Department: Revenue Operations

Function: GTM Analyst, Analytics

Location: NYC, SF, Toronto

The Revenue Operations team’s mission is to build and maintain the systems, data, and processes that power our go-to-market engine. We are looking for a RevOps Analyst, Analytics to join the team, reporting to the Head of Revenue Operations, focused on partnering with the business to deliver insight to drive measurable revenue growth and operational impact.

In This Role You May


Partner with GTM leadership to elevate reporting, systems and process to drive revenue growth and decision making
Manage, and enhance our quote-to-cash processes to ensure accuracy and compliance
Collaborate cross-functionally to enable the Revenue team to be able to see a 360 degree view of the customer in the CRM, including product usage, customer engagement (support tickets, sales emails, marketing campaigns, attribution) and contract data to drive revenue growth and improve sales productivity
Drive initiative to improve data hygiene, reporting accuracy and CRM governance
Manage urgent ad hoc operational requests


This Role Is a Good Match If You


Have 3+ years of experience in sales operations or a related role
Have Salesforce experience; CPQ Experience and Admin certification plus
Have familiarity with GTM tech stacks and how they support revenue workflows (CRM, enrichment, engagement, automation, analytics)
Bring 3+ years of big data experience: using BI tools such as Looker or Tableau and/or writing complex yet efficient SQL is a daily habit for you
Have experience getting your hands dirty with ETL and familiar with popular data warehouses (BigQuery, Snowflake, etc)
Are an excellent communicator with senior management audiences - explaining “what it is”, “why it matters” and “what should we do” in terms of your data and analysis; delivering customer-oriented KPIs and dashboards that drive alignment


If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees At Cohere Enjoy These Perks

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Clutch

Clutch is Canada’s largest online used car retailer, delivering a seamless, hassle-free car-buying experience to drivers everywhere. Customers can browse hundreds of cars from the comfort of their home, get the right one delivered to their door, and enjoy peace of mind with our 10-Day Money-Back Guarantee.

Named one of Canada’s Top Growing Companies two years in a row and awarded a spot on LinkedIn’s Top Canadian Startups list, we’re looking to add curious, hard-working, and driven individuals to our growing team.

Headquartered in Toronto, Clutch was founded in 2017. Clutch is backed by world-class investors including Canaan, BrandProject, Real Ventures, D1 Capital, and Upper90. To learn more, visit clutch.ca

About The Role

Clutch is seeking a Data Analytics / Analytics Engineering professional to build and maintain our analytics data stack, deliver reliable insights, and empower teams across the company. You’ll play a key role in defining metrics, building scalable data models, and enabling self-serve analytics for multiple business functions.

What You’ll Do


Build, maintain, and optimize analytics data stack, including ELT pipelines, dimensional models, and semantic layers using dbt, Snowflake, and Airflow
Collaborate with Marketing, Product, Operations, and Finance teams to define key metrics, improve reporting fidelity, and enable self-serve analytics
Own and evolve core data models and pipelines that drive dashboards, reporting, and downstream analysis
Apply best practices for data testing, observability, and CI/CD to ensure reliable, version-controlled, production-ready analytics code
Partner with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into performant, trustworthy data products
Contribute to internal data documentation and governance practices
Mentor team members on data modeling, tooling, and processes, bringing a scalable, engineering-first approach to analytics


What We’re Looking For


4–6 years of experience in analytics engineering, data analytics, or data engineering, ideally in fast-paced or high-growth environments
Strong SQL expertise and experience with large-scale, normalized, or dimensional datasets
Hands-on experience building data models with dbt in a production environment (CI, testing, documentation)
Experience with cloud data warehouses like Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift
Experience building semantic layers and enabling self-serve BI (Looker, Sigma, Tableau)
Proactive communicator able to collaborate with business stakeholders and technical teams
Strong ownership and accountability for data quality, performance, and maintainability
Familiarity with Python and orchestration tools (Airflow, Prefect, Dagster) for data pipelines is a plus


Why you’ll love it at Clutch


Autonomy & ownership – create your own path and own your work
Competitive compensation and equity incentives
Generous time off program
Health & dental benefits


Employment Type

Existing, Permanent Full-Time

Compensation & Benefits


Salary: $110,000 - $140,000 annually
Equity incentives
Comprehensive healthcare, dental, vision, prescriptions, life insurance, and dependent coverage
Pay decisions reflect skills, performance, market benchmarking, responsibilities, and working conditions


Clutch is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where all individuals have an opportunity to succeed. If you require accommodation at any stage of the interview process, please email talent@clutch.ca

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Job Summary

The Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst for the Specialty Reporting Services team will turn data into information, information into insight and insight into business decisions. The team member will have the ability to develop complex and appealing visualizations using a variety of BI Tools based on tightly established client turnaround times.

Duties And Responsibilities


Implementation of new reporting processes and procedures as required such as moving to automated reporting methods
Analyze reports for trends and variances across the programs and communicate findings
Design and prepare monthly, bimonthly or quarterly business reports including metrics/volumes and significant case summaries to clients using a variety of tools, including; Excel, Power BI, SSRS, PowerPoint
Convert data to usable information to drive growth, individual contract KPI’s and increased productivity
Act as the subject matter expert for specific Bayshore Specialty Rx Ltd data sets and act as a liaison with internal management and clients as required
Test and debug reporting and analysis using standard tools including but not limited to Excel,


Access, CRM and other database management tools/systems


Interpret data and develop actionable recommendations for business leaders
Identify, analyze, and interpret trends or patterns in complex data sets
Locate and define new process improvement opportunities
Ability to respond to customer inquiries and reporting needs efficiently as well as adapt to rapidly evolving client requirements
Knowledge of SQL queries to acquire data from primary and secondary data sources is desirable
Formulating and communicating meaningful and actionable process plans
Participate in ongoing internal and/or external continuing education activities
Adhere to Bayshore Policies and Procedure
Participate in quality activities and continuous improvement initiatives in keeping with the company’s Quality Management System
Participates in proactive Health & Safety activities while performing all duties. Is responsible to notify immediate Supervisor of any health and safety risks or concerns. Responsible for completing Accident Reports for all direct reports who injure themselves on the job, within 24 hours of the incident.
Maintains confidentiality of client and corporate information and discusses same only with appropriate Bayshore personnel


Qualifications

EDUCATION


Bachelor’s degree in information technology, statistics or data analysis


Experience


Expert level Power BI, Excel, and Dashboarding Skills
Minimum 2 years of experience working with complex data sets
Minimum 2 years of experience in analyzing and presenting results to stakeholders and clients
Minimum 2 years of experience with SQL and VBA is an asset
Data mining and in-depth knowledge of Data Science techniques is an assent
Experience working with pharmaceutical data is an asset


Other Skills And Abilities


Strong written and verbal communication skills


Primary Location

ON-Mississauga

Title: Analyst - Operational Reporting Enablement
Description:
At Allstate, great things happen when our people work together to protect families and their belongings from life’s uncertainties. And for more than 90 years, our innovative drive has kept us a step ahead of our customers’ evolving needs. From advocating for seat belts, air bags and graduated driving laws, to being an industry leader in pricing sophistication, telematics, and, more recently, device and identity protection.

Job Description

Who is Allstate:

Allstate Insurance Company of Canada is a leading home and auto insurer focused on providing its customers prevention and protection products and services for every stage of life. Serving Canadians since 1953, Allstate strives to reassure both customers and employees with its “You’re in Good Hands®” promise and is proud to have been named a Best Employer in Canada for nine consecutive years. Allstate is committed to making a positive difference in the communities in which it operates through partnerships with charitable organizations, employee giving and volunteerism. To learn more, visit www.allstate.ca. For safety tips and advice, visit www.goodhandsadvice.ca.

Through our Employee Value Proposition, we have worked hard to develop and nurture a culture where employees feel valued, experience personal growth, have career options and truly enjoy the work they do.

Role Designation: Hybrid with a requirement to go into our office based on business need.

Benefits To Joining Allstate


Flexible Work Arrangements
Employee discounts (15% on auto and property insurance, plus many other products and services)
Good Office program (receive up to 400$ back after purchasing office equipment)
Student Loan Payment Matching Program for Government Student loans
Comprehensive Retirement Savings Program with employer matched contributions
Annual Wellness allowance to support employees with improving health and wellbeing
Personal reflection day
Tuition Reimbursement
Working within the community and giving back!


Job description:

Allstate Canada Group is currently going through an important transformation of its Data environment with the implementation of a new Policy, Claims and Billing systems while continuing to support legacy platforms. We are growing our team and are actively looking to hire an Analyst - Operational Reporting Enablement to join our team!

Reporting to the Manager, Operational Reporting Enablement, you will be providing key technical expertise to acquire, investigate, manipulate and analyze data to support Allstate Canada Group’s business plans and results. The candidate will require an in-depth understanding of the data structure and environment and understand the implications from the day-to-day insurance business. This role enables accurate, traceable, and auditable data and analytics to support ongoing, ad‑hoc, and go‑live reporting needs for internal and external stakeholders. The candidate will require an in-depth understanding of the data structure and environment as well as the implications of insurance business processes on regulatory reporting outcomes. The candidate will also foster efficiency, bring forward creative solutions, and create awareness of opportunities to strengthen data quality and enablement across reporting initiatives. A proactive approach, strong collaboration skills, and the ability to think beyond standard approaches are expected.

Accountabilities:


Interpret the data from the user interface of the different source systems and master how it translates into the backend database
Build strong relationships with Claims, Policy and Finance teams to enable their needs in operational reporting and analytics needs
Help design and provide requirements for new or enhanced operational reporting data, analytics, and supporting reporting solutions
Monitor and ensure data quality of curated operational reporting data sets using appropriate validation, reconciliation, and audit‑support methods
Be heavily involved in testing of newly integrated data sources and/or environments
Develop and improve SAS/SQL programs to perform various complex analyses
Analyze change requests in the source systems or projects, assess their impact to operational reporting data layers and downstream reporting
Collaborate with DBAs, other IT teams, Architecture as required
Implement internal data process improvement: automating manual processes, optimizing data delivery, redesign/restructure database for more efficiency
Develop expertise and aim to become a subject matter expert on the entire data and analytics ecosystem
Develops a solid tactical understating of how the business works, what their operational processes look like, and provides directions as to where data and analytics could be leveraged to make more effective data-driven decisions.
Understands data visualization and develops solutions using tools such as Power BI in order to effectively communicate and support the translation of data and analytics insights in business terms to key stakeholders.


Qualifications:


Demonstrated experience applying analytical techniques to solve business problems and support data-driven decision making, with a strong focus on accuracy and reliability
Strong business acumen and the ability to communicate insights in both technical and non-technical terms.
Skilled in data visualization tools - Power BI or Tableau
Proactive, curious mindset with the ability to quickly learn and navigate new data models and evolving environments.
Hands-on experience with modern analytics ecosystems and methodologies (e.g., data lakes, data warehouses, data marts, ETL processes, data modeling, and data governance).
Proficiency in SQL/SAS programming for data development, cleansing, validation, integrity checks, reconciliation, and process optimization.
Skilled in MS Office tools, including Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and VBA.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities, with strong communication, collaboration, and stakeholder management skills; detail‑oriented with a disciplined approach to quality, controls, and documentation


Bonus Qualifications:


Proficiency in Microsoft Fabric and Python programming - understand data ingestion, data transformation, and data modeling


Allstate Canada Group has policies and practices that provide workplace accommodations. If you require accommodation, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.

À propos d’Allstate :

Allstate du Canada, compagnie d’assurance est un chef de file dans le domaine des assurances automobile et habitation; elle offre à ses clients des produits et services de prévention et de protection qui conviennent à toutes les étapes de la vie. Au service des Canadiens depuis 1953, l’entreprise met tout en œuvre pour que non seulement ses clients, mais aussi ses employés soient en bonnes mainsMD, comme en fait foi sa présence au palmarès des Employeurs de choix au Canada pendant neuf années de suite. Allstate du Canada tient à contribuer au mieux-être des communautés dans lesquelles elle exerce ses activités au moyen de partenariats avec des organismes caritatifs et de programmes misant sur la générosité de ses employés. Pour en savoir plus, visitez le www.allstate.ca. Pour connaître nos conseils en matière de sécurité, consultez le blogue Allstate Assurance au blogue.allstate.ca.

À Allstate, nous avons conscience que la qualité de nos produits découle directement du niveau de satisfaction du personnel à l’égard de l’entreprise. C’est pourquoi nous misons sur le maintien d’une culture où les employés se sentent valorisés et où ils peuvent poursuivre une carrière passionnante. Notre proposition de valeur aux employés permet ainsi de s’épanouir de façon unique.

Désignation du rôle : hybride, avec l’exigence de se rendre au bureau selon les besoins

Les avantages offerts par Allstate


La flexibilité des modalités de travail;
Des rabais d’employé (15 % sur les assurances automobile et habitation, ainsi que des rabais sur de nombreux autres produits et services);
Le programme Bureau en bonnes mains (jusqu’à 400 $ sont octroyés pour se procurer de l’équipement de bureau nécessaire à domicile);
Un programme de paiement de contrepartie pour le remboursement des prêts étudiants aux programmes gouvernementaux;
Un régime d’épargne-retraite complet prévoyant des cotisations de contrepartie de l’employeur;
Une allocation annuelle couvrant des dépenses effectuées pour améliorer sa santé et son bien-être;
Un Jour de réflexion personnelle;
Le remboursement de droits de scolarité;
Une implication dans la vie communautaire.


Description du poste :

En plus de continuer à soutenir ses plateformes existantes, le Groupe Allstate du Canada est en plein processus de transformation importante de son environnement de données dans le cadre de l’implantation de nouveaux systèmes de traitement des polices, des réclamations et de la facturation. Notre équipe, dès lors en plein essor, est à la recherche d’un ou d’une analyste - optimisation orientée rapports réglementaires!

Relevant de la directrice, optimisation orientée rapports réglementaires, le ou la titulaire de ce poste mettra à profit ses compétences techniques pour acquérir, manipuler et analyser des données, ainsi qu’enquêter sur celles-ci, afin de soutenir la production de rapports liés à la réglementation du Groupe Allstate du Canada. Le ou la candidat·e devra posséder une compréhension approfondie de la structure et de l’environnement des données ainsi que de l’incidence des processus d’assurance sur les résultats des rapports liés à la réglementation. Elle s’occupera de l’optimisation et de la mise en capacité de données et d’analyses caractérisées par leur exactitude, leur traçabilité et la possibilité de les vérifier afin de soutenir les besoins de production de rapports continus, ponctuels et de lancement pour les parties prenantes internes et externes. Il ou elle favorisera l’efficacité, proposera des solutions créatives et sensibilisera autrui aux occasions de renforcer la qualité des données et leur mise en œuvre dans le cadre des projets liés aux rapports. Une approche proactive, de solides compétences en collaboration et la capacité de penser au-delà des approches standard sont attendues.

Responsabilités :


Interpréter les données provenant de l’interface utilisateur de nos systèmes sources et très bien comprendre leurs effets sur notre structure de la base de données.
Établir des relations solides avec les équipes des réclamations, du traitement des polices et des finances afin d’optimiser et de mettre en capacité des rapports réglementaires conformes et de répondre aux besoins en matière d’analytique.
Aider à concevoir et à fournir les exigences en vue de l’optimisation et de la mise en capacité des données opérationnelles nouvelles ou améliorées des rapports et des solutions visant les rapports de soutien.
Surveiller et assurer la qualité des ensembles de données de rapports réglementaires sélectionnés en utilisant des méthodes appropriées de validation, de rapprochement et de soutien à l’audit.
Participer très activement à la mise à l’épreuve de sources ou d’environnements de données nouvellement intégrés.
Développer et améliorer les programmes SAS/SQL pour effectuer diverses analyses complexes.
Analyser les demandes de changement dans les systèmes sources ou les projets et évaluer leurs répercussions sur les couches de données de rapports réglementaires et en aval.
Collaborer avec les administrateurs de bases de données, d’autres équipes des Technologies et des équipes des services d’architecture, au besoin.
Améliorer les processus internes relatifs aux données et les rendre plus efficaces par le biais de l’automatisation des processus manuels, de l’optimisation de la diffusion des données, ainsi que la refonte/restructuration de la base de données.
Développer une expertise dans l’écosystème entier des données et de l’analytique et viser à devenir un expert ou une experte en la matière.
Développer une solide compréhension tactique du fonctionnement de l’entreprise, de ses processus opérationnels et fournir une orientation au sujet de l’endroit où les données et les analyses pourraient être mises à profit pour prendre des décisions plus efficaces fondées sur les données.
Comprendre la visualisation des données et élaborer des solutions à l’aide d’outils comme Power BI afin de communiquer efficacement et d’appuyer la traduction des données et de l’analytique en termes opérationnels aux intervenants clés.


Exigences :


Expérience démontrée dans l’application de techniques analytiques pour résoudre des problèmes opérationnels et soutenir la prise de décision basée sur les données, en accordant une forte priorité à l’exactitude et à la fiabilité;
Solide sens des affaires et capacité à communiquer des analyses à la fois en termes techniques et non techniques;
Compétence en outils de visualisation de données tels que Power BI et Tableau;
Esprit proactif et curieux avec la capacité d’apprendre rapidement et de naviguer dans de nouveaux modèles de données et des environnements en évolution;
Expérience pratique avec les écosystèmes et méthodologies analytiques modernes (par exemple, lacs de données, entrepôts de données, magasins de données, processus d’extraction, de transformation et de chargement de données, modélisation des données et gouvernance des données);
Maîtrise de la programmation SAS/SQL pour le développement des données, le nettoyage, la validation, les contrôles d’intégrité, la réconciliation et l’optimisation des processus;

Job Description

Who is Allstate:

Allstate Insurance Company of Canada is a leading home and auto insurer focused on providing its customers prevention and protection products and services for every stage of life. Serving Canadians since 1953, Allstate strives to reassure both customers and employees with its “You’re in Good Hands®” promise and is proud to have been named a Best Employer in Canada for nine consecutive years. Job description:

Allstate Canada Group is currently going through an important transformation of its Data environment with the implementation of a new Policy, Claims and Billing systems while continuing to support legacy platforms. We are growing our team and are actively looking to hire an Analyst - Operational Reporting Enablement to join our team!

Reporting to the Manager, Operational Reporting Enablement, you will be providing key technical expertise to acquire, investigate, manipulate and analyze data to support Allstate Canada Group’s business plans and results. The candidate will require an in-depth understanding of the data structure and environment and understand the implications from the day-to-day insurance business. This role enables accurate, traceable, and auditable data and analytics to support ongoing, ad‑hoc, and go‑live reporting needs for internal and external stakeholders. The candidate will require an in-depth understanding of the data structure and environment as well as the implications of insurance business processes on regulatory reporting outcomes. The candidate will also foster efficiency, bring forward creative solutions, and create awareness of opportunities to strengthen data quality and enablement across reporting initiatives. A proactive approach, strong collaboration skills, and the ability to think beyond standard approaches are expected.

Accountabilities:

  • Interpret the data from the user interface of the different source systems and master how it translates into the backend database
  • Build strong relationships with Claims, Policy and Finance teams to enable their needs in operational reporting and analytics needs
  • Help design and provide requirements for new or enhanced operational reporting data, analytics, and supporting reporting solutions
  • Monitor and ensure data quality of curated operational reporting data sets using appropriate validation, reconciliation, and audit‑support methods
  • Be heavily involved in testing of newly integrated data sources and/or environments
  • Develop and improve SAS/SQL programs to perform various complex analyses
  • Analyze change requests in the source systems or projects, assess their impact to operational reporting data layers and downstream reporting
  • Collaborate with DBAs, other IT teams, Architecture as required
  • Implement internal data process improvement: automating manual processes, optimizing data delivery, redesign/restructure database for more efficiency
  • Develop expertise and aim to become a subject matter expert on the entire data and analytics ecosystem
  • Develops a solid tactical understating of how the business works, what their operational processes look like, and provides directions as to where data and analytics could be leveraged to make more effective data-driven decisions.
  • Understands data visualization and develops solutions using tools such as Power BI in order to effectively communicate and support the translation of data and analytics insights in business terms to key stakeholders.

Qualifications:

  • Demonstrated experience applying analytical techniques to solve business problems and support data-driven decision making, with a strong focus on accuracy and reliability
  • Strong business acumen and the ability to communicate insights in both technical and non-technical terms.
  • Skilled in data visualization tools - Power BI or Tableau
  • Proactive, curious mindset with the ability to quickly learn and navigate new data models and evolving environments.
  • Hands-on experience with modern analytics ecosystems and methodologies (e.g., data lakes, data warehouses, data marts, ETL processes, data modeling, and data governance).
  • Proficiency in SQL/SAS programming for data development, cleansing, validation, integrity checks, reconciliation, and process optimization.
  • Skilled in MS Office tools, including Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and VBA.
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities, with strong communication, collaboration, and stakeholder management skills; detail‑oriented with a disciplined approach to quality, controls, and documentation

Bonus Qualifications:

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Fabric and Python programming - understand data ingestion, data transformation, and data modeling

Allstate du Canada, compagnie d’assurance est un chef de file dans le domaine des assurances automobile et habitation; elle offre à ses clients des produits et services de prévention et de protection qui conviennent à toutes les étapes de la vie. À Allstate, nous avons conscience que la qualité de nos produits découle directement du niveau de satisfaction du personnel à l’égard de l’entreprise. Notre proposition de valeur aux employés permet ainsi de s’épanouir de façon unique.

Les avantages offerts par Allstate

Description du poste :

En plus de continuer à soutenir ses plateformes existantes, le Groupe Allstate du Canada est en plein processus de transformation importante de son environnement de données dans le cadre de l’implantation de nouveaux systèmes de traitement des polices, des réclamations et de la facturation. Notre équipe, dès lors en plein essor, est à la recherche d’un ou d’une analyste - optimisation orientée rapports réglementaires!

Relevant de la directrice, optimisation orientée rapports réglementaires, le ou la titulaire de ce poste mettra à profit ses compétences techniques pour acquérir, manipuler et analyser des données, ainsi qu’enquêter sur celles-ci, afin de soutenir la production de rapports liés à la réglementation du Groupe Allstate du Canada. Le ou la candidat·e devra posséder une compréhension approfondie de la structure et de l’environnement des données ainsi que de l’incidence des processus d’assurance sur les résultats des rapports liés à la réglementation. Elle s’occupera de l’optimisation et de la mise en capacité de données et d’analyses caractérisées par leur exactitude, leur traçabilité et la possibilité de les vérifier afin de soutenir les besoins de production de rapports continus, ponctuels et de lancement pour les parties prenantes internes et externes. Il ou elle favorisera l’efficacité, proposera des solutions créatives et sensibilisera autrui aux occasions de renforcer la qualité des données et leur mise en œuvre dans le cadre des projets liés aux rapports. Une approche proactive, de solides compétences en collaboration et la capacité de penser au-delà des approches standard sont attendues.

Responsabilités :

  • Interpréter les données provenant de l’interface utilisateur de nos systèmes sources et très bien comprendre leurs effets sur notre structure de la base de données.

  • Aider à concevoir et à fournir les exigences en vue de l’optimisation et de la mise en capacité des données opérationnelles nouvelles ou améliorées des rapports et des solutions visant les rapports de soutien.
  • Surveiller et assurer la qualité des ensembles de données de rapports réglementaires sélectionnés en utilisant des méthodes appropriées de validation, de rapprochement et de soutien à l’audit.
  • Participer très activement à la mise à l’épreuve de sources ou d’environnements de données nouvellement intégrés.
  • Développer et améliorer les programmes SAS/SQL pour effectuer diverses analyses complexes.
  • Analyser les demandes de changement dans les systèmes sources ou les projets et évaluer leurs répercussions sur les couches de données de rapports réglementaires et en aval.
  • Collaborer avec les administrateurs de bases de données, d’autres équipes des Technologies et des équipes des services d’architecture, au besoin.

  • Comprendre la visualisation des données et élaborer des solutions à l’aide d’outils comme Power BI afin de communiquer efficacement et d’appuyer la traduction des données et de l’analytique en termes opérationnels aux intervenants clés.

Exigences :

Title: Analyst - Analytics Engineering - Toronto
Description:
Description

Location: Toronto Metropolitan Area

Base Salary: $95,000 – $105,000 (CAD)

Company Overview

Teragonia is the AI Operating System for Private Equity, helping sponsors and their portfolio companies turn fragmented operational data into decisions that move EBITDA. We operate in the highest-pressure segment of enterprise performance, where outcomes are time-bound by a 3 to 5 year hold period and execution velocity defines returns. Through Astradis™, our AI platform, we unify structured and unstructured data, generate prescriptive insights in near real time, and embed them directly into operating workflows with clear ownership and measurable impact . We partner with PE firms managing more than US$400B in assets and are rapidly expanding globally.

Our work is measured in EBITDA outcomes. Across PE portfolio companies, we drive 3% to 5% EBITDA uplift, accelerate roll-ups through standardized data and KPI frameworks, and maintain continuous exit readiness with high-fidelity performance instrumentation . We are replacing episodic consulting with a compounding AI platform that scales expertise across companies, industries, and ownership cycles. Teragonia is built for high-agency builders who want early responsibility, direct exposure to world's leading private equity firms and their portfolio CXOs, and the opportunity to help define the operating layer for value creation in the AI era.

Teragonia offers a comprehensive career development platform through cross-learning within our multidisciplinary structure. We nurture a diverse, inclusive, collaborative, respectful, and collegial environment. Our competitive compensation package aligns with the technology industry leaders, encompassing 401k contribution matching, medical insurance, and additional benefits.

Read more about our mission, vision, and values on our website:


About Teragonia: https://teragonia.com/company/
Our Values: https:// teragonia.com/careers/
Our Purpose: https://teragonia.com/value-orchestration/


Teragonia's Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) Department Summary

Teragonia’s Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) division bridges finance, consulting, and technology to support portfolio companies. We combine consulting rigor and AI to address complex challenges and enhance enterprise value through platform solutions and advisory. We prioritize professional growth through mentorship and cross-functional learning.

The Astradis platform provides real-time intelligence through a function-specific command center, combining structured and unstructured data via our Fibron AI layer. Our platform powers diagnostic dashboards, forecasting models, pricing tools, and operations modules, enabling clients to transition from reactive reporting to predictive strategy.

Requirements

Job Summary

We are looking for a talented Analytics Engineer to join our team as an Analyst. As an Analyst, you will work closely with our team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions for our private equity-backed company clients. You will assist in understanding their business needs and developing tailored analytics solutions.

Responsibilities


Support a team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions and be an active contributor to executing on engagement work
Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in working closely with clients to understand their business needs and develop tailored analytics solutions
Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in translating Client communicated business needs into an exploratory data analysis program and lead team efforts to execute
Assist analytics engineering teams in designing, developing, maintaining, and optimizing complex enterprise data models to help solve operational and financial business problems
Develop and maintain high-quality, visually appealing reports and dashboards to communicate complex analytics insights and recommendations to senior client executives, ensuring that findings are easy to understand and actionable by senior executives
Make a concerted effort to begin to develop and maintain strong relationships with clients and internal stakeholders
Participate in and assist in project meetings and contribute to project deliverables
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and prioritize tasks to meet deadlines
Assist in developing and implementing process improvements to increase team efficiency and productivity
Collaborate with data engineers to design, develop, maintain, and optimize data pipelines critical to the integrity of the enterprise data models
Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in data analytics and apply this knowledge to improve our solutions


Overview

As an Analyst, you will be a key contributor to our service delivery, innovation, and practice development. You will have the opportunity, and the management support, to develop specialized knowledge of data analytics, data science, and AI applications in a chosen industry or domain. You will possess excellent communication and presentation skills and be eager to develop as a key contributor in the Value Creation and Analytics (VC&A) Consulting division and develop executive presence as your career with us progresses to Associate, Manager, Principal, Director, and Managing Director levels. We will provide appropriate mentorship and company selected resources for your leadership skill development.

Technical Skills

As an Analyst, you will be quick to understand the client’s business needs and execute on delivering data-driven solutions. You will play a direct role in driving engagement workflows, deliverables, and timelines, ensuring the quality of enterprise-grade data pipelines and business intelligence applications. These efforts aim to enhance pricing, customer segmentation, sales effectiveness, customer lifetime value, attrition management, workforce scheduling, and cost optimization. You will be actively contributing to and delivering the team’s insights around the ideation, design and development of various analytics, data science and generative AI products, working hand-in-hand with our software engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers. 

The ideal candidate is a proactive problem-solver who takes full ownership of delivering high-quality client work. This individual thrives on delving into technical details to ensure the engagement team successfully meets deliverables by employing analytical techniques to develop crucial business insights for our clients. You will have excellent attention to detail and project management skills, essential for working effectively within our diverse, multidisciplinary team. As part of a collaborative group that includes business analysts, software engineers, data engineers, analytics engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers, you must demonstrate strong teamwork abilities. Your role will require not only individual excellence but also seamless integration and cooperation with team members to achieve shared goals and deliver impactful results.

Transforming low-hygiene data sets to develop organized databases with high fidelity is a necessary part of the job. The ideal candidate is an expert in data wrangling skills and possesses excellent knowledge of the software tools used for service delivery, including data manipulation and data visualization software. You will develop deep expertise in the Teragonia technology stack, for which we will provide appropriate training resources to the extent such tools are new to you. 

Engagement and Client Management

You could be delivering multiple client engagements simultaneously, or a large and complex engagement, and the scope of the engagement could evolve based on changing client needs. The financial sponsor universe demands actionable insights in a relatively short timeframe. The ideal candidate will be excited about driving critical client deliverables and solving a variety of business problems in parallel, in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

The engagement planning and management are critical to maintaining high quality delivery and efficiency. You will actively support the Engagement Manager and Associate by delivering on the engagement plan and strategy and contribute your own ideas, asking questions, challenging the ideas and timelines presented to ensure that high quality client deliverables can be delivered in the timeframe our clients request them. You will also support the Engagement Manager and Associate with assessing the project management plan including resource allocation, tasks assignment, priorities, timelines, budget vs actual, fee updates, and more. 

You will have strong communication and presentation skills and be able to clearly articulate to the team, engagement leader(s) and the client, the business applications and impact of the data analytics assets delivered.

Other Key Responsibilities


Innovation – Support cross functional innovation initiatives
Specialization – Develop a sector or domain specialty
Recruitment – Actively contribute to Teragonia’s recruitment efforts to attract top talent
Marketing and business development – Assist executive leadership and officers with strategic marketing initiatives
Thought leadership - Assist with conducting research for thought leadership initiatives


Requirements

Necessary Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Industrial Engineering, or a related field with stellar academic performance from leading universities
2 years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, software engineering or a related field at global consulting firms, top financial services firms, or high-growth startups
Strong interest in learning how analytics insights drive business value
Advanced programming skills in SQL and Python
Experience in performing exploratory data analysis in a professional setting
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or PowerBI
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with cloud-based analytics platforms and big data technologies such as Snowflake, GCP, DBT, FiveTran, Dataiku, etc.
High business acumen and advanced understanding of connectivity between operational data and financial data (ERP CRM PoS HRIS P&L / trial balance / journal entries, etc.)
Experience with data modeling methodologies and concepts (e.g., Kimball Modeling, Data Vault, Data Mesh, etc.)
Strong experience with data modeling
Certification in data science or analytics (e.g., Certified Analytics Professional)
Master's in Business Analytics, Computer Science, or similar advanced degree


Benefits

Canadian Benefits


Competitive Total Compensation - Market-benchmarked salary and bonus structure to attract and retain top talent.
Additional Compensation - Performance-based incentives and signing bonus, as applicable.
Visa Sponsorship - May be available for eligible candidates to join our diverse team subject to applicable laws and regulations in Canada.
RRSP Plan with Employer Contributions - We help secure your future with matching RRSP contributions up to 6.0% of your annual base salary.
WSIB Coverage - Workplace Safety and Insurance Board coverage as required by Ontario law.
Professional Development Opportunities - We will sponsor company-selected training sessions, conferences, and certifications to foster your growth.
Meal Stipend - Reimbursement for lunch at the office, and dinner too if you are working past 7 pm, up to a limit.
Fitness-Wellness Stipend - For fitness & wellness programs, such as gym memberships, up to a limit.
Paid Time Off (PTO) - Eligible for 15 to 20 days PTO based on applicable level. Vacation pay is calculated as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Vacation time may be carried over up to a maximum allowable under ESA requirements.
Statutory Holidays - ESA statutory holidays as well as additional company holidays: New Year’s Day, Family Day, Good Friday, Victoria Day, Canada Day, Labour Day, Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, Boxing Day, Easter Monday, Civic Holiday, Remembrance Day
Comprehensive Healthcare, Vision, and Dental Insurance - We prioritize your health and wellness with insurance coverage through leading Canadian providers equivalent to or exceeding provincial requirements.
Fertility and family-building benefits through leading Canadian healthcare providers.
Flexible Benefits Plan - We provide the opportunity to manage your healthcare expenses efficiently through Canadian tax-advantaged benefit plans where available.
Mental Health Support - Access to mental health support through Canadian mental health providers covered under our healthcare plan.
Health Navigation Services - Expert assistance to help you navigate the complexities of the Canadian healthcare system.
Disability Insurance - We provide short term disability insurance through a leading Canadian provider covering up to 26 weeks at 60% of weekly earnings up to $1,500, exceeding Employment Insurance requirements.
Sick Days - 5 paid sick days annually that reset each year, as required under the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Additional unpaid sick leave available as per ESA requirements.
Leaves of Absence - Teragonia provides unpaid statutory leaves as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA), including pregnancy, parental, caregiver, and bereavement leave. Eligibility and duration follow provincial regulations. Additional unpaid leaves or flexible arrangements may be considered on a case-by-case basis.
Paid Parental Leave - Paid Parental Leave of 12 weeks after 6 months of service with the company.


Flexible Work Policy

We promote flexible work to support wellbeing and performance. Remote work is available though in-person collaboration is encouraged for planning and team connection. Toronto based employees have no mandated office days but are expected in person for key meetings. Employees outside Toronto may work remotely with occasional travel for events. Early career professionals with less than three years of experience should live within commuting distance to receive in person mentorship. Relocation support is available on a case-by-case basis.

Working Hours and ESA Exemption

This position is classified as a specialized professional role under O. Reg. 285/01, s. 2 of Ontario's Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA) and is therefore exempt from overtime pay requirements. While standard hours of work are expected to be approximately 40 hours per week, additional hours may be required to meet client deliverable sand business needs. Such expectations are reflected in the annual salary.

Canadian Experience

We welcome applications from all qualified candidates. Canadian work experience is not a requirement for this position.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Statement

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing a respectful, inclusive, harassment free workplace. We hire based on merit and qualifications in line with the Ontario Human Rights Code and Employment Standards Act. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds.

Accommodation For Disabilities

We are committed to dignity, independence, inclusion, and equal opportunity under the Ontario Human Rights Code and AODA. We provide reasonable accommodations during recruitment and employment. Employees needing support should contact their manager or Human Resources representative.

Retention Notice

In compliance with Ontario's employment standards, Teragonia retains copies of all publicly advertised job postings and associated application forms for a period of three years.

Recruiting Process

Our recruitment process is designed to ensure a thoughtful and comprehensive evaluation of candidates, as well as alignment with the team’s objectives and company values. Candidates are encouraged to ask questions and engage openly throughout. The process typically involves the following stages:


Initial Interviews with Managers – Two interviews focused on fit and goals
Technical Interviews with Principals – One or two sessions assessing skills and problem-solving
Final Interview with Founders – Discuss strategic alignment and long-term potential


AI Use Disclosure

We use artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to support the screening and assessment.

Accessibility in Recruitment

We are committed to accessibility. If you require accommodation during any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us in advance. We will support you in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).
