We are replacing episodic consulting with a compounding AI platform that scales expertise across companies, industries, and ownership cycles. Teragonia is built for high-agency builders who want early responsibility, direct exposure to world's leading private equity firms and their portfolio CXOs, and the opportunity to help define the operating layer for value creation in the AI era.

Teragonia offers a comprehensive career development platform through cross-learning within our multidisciplinary structure. We nurture a diverse, inclusive, collaborative, respectful, and collegial environment. Our competitive compensation package aligns with the technology industry leaders, encompassing 401k contribution matching, medical insurance, and additional benefits.

Read more about our mission, vision, and values on our website:

  • About Teragonia: https://teragonia.com/company/
  • Our Values: https:// teragonia.com/careers/
  • Our Purpose: https://teragonia.com/value-orchestration/

Teragonia's Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) Department Summary

Teragonia’s Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) division bridges finance, consulting, and technology to support portfolio companies. We combine consulting rigor and AI to address complex challenges and enhance enterprise value through platform solutions and advisory. We prioritize professional growth through mentorship and cross-functional learning.

The Astradis platform provides real-time intelligence through a function-specific command center, combining structured and unstructured data via our Fibron AI layer. Our platform powers diagnostic dashboards, forecasting models, pricing tools, and operations modules, enabling clients to transition from reactive reporting to predictive strategy.

Requirements

Job Summary

We are looking for a talented Analytics Engineer to join our team as an Analyst. As an Analyst, you will work closely with our team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions for our private equity-backed company clients. You will assist in understanding their business needs and developing tailored analytics solutions.

Responsibilities

  • Support a team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions and be an active contributor to executing on engagement work
  • Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in working closely with clients to understand their business needs and develop tailored analytics solutions
  • Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in translating Client communicated business needs into an exploratory data analysis program and lead team efforts to execute
  • Assist analytics engineering teams in designing, developing, maintaining, and optimizing complex enterprise data models to help solve operational and financial business problems
  • Develop and maintain high-quality, visually appealing reports and dashboards to communicate complex analytics insights and recommendations to senior client executives, ensuring that findings are easy to understand and actionable by senior executives
  • Make a concerted effort to begin to develop and maintain strong relationships with clients and internal stakeholders
  • Participate in and assist in project meetings and contribute to project deliverables
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and prioritize tasks to meet deadlines
  • Assist in developing and implementing process improvements to increase team efficiency and productivity
  • Collaborate with data engineers to design, develop, maintain, and optimize data pipelines critical to the integrity of the enterprise data models
  • Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in data analytics and apply this knowledge to improve our solutions

Overview

As an Analyst, you will be a key contributor to our service delivery, innovation, and practice development. You will have the opportunity, and the management support, to develop specialized knowledge of data analytics, data science, and AI applications in a chosen industry or domain. You will possess excellent communication and presentation skills and be eager to develop as a key contributor in the Value Creation and Analytics (VC&A) Consulting division and develop executive presence as your career with us progresses to Associate, Manager, Principal, Director, and Managing Director levels. We will provide appropriate mentorship and company selected resources for your leadership skill development.

Technical Skills

As an Analyst, you will be quick to understand the client’s business needs and execute on delivering data-driven solutions. You will play a direct role in driving engagement workflows, deliverables, and timelines, ensuring the quality of enterprise-grade data pipelines and business intelligence applications. These efforts aim to enhance pricing, customer segmentation, sales effectiveness, customer lifetime value, attrition management, workforce scheduling, and cost optimization. You will be actively contributing to and delivering the team’s insights around the ideation, design and development of various analytics, data science and generative AI products, working hand-in-hand with our software engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers. 

The ideal candidate is a proactive problem-solver who takes full ownership of delivering high-quality client work. This individual thrives on delving into technical details to ensure the engagement team successfully meets deliverables by employing analytical techniques to develop crucial business insights for our clients. You will have excellent attention to detail and project management skills, essential for working effectively within our diverse, multidisciplinary team. As part of a collaborative group that includes business analysts, software engineers, data engineers, analytics engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers, you must demonstrate strong teamwork abilities. Your role will require not only individual excellence but also seamless integration and cooperation with team members to achieve shared goals and deliver impactful results.

Transforming low-hygiene data sets to develop organized databases with high fidelity is a necessary part of the job. The ideal candidate is an expert in data wrangling skills and possesses excellent knowledge of the software tools used for service delivery, including data manipulation and data visualization software. You will develop deep expertise in the Teragonia technology stack, for which we will provide appropriate training resources to the extent such tools are new to you. 

Engagement and Client Management

You could be delivering multiple client engagements simultaneously, or a large and complex engagement, and the scope of the engagement could evolve based on changing client needs. The financial sponsor universe demands actionable insights in a relatively short timeframe. The ideal candidate will be excited about driving critical client deliverables and solving a variety of business problems in parallel, in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

The engagement planning and management are critical to maintaining high quality delivery and efficiency. You will actively support the Engagement Manager and Associate by delivering on the engagement plan and strategy and contribute your own ideas, asking questions, challenging the ideas and timelines presented to ensure that high quality client deliverables can be delivered in the timeframe our clients request them. You will also support the Engagement Manager and Associate with assessing the project management plan including resource allocation, tasks assignment, priorities, timelines, budget vs actual, fee updates, and more. 

You will have strong communication and presentation skills and be able to clearly articulate to the team, engagement leader(s) and the client, the business applications and impact of the data analytics assets delivered.

Other Key Responsibilities

  • Innovation – Support cross functional innovation initiatives
  • Specialization – Develop a sector or domain specialty
  • Recruitment – Actively contribute to Teragonia’s recruitment efforts to attract top talent
  • Marketing and business development – Assist executive leadership and officers with strategic marketing initiatives
  • Thought leadership - Assist with conducting research for thought leadership initiatives

Requirements

Necessary Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Industrial Engineering, or a related field with stellar academic performance from leading universities
  • 2 years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, software engineering or a related field at global consulting firms, top financial services firms, or high-growth startups
  • Strong interest in learning how analytics insights drive business value
  • Advanced programming skills in SQL and Python
  • Experience in performing exploratory data analysis in a professional setting
  • Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or PowerBI
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with cloud-based analytics platforms and big data technologies such as Snowflake, GCP, DBT, FiveTran, Dataiku, etc.
    • High business acumen and advanced understanding of connectivity between operational data and financial data (ERP CRM PoS HRIS P&L / trial balance / journal entries, etc.)
  • Experience with data modeling methodologies and concepts (e.g., Kimball Modeling, Data Vault, Data Mesh, etc.)
  • Strong experience with data modeling
  • Certification in data science or analytics (e.g., Certified Analytics Professional)
  • Master's in Business Analytics, Computer Science, or similar advanced degree

    • Benefits

    Canadian Benefits

    • Competitive Total Compensation - Market-benchmarked salary and bonus structure to attract and retain top talent.
    • Additional Compensation - Performance-based incentives and signing bonus, as applicable.
    • Visa Sponsorship - May be available for eligible candidates to join our diverse team subject to applicable laws and regulations in Canada.
    • RRSP Plan with Employer Contributions - We help secure your future with matching RRSP contributions up to 6.0% of your annual base salary.
    • WSIB Coverage - Workplace Safety and Insurance Board coverage as required by Ontario law.
    • Professional Development Opportunities - We will sponsor company-selected training sessions, conferences, and certifications to foster your growth.
    • Meal Stipend - Reimbursement for lunch at the office, and dinner too if you are working past 7 pm, up to a limit.
    • Fitness-Wellness Stipend - For fitness & wellness programs, such as gym memberships, up to a limit.
    • Paid Time Off (PTO) - Eligible for 15 to 20 days PTO based on applicable level. Vacation pay is calculated as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Vacation time may be carried over up to a maximum allowable under ESA requirements.
    • Statutory Holidays - ESA statutory holidays as well as additional company holidays: New Year’s Day, Family Day, Good Friday, Victoria Day, Canada Day, Labour Day, Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, Boxing Day, Easter Monday, Civic Holiday, Remembrance Day
    • Comprehensive Healthcare, Vision, and Dental Insurance - We prioritize your health and wellness with insurance coverage through leading Canadian providers equivalent to or exceeding provincial requirements.
    • Fertility and family-building benefits through leading Canadian healthcare providers.
    • Flexible Benefits Plan - We provide the opportunity to manage your healthcare expenses efficiently through Canadian tax-advantaged benefit plans where available.
    • Mental Health Support - Access to mental health support through Canadian mental health providers covered under our healthcare plan.
    • Health Navigation Services - Expert assistance to help you navigate the complexities of the Canadian healthcare system.
    • Disability Insurance - We provide short term disability insurance through a leading Canadian provider covering up to 26 weeks at 60% of weekly earnings up to $1,500, exceeding Employment Insurance requirements.
    • Sick Days - 5 paid sick days annually that reset each year, as required under the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Additional unpaid sick leave available as per ESA requirements.
    • Leaves of Absence - Teragonia provides unpaid statutory leaves as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA), including pregnancy, parental, caregiver, and bereavement leave. Eligibility and duration follow provincial regulations. Additional unpaid leaves or flexible arrangements may be considered on a case-by-case basis.
    • Paid Parental Leave - Paid Parental Leave of 12 weeks after 6 months of service with the company.

    Flexible Work Policy

    We promote flexible work to support wellbeing and performance. Remote work is available though in-person collaboration is encouraged for planning and team connection. Toronto based employees have no mandated office days but are expected in person for key meetings. Employees outside Toronto may work remotely with occasional travel for events. Early career professionals with less than three years of experience should live within commuting distance to receive in person mentorship. Relocation support is available on a case-by-case basis.

    Working Hours and ESA Exemption

    This position is classified as a specialized professional role under O. Reg. 285/01, s. 2 of Ontario's Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA) and is therefore exempt from overtime pay requirements. While standard hours of work are expected to be approximately 40 hours per week, additional hours may be required to meet client deliverable sand business needs. Such expectations are reflected in the annual salary.

    Canadian Experience

    We welcome applications from all qualified candidates. Canadian work experience is not a requirement for this position.

    Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Statement

    We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing a respectful, inclusive, harassment free workplace. We hire based on merit and qualifications in line with the Ontario Human Rights Code and Employment Standards Act. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds.

    Accommodation For Disabilities

    We are committed to dignity, independence, inclusion, and equal opportunity under the Ontario Human Rights Code and AODA. We provide reasonable accommodations during recruitment and employment. Employees needing support should contact their manager or Human Resources representative.

    Retention Notice

    In compliance with Ontario's employment standards, Teragonia retains copies of all publicly advertised job postings and associated application forms for a period of three years.

    Recruiting Process

    Our recruitment process is designed to ensure a thoughtful and comprehensive evaluation of candidates, as well as alignment with the team’s objectives and company values. Candidates are encouraged to ask questions and engage openly throughout. The process typically involves the following stages:

    • Initial Interviews with Managers – Two interviews focused on fit and goals
    • Technical Interviews with Principals – One or two sessions assessing skills and problem-solving
    • Final Interview with Founders – Discuss strategic alignment and long-term potential

    AI Use Disclosure

    We use artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to support the screening and assessment.

    Accessibility in Recruitment

    We are committed to accessibility. If you require accommodation during any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us in advance. We will support you in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Location: Toronto, 100% in-office, 8am-5pm

Salary: $60,000~80,000

Bonus: Maximum 50% per annum

Vacancies: 1 new




Company:

Join our innovative team connecting and entertaining players worldwide on the world’s largest online poker network and at the biggest poker events.




Home of World Champion Daniel Negreanu and holder of the Guinness World Record for the largest-ever online poker prize pool, GGPoker leads the industry.




Headquartered in Toronto with offices in nine major cities and 700+ international team members, NSUS is shaping the future of gaming. As the global market leader in online poker, we’re looking for driven, creative talent to join the World’s #1.




Be part of the best in iGaming.




Position:

We are looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst to join our lean, fast-moving Business Intelligence team in the online gaming industry. You’ll work in a high-impact environment where data drives every decision. This role is ideal for someone who is naturally inquisitive, proactive, and thrives at uncovering the story behind the data.




You will report directly to the Head of Business Intelligence.




Duties and Responsibilities:




Proactive Monitoring & Issue Detection: Investigate "the why" behind the numbers immediately.
Data Visualization & Reporting: Build and maintain dynamic Tableau dashboards that non-technical stakeholders can understand instantly.
Communicate Findings to Stakeholders: Translate technical findings into clear, concise executive summaries.
Data Analysis: Analyze large, complex datasets to identify trends and support strategic business decisions
Data Design: Design and optimize SQL queries from the data warehouse to extract critical information that meets stakeholder needs
KPI Development: Support team with the development and improvement of KPIs such as LTV, Churn, CAC.
Forecasting & Budgeting: Monitor and track regional performance against the forecasting model.




Requirements:




Project management & prioritization skills
Strong sense of curiosity, initiative, and thrives in a high-pressure environment
Clear communicator able to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills - able to bridge the gap between quantitative trends and qualitative business reality
Proficient in SQL and experience with cloud-based data warehouses (Snowflake preferred)
Experience with Tableau or other BI visualization tools (preferred)
Experience in iGaming, fintech, or high-volume data environments is an asset




Total Rewards:




Annual salary and promotion review
Max 50% bonus per annum
Flexible benefits
Commute coverage
Endless snacks and beverages




We are an equal-opportunity employer and we are committed to building a diverse, inclusive, and welcoming workplace for all. NSUS is committed to providing a barrier-free work environment in concert with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act and the Ontario Human Rights Code. As such, NSUS will make accommodations available to applicants with disabilities upon request during the recruitment process




We use AI tools to help review applications. All results are reviewed by a human, and all hiring decisions are made by a person. Your information is used only to assess your qualifications and is handled in accordance with Ontario's privacy and human rights laws.

To request an accommodation or ask about our process, contact us at hr@nsusgroup.com.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
THE POSITION

Our roster has an opening with your name on it

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our team. You will derive insights for group-wide and commercial teams in order to drive actionable insights about the business. You will work alongside a group of other analysts through various individual and team assignments to continually evolve the various tools, models, and data visualizations used to drive the FanDuel Group business.

In addition to the specific responsibilities outlined above, employees may be required to perform other such duties as assigned by the Company. This ensures operational flexibility and allows the Company to meet evolving business needs.

THE GAME PLAN

Everyone on our team has a part to play


Be the strategic partner and data expert with Growth/CRM to drive important key insights
Work closely with stakeholders to drive collaboration and maintain strategic roadmap
Deliver key strategic projects that are critical to business goals set for the year
Create, modify and evolve models and data visualizations to better understand and explain the day to day performance of various commercial KPIs
Create and maintain automated business critical reporting processes visible to the commercial and group-wide teams
Participate in presentations focused on using analysis to drive new business outcomes
Field and fulfill requests for various business inquires



THE STATS

What we're looking for in our next teammate


3+ year of experience in an analytics role preferred
Bachelor's Degree in a highly numerate major (Math, Stats, Econ, etc.) or relevant experience in data and/or business analysis
Strong personal organizational and time management skills
Ability to work in a fast paced environment with a lot of ambiguity
Proficiency in SQL and Excel required; experience with Python preferred
Strong problem solving skills, allied with track record of transforming data into actionable business insight
Experience working in groups and leading smaller intra year projects
Experience collaboratively working with colleagues in other countries and time zones preferred
Interest in Fantasy Sports and/or Sports Gaming preferred



About Fanduel

FanDuel Group is the premier mobile gaming company in the United States and Canada. FanDuel Group consists of a portfolio of leading brands across mobile wagering including: America’s #1 Sportsbook, FanDuel Sportsbook; its leading iGaming platform, FanDuel Casino; the industry’s unquestioned leader in horse racing and advance-deposit wagering, FanDuel Racing; and its daily fantasy sports product.

In addition, FanDuel Group operates FanDuel TV, its broadly distributed linear cable television network and FanDuel TV+, its leading direct-to-consumer OTT platform. FanDuel Group has a presence across all 50 states, Canada, and Puerto Rico.

The company is based in New York with US offices in Los Angeles, Atlanta, and Jersey City, as well as global offices in Canada and Scotland. The company’s affiliates have offices worldwide, including in Ireland, Portugal, Romania, and Australia.

FanDuel Group is a subsidiary of Flutter Entertainment, the world's largest sports betting and gaming operator with a portfolio of globally recognized brands and traded on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: FLUT).

Player Benefits

We treat our team right

This role may offer the following benefits: extended medical, vision, and dental coverage; life insurance; disability insurance; a retirement program with employer contributions; among other employee benefits. This role may also be eligible for short-term or long-term incentive compensation, including, but not limited to, cash bonuses and stock program participation. This role includes 12 holidays and two (2) floating holidays. FanDuel offers paid sick time in accordance with applicable Canadian law. Check out all our benefits here: FanDuel Total Rewards. *Benefits differ across country and job type.

FanDuel is an equal opportunities employer and we believe, as one of our principles states, “We are One Team!”. As such, we are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, or any other characteristic protected by provincial, local or federal law. We believe FanDuel is strongest and best able to compete if all employees feel valued, respected, and included.

FanDuel is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please email Benefits@fanduel.com.

The Company intends to use artificial intelligence in the process of screening, assessing, and/or selecting applicants for this role.

The applicable salary range for this position is $88,000 - $110,000 CAD, which is dependent on a variety of factors including relevant experience, location, business needs and market demand. This role may offer the following benefits: extended medical, vision, and dental coverage; life insurance; disability insurance; a retirement program with employer contributions; among other employee benefits. This role may also be eligible for short-term or long-term incentive compensation, including, but not limited to, cash bonuses and stock program participation. This role includes 12 holidays and two (2) floating holidays. FanDuel offers paid sick time in accordance with applicable Canadian law.

Title: BI Engineer
Description:
Charger Logistics Inc. is a world-class asset-based carrier with locations across North America. With over 20 years of experience providing the best logistics solutions, Charger Logistics has transformed into a world-class transport provider and continues to grow.

Charger Logistics invests time and support into its employees, providing them with the room to learn, grow their expertise, and advance their careers. We are an entrepreneurial-minded organization that welcomes and supports individual ideas and strategies. We are currently expanding and looking to add a motivated individual to our BI Engineering team based in our Brampton office.

The BI Engineer will work collectively and collaboratively with the IT Development team. This is a technical role within our IT Analytics team that requires a highly motivated and experienced BI professional to participate and contribute in all areas of advanced reporting and analytics. The BI Engineer is expected to scope, analyze, develop, automate, enhance, and validate BI reporting & analytics, and dashboard solutions in a timely manner.

Responsibilities{{{{:}}}}


Transform complex data sets into meaningful conclusions and actionable recommendations
Gather, organize, and analyze data from various load boards, identify areas of opportunity, and support business decisions
Analyze large amounts of structured and unstructured data and recommend broad-based innovative improvement initiatives
Produce and maintain scalable, automated, user-friendly reporting systems and dashboards
Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, reporting tools, and structures to track key metrics
Work with end users to understand their conceptual needs and translate them into production-ready solutions
Own the design, development, and maintenance of ongoing metrics, reports, and analyses used to drive key business decisions
Advanced ability to draw insights from data and communicate them clearly to stakeholders and senior management, both verbally and in writing


Requirements


Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field with 4+ years of industry experience
Advanced SQL skills for data acquisition, transformation, and analysis
Experience developing and operating large-scale data structures for business intelligence analytics using ETL/ELT processes, OLAP technologies, and data modeling
Proficiency in configuring and using Tableau or other BI applications to visualize data and create dashboards/scorecards (Tableau Desktop certification preferred)
Strong knowledge of ETL design and development using Integration Services (SSIS)
Extensive experience in developing, maintaining, and managing Tableau-driven dashboards and analytics, including Tableau administration/architecture
General understanding of databases such as MySQL, SQL Server, and Snowflake
Experience with Snowflake and PySpark is a plus
Professional experience with reporting packages and tools (Power BI, Tableau, Matillion, etc.) and general programming
Experience in the Logistics, Transportation, or Supply Chain industries is an asset
Advanced ability to analyze data and clearly communicate insights to stakeholders and senior management


Benefits


Competitive Salary
Healthcare Benefit Package
Career Growth

  • Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, reporting tools, and structures to track key metrics
  • Work with end users to understand their conceptual needs and translate them into production-ready solutions
  • Own the design, development, and maintenance of ongoing metrics, reports, and analyses used to drive key business decisions
  • Advanced ability to draw insights from data and communicate them clearly to stakeholders and senior management, both verbally and in writing

Requirements

  • Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field with 4+ years of industry experience
  • Advanced SQL skills for data acquisition, transformation, and analysis
  • Experience developing and operating large-scale data structures for business intelligence analytics using ETL/ELT processes, OLAP technologies, and data modeling
  • Proficiency in configuring and using Tableau or other BI applications to visualize data and create dashboards/scorecards (Tableau Desktop certification preferred)
  • Strong knowledge of ETL design and development using Integration Services (SSIS)
  • Extensive experience in developing, maintaining, and managing Tableau-driven dashboards and analytics, including Tableau administration/architecture
  • General understanding of databases such as MySQL, SQL Server, and Snowflake
  • Experience with Snowflake and PySpark is a plus
  • Professional experience with reporting packages and tools (Power BI, Tableau, Matillion, etc.) and general programming
  • Experience in the Logistics, Transportation, or Supply Chain industries is an asset
  • Advanced ability to analyze data and clearly communicate insights to stakeholders and senior management

Benefits

  • Competitive Salary
  • Healthcare Benefit Package
  • Career Growth

Title: Data Analytics Investigator
Description:
Requisition ID: 259868

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

Contributes to the overall success of Digital Forensic Services Analytics / Corporate Security globally ensuring specific individual goals, plans, initiatives are executed / delivered in support of the team’s business strategies and objectives. Ensures all activities conducted are in compliance with governing regulations, internal policies and procedures.

Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:


Champions a customer focused culture to deepen client relationships and leverage broader Bank relationships, systems and knowledge.
Assist with the design and implementation of the overall enterprise insider threat program, user behavioral analytics and future data modelling and analytics initiatives in support of investigations, criminal activity and risk mitigation.
Contributes to the design of complex predictive models and pattern detection algorithms in Splunk to detect suspicious digital fraud alerts and user behavioral activities. Decode and debug complex Search queries, build Splunk Use Cases for financial crime, employee monitoring, threat intelligence, Online banking, PCI & PII use cases.
Contributes to the support and maintenance of the Banks’ enterprise insider threat intelligence program, and Corporate Security’s digital fraud investigation mechanisms by updating or creating new Splunk data models, rules, alerts, and/or dashboards to provide relative real time actionable intelligence.
Analyze, review, and provide insight into the Bank’s enterprise insider threat program, major digital fraud and financial crimes investigations, other intelligence big data analytics industry initiatives to proactively support business decisions and processes.
Keep abreast of emerging insider threat techniques, digital fraud trends, investigative techniques, evolving regulatory requirements and assess potential impact to the bank.
Establish and maintain effective dialogue with internal technology and business partners to identify opportunities for new initiatives, development, and risk mitigation.
Understand how the Bank’s risk appetite and risk culture should be considered in day-to-day activities and decisions.
Actively pursues effective and efficient operations of their respective areas in accordance with Scotiabank’s Values, its Code of Conduct and the Global Sales Principles, while ensuring the adequacy, adherence to and effectiveness of day-to-day business controls to meet obligations with respect to operational, compliance, AML/ATF/sanctions and conduct risk.
Champions a high performance environment and contributes to an inclusive work environment.




Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? - We'd love to work with you if you have:


Post-secondary degree in relevant STEM discipline (Computer Science, Electrical/Computer/Software Engineering, and Statistics/Mathematics) and/or 2+ year similar hands-on work experience preferred.
Solid working experience in an analytical role, using expert Splunk SPL query language to create dashboards, algorithms, rules, and/or alerts, preferably in the context of user behavioral analytics and digital fraud analysis.
Demonstrated ability working with analytical and computing tools, such as like Splunk, Microsoft Power BI, SAS, Java, R, Python, Hadoop, HIVE, Spark, etc.
Training and/or certifications in Splunk Enterprise Version 7.x and 8.x and Data Analytics would be an asset.
Strong verbal, written, presentation & communication skills.
Creative, out-of-the-box thinker with strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate data with attention to detail and accuracy.
Must be self-motivated, self-starter, energetic and results-oriented and be committed to adding value to the organization.
Knowledge of industry trends regarding fraud and experience in fraud detection and prevention is an asset.




What’s in it for you?


An inclusive & collaborative working environment that encourages creativity, curiosity, and celebrates success!
We offer a competitive rewards package: Performance bonus, Employee Share Ownership Program, and Pension Plan Matching, Health Benefits from day one!
Your career matters! You will have access to career development and progression opportunities.


Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Advanced Analyst: Apex
Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

The Data & Insights (D&I) team transforms data into actionable insights that power EA. We guide strategy and governance, build data and AI solutions, and ensure personalization experimentation and analytics; partnering across teams to improve decisions and create more meaningful player experiences.

Reporting to the Senior Manager of Analytics, you will have an impact on all game systems in Apex Legends, one of the world's biggest shooter franchises.

This is a hybrid remote/in-office role.

Responsibilities


Conduct A/B tests and causal inference to evaluate the impact of game content and feature changes.
Analyze behavioral data to provide insights into game features.
Collaborate with team members to improve the game optimization strategies in Apex Legends.
Model and simulate complex gameplay systems throughout the development lifecycle.
Design dashboards and presentations that communicate insights to team members.
Partner with Data Engineers and Architects to promote scalable analysis.


Qualifications


5+ years of professional experience informing product strategy with data analysis in gaming or adjacent digital products with high independence.
Degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Economics, or related discipline).
Expert–level SQL proficiency with experience extracting insights from complex datasets.
Experience applying hypothesis testing, modeling, and experiment evaluation using Python, familiarity with advanced experiment design and analysis approaches.
Experience designing self-service dashboards using Looker, Tableau, or other similar tools.
Familiarity with game design principles, player retention, and monetization models.


We want to connect you with job opportunities that align with your interests, skills, and expertise. When you create an EA Careers Account and are logged into the portal, you can click “Get Recommendations” to view a curated list of job openings. These recommendations are enhanced by automated processing, including artificial intelligence, and take into account your skills and experience. However, all employment decisions are made by our hiring teams, not by automated systems.

Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$114,300 - $156,200 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

Title: Analyst, Recreation Planning
Description:
Job Opening Number: 107129

Job Requisition Number: 294

Number of Positions: 1

Job Type: Management and Administration/Union

Job Code: Analyst, Recreation Planning

Department: COMMUNITY SERVICES

Division: Recreation

Salary Range

1st Step $76,367.20 per annum

2nd Step: $80,353.00 per annum

Job Rate: $84,593.60 per annum

Job Grade: 7

Job Status and Duration: Full Time (FT), Regular (R)

Hours of Work: 35 Hour work week

Location: 175 Sandalwood

Posting Date: May 22, 2026

Closing Date: June 4, 2026

Notice to Internal Applicants: To ensure your application is processed as internal, please submit your application using your City of Brampton work email address.

External and internal applicants are now being considered.

Position Duties

Reports to Coor, Recreation Services. To provide research, analysis and support for Recreation Planning teams including Line of Business (LOB), Marketing and Facility Planning projects that are integral to the Recreation Division and Community Services Department.


Conducts research and analysis related to LOBs, Facility Profiles and program area to establish historical and current trends.
Perform analysis of external, third party and related research or reports.
Conduct annual benchmarking for rates and fees process.
Analyze complex issues and reveal hidden patterns in data to facilitate informed decision-making.
Develop and maintain a database of Facility Profiles to assist staff with their decision making process with respect to programs and offerings.
Develop and maintain a database of customers that are involved or have expressed interest in Recreation.
Ensure profiles are up to date through annual audits and refresh.
Compile and distribute statistical reports that will include key performance indicators of program success in LOBs.
Create and validate data in reports to Committee/Council on program activities that arise in connection with Recreation.
Identify opportunities and develop recommendations resulting from statistical report analysis.
Become a subject matter expert with third party software with respect to demographics and customer profiles.
Track progress on City of Brampton and/or Region of Peel initiated capital projects affecting the Recreation Facility portfolio.
Attend meetings to provide technical or specialist support to internal and external stakeholders, Committee and Council.
Liaise with staff from various departments to facilitate information exchange, identify issues and upcoming projects.
Utilize multiple data sources as well as internal resources (i.e. Registration Software, Dashboards) to create user friendly outputs that assist in decision making across the division (Supervisors, Managers and Director).
This position is required to have a strong understanding of GIS applications, StatsCan data and other 3rd party data sources including the methodology required to create and share information.
Other similar and related duties as assigned.


Education

SELECTION CRITERIA:


High school (Grade 12) graduation plus an additional program of three to four years or equivalent in Geographic/Environmental Studies, Urban Planning, Economic Development, Computer Science, Business Management, Information Technology, Bachelor of Commerce or Bachelor of Technology or related studies.


Experience


Over two (2) years, up to and including four (4) years’ experience in a research environment with detail oriented duties (i.e. benchmarking, program analysis, program input and verification, GIS mapping).


Required Licence Certification


Must possess a valid class G driver’s license.


Other Skills And Assets


Experience in a municipal or recreation setting is an asset.
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.
Demonstrated proficiency in applications such as MS Office, Power BI, Database applications, and website content/authoring tools.
Proficiency in GIS applications is an asset.
Working knowledge of desktop publishing applications.
Familiarity with the municipal government legislative and regulatory context.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Exceptional written and verbal communication and interpersonal skills with an emphasis on customer service.


Additional Information

Interview: Our recruitment process may be completed with video conference technology.


Various tests and/or exams may be administered as part of the selection criteria.


Applicants who do not meet the educational requirements but have direct related experience may be considered.

As part of the corporation’s Modernizing Job Evaluation project, this position will undergo an evaluation which may result in a change to the rate of compensation. Any changes affecting this position will be communicated as information becomes available.

If this opportunity matches your interest and experience, please apply online by clicking the apply now button by June 4, 2026 and complete the attached questionnaire.

We thank all applicants; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted. The successful candidate(s) will be required, as a condition of employment, to execute a written employment agreement. A criminal record search will be required of the successful candidate to verify the absence of a criminal record for which a pardon has not been granted.

As part of the application process, applicants will be invited to complete a self identification survey. The survey is voluntary. Participation in the survey will have no impact on hiring decisions. All information collected is confidential and will not be shared with the hiring manager. The surveys will be anonymized and will be kept separate from applicant or employee files, such that the individuals who completed the surveys will not be identifiable. The results of the survey will assist in the analysis of disaggregated metrics for organizational planning purposes and our commitment to advance and foster diversity, equity, and inclusion. The City may use anonymized data to produce aggregate reports for internal or external use.

The City of Brampton uses email to communicate with applicants for open job competitions. It is the applicant's responsibility to include an updated email address that is checked daily and accepts emails from unknown users. Time sensitive correspondence is sent via email (i.e. testing bookings, interview dates) and it is imperative that applicants check their email regularly. If we do not hear back from applicants, we will assume that you are no longer interested in the employment opportunity and your application will be removed from the competition.

If you would like to request content in an alternate format, please contact the Accessibility office by submitting a new Alternate Format Request.

Title: Pricing Strategy Analyst
Description:
Sysco is the global leader in foodservice distribution. With over 71,000 colleagues and a fleet of over 13,000 vehicles, Sysco operates approximately 333 distribution facilities worldwide and serves more than 700,000 customer locations. We offer our colleagues the opportunity to grow personally and professionally, to contribute to the success of a dynamic organization, and to serve others in a manner that exceeds their expectations. We're looking for talented, hard-working individuals to join our team. Come grow with us and let us show you why Sysco is at the heart of food and service.

Internal Job Posting



Compensation

$55,700 - $104,600

Purpose

This role will provide analytical insights to measure pricing strategies based on customer, item, regional performance. They will work cross-functionally to gather required inputs to report on price setting decisions, and own performance analytics and insights to drive profitability improvements. This role will require ability to synthesize and communicate results, recommend a course of actions, as well as implement the solutions.

Responsibilities

Perform weekly pricing strategy review and determine recommended adjustments to pricing strategies based on underperforming metrics

Recommend pricing lever changes and analyze impacts to profitability, volume forecast, and customer basket

Conduct performance analytics and generate insights to identify improvement opportunities

Ensure data integrity and configuration accuracy in performance reporting

Work with cross-functional team to collect inputs and design requirements to problem solve or develop pricing recommendations

Complies with Sysco's Food Safety Policy and procedures to ensure food safety, quality and legality requirements are met.

Is willing to work safely with minimal environmental impact and understands the importance of reporting all hazards, incidents, and environmental spills immediately to their Supervisor.

Understands that they play an integral role in their own safety and that of their colleagues and is willing to speak out when hazards are present.

Perform other duties as assigned.



Qualifications



Three (3+) years of analytical experience with knowledge in SQL

Experience in price modeling and model configurations

Completion of post-secondary education in Business Management, Commerce, Economics, Finance or similar field required

Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office including Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook

Comfortable in Virtual and Physical settings. Strong written and verbal communication skills across diverse levels (sales associate to customer to ELT)

English primary. French preferred as a second language.

55 700 $ - 104 600 $

Objectif

Ce rôle fournira des analyses permettant d'évaluer les stratégies de tarification en fonction du rendement par client, article et région. La personne collaborera avec des équipes interfonctionnelles pour recueillir les données nécessaires afin de soutenir les décisions en matière d'établissement des prix, et elle sera responsable des analyses de performance ainsi que des recommandations visant à améliorer la rentabilité. Ce rôle exige la capacité de synthétiser et de communiquer les résultats, de recommander des plans d'action et d'assurer la mise en œuvre des solutions.

Responsabilités

Effectuer une revue hebdomadaire de la stratégie de tarification et déterminer les ajustements recommandés en fonction des indicateurs de rendement insuffisants

Recommander des modifications aux leviers de tarification et analyser les impacts sur la rentabilité, les prévisions de volume et le panier client

Réaliser des analyses de performance et générer des insights afin d'identifier des occasions d'amélioration

Assurer l'intégrité des données et l'exactitude des configurations dans les rapports de performance

Collaborer avec des équipes interfonctionnelles pour recueillir les informations nécessaires et définir les exigences afin de résoudre des problèmes ou formuler des recommandations de prix

Se conformer à la politique et aux procédures de Sysco en matière de salubrité alimentaire afin de garantir le respect des exigences de sécurité, de qualité et de conformité légale

Être disposé à travailler en toute sécurité, en minimisant l'impact environnemental, et comprendre l'importance de signaler immédiatement à son superviseur tout danger, incident ou déversement environnemental

Comprendre l'importance de son rôle dans sa propre sécurité et celle de ses collègues, et être prêt à intervenir lorsqu'un risque est présent

Accomplir toute autre tâche assignée

Trois (3+) années d'expérience en analyse, avec connaissance du SQL

Expérience en modélisation de prix et en configuration de modèles

Diplôme postsecondaire en gestion des affaires, commerce, économie, finance ou domaine similaire requis

Maîtrise avancée de Microsoft Office, incluant Excel, Word, PowerPoint et Outlook

À l'aise dans des environnements virtuels et physiques. Excellentes aptitudes en communication écrite et verbale auprès de divers interlocuteurs (du représentant des ventes au client, jusqu'à l'équipe de direction)

L'anglais est requis. Le français est un atout comme deuxième langue.

Title: Analyst, Databases
Description:
Find Your Limitless with Reliance

By joining Reliance as an Analyst, Databases, you’ll become an important Team Member and will be provided with the tools you need to succeed. We have ambitious growth plans and are looking for Team Members who want to grow with us. At Reliance, we pride ourselves on the fact that we provide limitless opportunities for growth and we encourage it by rewarding our Team Members for their success in many ways. If you’re ready to find your limitless – join us!

What do we offer?


The tools you need to succeed – including the tools for the job as well as training and development programs
A successful and knowledgeable team to be part of and learn from
Competitive base pay plus the ability to earn more tied to your success – the only limit is your potential
A great benefits plan for you / your family
Career paths so you can advance in the company
Awarded Canada’s Most Admired Corporate Cultures with a proudly diverse and inclusive team


What will you do?


Supports and contributes to evolving the Enterprise Data Strategy and Architecture
Working knowledge of traditional database systems applying ETL and data movement solutions to support target data models.
Ability to enhance and develop detailed architecture and technical design documentation and transitional and data flows
Ability to work as a lead both independently and / or as a team member to solution design, including work by Vendors & Internal Partners
Maintain COTS (Commercial off-the-shelf) applications including interfaces, configuration, database tuning and GUI
Monitor vendor bulletins, sites, publication and users group for enhancements and release.
Manage features and enhancement to mobile and web user applications
Provide 2nd level support and triage assigned tickets based on priorities.
Analyzing and improving current system integrations and migration strategies
Identifying, debugging, and advising on system errors or architecture issues
Work with project team members and business analysts to understand requirements and design solutions to support the project requirements
Understand the software development life cycle, involve, and support each stage of SDLC
Support offshore team overseeing all development activities.
Ability to work in an Agile environment. Analyze issues with other team members and respond quickly to other business needs within the deadlines.
Ability to communicate end to end data outcomes to all levels of the team hierarchy.
Knowledge and know-how to troubleshoot potential issues and resolution
Performs code reviews and makes sure development team is following standards. Ensures proper Test Coverage and Unit Testing is performed. Identify ways to utilize platform to improve productivity and make recommendations to support an organization scaling at a rapid pace
Provide technical leadership, setting best practices (e.g., integration and application development, deployment, testing, iterative refinement).
Self-sufficient, self-starters & quick learners on understanding Reliance’s current integration environment.


What do you bring?


College diploma or university degree in the field of computer science, information systems
5+ years of application development, configuration, and support experience
Excellent understanding of application development environments, techniques, and the key operational activities to maintaining enterprise level applications.
Knowledge on Enterprise Service Bus (ESB) Tools and Azure Pipelines
Experienced with Azure Data tools such as Azure Storage accounts, Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure Automation Accounts, Azure Key vault, Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure SQL Database, Azure Analysis Services and Azure Fabric Services.
Experienced with MS SQL SSIS, MS SQL SSRS, oData, SOAP/REST API’s, BLOG storage, Runbooks, Notebooks, Functions and code version control GIT / GitHub and DevOps
Knowledgeable C#, Microsoft ASP.NET Web Forms, JAVA, Phyton, Json
Demonstrated experience working with large-scale, high-availability applications.
Experience in Database Design and development with Business Intelligence using SQL Server 2016/2019, Integration Services (SSIS), DTS Packages, SQL Server Analysis Services (SSAS), DAX, OLAP Cubes, Star Schema and Snowflake Schema.
Used Azure download/upload tasks to download and upload blobs.
Create automated workflows with the help of triggers, runbooks and ETL sequence.
Excellent time and workload management skills.
Good knowledge of System Development Life Cycle (SDLC).
Knowledge database structuring, optimization, tuning, analysis, and recommendation.
Design, develop and implement complex database systems, programs, and applications
Provide thought leadership with scoping solution design for process improvements, ERD diagrams, Database architecture, and documentation.
Excellent interpersonal and team leadership skills
Team player with good logical reasoning ability, coordination and able to complete projects independently.
Can act as a mentor to their fellow team members
Fluent in written and spoken English
Experience driving continuous improvement


Why Reliance?

As a leader in home services and energy efficient solutions for over 60 years, Reliance Home Comfort® (Reliance) is committed to being the Destination of Choice by delivering exceptional experiences for customers seeking comfort and team members seeking a rewarding, inclusive workplace. With over 2 million customer relationships, Reliance is a trusted source and has become one of Canada’s largest providers of water heaters, furnaces, heat pumps, air conditioners, water purification, plumbing, protection plans, smart home, electrical, and EV chargers. Headquartered in Toronto, Reliance employs a workforce of over 2,700 Team Members across 28 branches in Canada and two locations in the United States. Reliance has been certified as a Great Place to Work in 2024 and has been recognized as one of Canada's Most Admired Corporate Cultures by Waterstone Human Capital, for eight consecutive years since 2016. Reliance remains focused on sustainability, workplace safety, diversity, inclusivity, and prudent governance.

This posting is for an existing vacancy.

We use AI enabled tools to support parts of our selection process; however, every hiring decision is reviewed and finalized by our people.

Reliance Home Comfort is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome and encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds including minorities and those with disabilities. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

For more information, please visit www.reliancehomecomfort.com.

Title: PLM & CAD Systems Administrator
Description:
Saltworks Technologies is a leading Canadian cleantech company solving two of the world's most pressing industrial challenges: water sustainability and critical minerals supply. We design and build advanced industrial water treatment and lithium refining systems for some of the world's most recognized companies – in semiconductors, mining, automotive, and more. Saltworks is a 2025 Fast 50 Company and EY Entrepreneur of the Year award winner.

The role:

Saltworks is seeking a PLM & CAD Systems Administrator to support the day‑to‑day operation of our CAD and PLM environments, with a focus on engineering data management, CAD libraries, and design control.

Working closely with engineering and technical teams, this position helps maintain accurate, traceable data across CAD and PLM systems. This is a good fit for someone early in their career who enjoys working with structured technical information, improving how systems and processes work together, and building deeper expertise in engineering systems.

Key responsibilities:



Support the day-to-day administration and configuration of PLM and CAD systems.
Maintain CAD libraries, standard parts, templates, and item lifecycles.
Assist with revision control, engineering changes, and BOM management.
Contribute to system enhancements, upgrades, and testing activities.
Develop and maintain high quality documentation for workflows and best practices.
Troubleshoot data or workflow issues in collaboration with team members.
Gather feedback from team members and suggest improvements to systems or processes.


You will be a great fit if you have:


1–3 years of experience in engineering, manufacturing, quality, supply chain, or a related technical role.
A bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering or Computer Science, or a diploma in Mechanical Engineering Technology or Computer Science.
Experience working with revision-controlled engineering drawings and BOMs.
Hands-on experience with SolidWorks and AutoCAD Plant 3D.
Exposure to PDM or PLM systems (e.g., Aras PLM).
Strong attention to detail and solid problem-solving skills.
Clear written and verbal communication skills.


Bonus points if you have experience with:


Reading and working with P&ID drawings.
ERP systems (e.g., Syspro).
Project management tools (e.g., Wrike).
Heavy industry, piping, or structural steel environments.
Scripting or automation experience (SQL, VBA, C#, Python).


Why join Saltworks?

At Saltworks, you'll collaborate with talented colleagues to solve complex, real-world problems – designing and delivering innovative water treatment and lithium refining systems for some of the world's most recognized companies. Every team member contributes meaningfully, from early-stage development through field deployment.

Saltworks is investing intentionally in artificial intelligence for engineering and technical work. Engineering offers one of the longest runways for AI-driven productivity and automation, and Saltworks aims to be at the forefront of this shift. We provide the training, computational capacity, and practical tools needed for engineers and technical professionals to apply AI meaningfully in their work.

We offer:


Competitive compensation package, including medical, dental, vision, RRSP matching, life insurance, AD&D coverage, and an education allowance.
Culture built on safety, innovation, diversity, and continuous learning.
Collaborative, cross-functional team environment where every member contributes to project outcomes.
Regular social activities and company events.


How to apply:

If you would like to join the Saltworks team, please submit your resume (cover letters are not required).

While we sincerely appreciate all applications, only candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. All applications are considered confidential

Kindly, no recruiters.

Salary: Negotiable based on your experience, qualifications, and alignment with comparable roles internally.

Location: 13800 Steveston Hwy, Richmond, BC V6W 1A8

Title: Senior Business Analyst, Finance Solutions
Description:
About Us

Mark Anthony Group is an entrepreneurial drinks company, built from the ground up by thinking differently, innovating and doing the unexpected. Our company is rooted in family values, a bold vision and relentless determination to continuously raise the bar and make a positive difference in consumers' lives. Founded in 1972, we’ve grown organically from a one-person import wine business into an international drinks company. Our hallmarks include a portfolio of iconic beverage brands—White Claw® Hard Seltzer, Mike’s Hard Lemonade, and Olé Cocktail Co. alongside a collection of luxury wineries in the Okanagan Valley, notably Mission Hill Family Estate, CedarCreek Estate Winery, Martin’s Lane Winery, and CheckMate Artisanal Winery.




At Mark Anthony Brands we hire passionate people because nothing great is done without passion. We trust those closest to the work to do what’s right, to act like an owner. We are growing a lasting legacy, where people come first.




What You'll Do:

As our new Senior Business Analyst, Finance Solutions, you will help deliver and sustain enterprise finance operations and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) solutions. Serving as a bridge between Information Technology (IT) and the business, you’ll own end-to-end delivery from requirements gathering through post-deployment support across key platforms including Coupa, Robotics Process Automation (RPA), and QAD (ERP). You’ll play a critical role in improving data quality, driving user adoption, and enabling high-impact system enhancements for Corporate Finance partners.




This is a high-visibility role at the center of finance transformation, offering hands-on ownership across delivery and support, deep exposure to enterprise platforms, and close partnership with senior finance stakeholders to drive measurable business impact.




Additional key responsibilities include:

Provide end-to-end business analysis across ERP, Source-to-Pay (S2P), and automation initiatives, including requirements gathering, functional design, and solution documentation.
Lead User Acceptance Testing (UAT) coordination, data validation, and hypercare activities to ensure smooth cutovers and stable releases.
Establish and support data governance activities, including master data analysis, data load activities, and ongoing data quality improvement.
Serve as lead business analyst and Tier 3 support for Finance Operations applications, ensuring system uptime, data accuracy, and effective issue resolution.
Build strong stakeholder relationships across Corporate Finance and Operational Finance, aligning IT capabilities to business needs through workshops and training sessions.
Own backlog management and prioritization for Finance Operations solutions, ensuring enhancements are clearly scoped and value-driven.
Track product performance, including service levels and Quality Assurance (QA), and contribute to financial management of initiatives where applicable.
Partner cross-functionally with Legal, Risk, Treasury, and Corporate Shared Services to support solution delivery, change impacts, and adoption.




What You Will Learn In This Role

How manufacturing industry finance and procurement processes operate end-to-end, and how technology enables them at scale.
Deep understanding of the finance technology stack, including procurement tools (Coupa), Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems (QAD), and Robotics Process Automation (RPA).
How to improve data quality through data governance practices, master data analysis, and structured validation and controls.
How to drive user adoption through workshops, training, and change support across diverse stakeholder groups.




What You'll Bring:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
3+ years’ experience in a Business Analyst, Product Analyst, or product delivery role supporting enterprise solutions.
Hands-on delivery experience with procurement and finance platforms (Coupa strongly preferred) and ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) solutions such as QAD.
Demonstrated experience in data governance, master data management, and data quality improvement initiatives.
Strong analytical, stakeholder management, and communication skills, with experience running workshops and enabling user adoption.
CBAP (Certified Business Analysis Professional), AWS certifications, Agile certifications (e.g., CSPO / CSM) are a plus.




Who You Are

Strong collaboration, teamwork and relationship building skills across multiple levels and functions in an organization.
Ability to operate at both a strategic and executional level; ideate and directly execute.
Drive growth & outcomes; activate complex work, enlist others effectively, anticipate and remove roadblocks. Move at pace.
Have a positive, proactive ‘can do’ attitude and an ambitiously curious mindset to be the best in your craft.
Promote ideas & curiosity; adding to your expertise, effectively articulates change, offers ideas and solutions.
Actively care & develop; championing our culture and cultivating trust.
Excellent communicator, compelling storyteller and fascinated by consumer behavior.




Why Join Us?

Mark Anthony is committed to hiring, engaging, and growing qualified, talented, and motivated team members at every level of our organization. We offer competitive compensation that recognizes individual and company performance. The typical hiring range for this position is $80,000 - $100,000 annually; the base pay is determined by market location and job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and education.




As part of our Total Rewards program, we are also proud to offer:

Company-matched RRSP contributions
Extended health and dental benefits, and mental health plans with 24/7 access to a primary care physician via Lumino Health
Maternity & Adoption Leave Top Up and Paid Secondary Caregiver Leave
Paid time off
An extensive offering of world leading learning and development programs to empower your career development – in-person and digital offerings with internationally renowned resources like Franklin Covey and Lumina.




Other perks include:

Located in a prime Vancouver neighborhood (near transit)
Hybrid work environment of 4-days a week in-office
Company paid parking

About Us

Mark Anthony Group is an entrepreneurial drinks company, built from the ground up by thinking differently, innovating and doing the unexpected. Our company is rooted in family values, a bold vision and relentless determination to continuously raise the bar and make a positive difference in consumers' lives. Founded in 1972, we’ve grown organically from a one-person import wine business into an international drinks company. Our hallmarks include a portfolio of iconic beverage brands—White Claw® Hard Seltzer, Mike’s Hard Lemonade, and Olé Cocktail Co. alongside a collection of luxury wineries in the Okanagan Valley, notably Mission Hill Family Estate, CedarCreek Estate Winery, Martin’s Lane Winery, and CheckMate Artisanal Winery.


At Mark Anthony Brands we hire passionate people because nothing great is done without passion. We trust those closest to the work to do what’s right, to act like an owner. We are growing a lasting legacy, where people come first.


What You'll Do:

As our new Senior Business Analyst, Finance Solutions, you will help deliver and sustain enterprise finance operations and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) solutions. Serving as a bridge between Information Technology (IT) and the business, you’ll own end-to-end delivery from requirements gathering through post-deployment support across key platforms including Coupa, Robotics Process Automation (RPA), and QAD (ERP). You’ll play a critical role in improving data quality, driving user adoption, and enabling high-impact system enhancements for Corporate Finance partners.


This is a high-visibility role at the center of finance transformation, offering hands-on ownership across delivery and support, deep exposure to enterprise platforms, and close partnership with senior finance stakeholders to drive measurable business impact.


Additional key responsibilities include:

  • Provide end-to-end business analysis across ERP, Source-to-Pay (S2P), and automation initiatives, including requirements gathering, functional design, and solution documentation.
  • Lead User Acceptance Testing (UAT) coordination, data validation, and hypercare activities to ensure smooth cutovers and stable releases.
  • Establish and support data governance activities, including master data analysis, data load activities, and ongoing data quality improvement.
  • Serve as lead business analyst and Tier 3 support for Finance Operations applications, ensuring system uptime, data accuracy, and effective issue resolution.
  • Build strong stakeholder relationships across Corporate Finance and Operational Finance, aligning IT capabilities to business needs through workshops and training sessions.
  • Own backlog management and prioritization for Finance Operations solutions, ensuring enhancements are clearly scoped and value-driven.
  • Track product performance, including service levels and Quality Assurance (QA), and contribute to financial management of initiatives where applicable.
  • Partner cross-functionally with Legal, Risk, Treasury, and Corporate Shared Services to support solution delivery, change impacts, and adoption.


What You Will Learn In This Role

  • How manufacturing industry finance and procurement processes operate end-to-end, and how technology enables them at scale.
  • Deep understanding of the finance technology stack, including procurement tools (Coupa), Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems (QAD), and Robotics Process Automation (RPA).
  • How to improve data quality through data governance practices, master data analysis, and structured validation and controls.
  • How to drive user adoption through workshops, training, and change support across diverse stakeholder groups.


What You'll Bring:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
  • 3+ years’ experience in a Business Analyst, Product Analyst, or product delivery role supporting enterprise solutions.
  • Hands-on delivery experience with procurement and finance platforms (Coupa strongly preferred) and ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) solutions such as QAD.
  • Demonstrated experience in data governance, master data management, and data quality improvement initiatives.
  • Strong analytical, stakeholder management, and communication skills, with experience running workshops and enabling user adoption.
  • CBAP (Certified Business Analysis Professional), AWS certifications, Agile certifications (e.g., CSPO / CSM) are a plus.


Who You Are

  • Strong collaboration, teamwork and relationship building skills across multiple levels and functions in an organization.
  • Ability to operate at both a strategic and executional level; ideate and directly execute.
  • Drive growth & outcomes; activate complex work, enlist others effectively, anticipate and remove roadblocks. Move at pace.
  • Have a positive, proactive ‘can do’ attitude and an ambitiously curious mindset to be the best in your craft.
  • Promote ideas & curiosity; adding to your expertise, effectively articulates change, offers ideas and solutions.
  • Actively care & develop; championing our culture and cultivating trust.
  • Excellent communicator, compelling storyteller and fascinated by consumer behavior.


Why Join Us?

Mark Anthony is committed to hiring, engaging, and growing qualified, talented, and motivated team members at every level of our organization. We offer competitive compensation that recognizes individual and company performance. The typical hiring range for this position is $80,000 - $100,000 annually; the base pay is determined by market location and job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and education.


As part of our Total Rewards program, we are also proud to offer:

  • Company-matched RRSP contributions
  • Extended health and dental benefits, and mental health plans with 24/7 access to a primary care physician via Lumino Health
  • Maternity & Adoption Leave Top Up and Paid Secondary Caregiver Leave
  • Paid time off
  • An extensive offering of world leading learning and development programs to empower your career development – in-person and digital offerings with internationally renowned resources like Franklin Covey and Lumina.


Other perks include:

  • Located in a prime Vancouver neighborhood (near transit)
  • Hybrid work environment of 4-days a week in-office
